mapped.title: VIVA Concerti : Where the twain shall meet
mapped.description:
VIVA Concerti: Where the Twain Shall Meet is a landmark concert that brings together music, culture and compassion in support of children with cancer. Presented in conjunction with VIVA Foundation's 20th anniversary, the concert reflects VIVA's belief that hope, healing and humanity transcend race, language, religion and nationality.

At the heart of the programme is a specially curated musical journey featuring one movement from each of eight celebrated concertos. The repertoire showcases masterpieces by Tchaikovsky, Chopin, Rachmaninov, Liszt, Beethoven and Gershwin, alongside the iconic Butterfly Lovers and Yellow River concertos, bringing together some of the most beloved works from both Western and Eastern musical 
traditions.

Performed by talented musician Niu Niu (Zhang Shengliang), with the Metropolitan Festival Orchestra under the baton of maestro Chan Tze Law, the programme offers audiences a rare opportunity to experience these timeless masterpieces within a single evening, creating a rich musical dialogue where East and West meet through the universal language of music.

The concert itself marks a significant milestone in classical music performance. It is believed to be among the first occasions that a soloist and orchestra will present one movement each from eight different concertos in a single concert. This unprecedented concept not only celebrates the diversity and brilliance of the concerto repertoire but also reflects the concert's overarching theme of connection, bringing together different cultures, traditions and musical voices in one extraordinary performance.

Adding to the historic nature of the evening, Niu Niu will perform not only as a pianist but also as a violinist for a short version of the Butterfly Lovers Concerto with the orchestra. He will perform a violin concerto movement with the orchestra, marking the first time he has appeared publicly as a concerto soloist on the violin in Asia. The opportunity to witness a world-renowned pianist perform as both piano and violin soloist within the same concert is exceptionally rare and represents a remarkable artistic achievement.

The title Where the Twain Shall Meet is inspired by Rudyard Kipling's famous line, "East is East, and West is West, and never the twain shall meet." Through this concert, VIVA seeks to convey the opposite message—that the twain can indeed meet. Music has long served as a universal language capable of bridging cultures, traditions and 
peoples. As a Singapore charity dedicated to improving the lives of children with cancer, VIVA believes that compassion knows no borders. Through our work in research, patient support and survivorship programmes, we seek to help children regardless of race, language, religion or nationality.

The concert symbolises Singapore's role as a bridge between East and West, bringing together musical traditions from different cultures in a spirit of friendship, understanding and peace. 

Combining artistic innovation, cultural exchange and philanthropy, VIVA Concerti: Where the Twain Shall Meet promises to be a historic evening that celebrates the power of music to unite people and inspire hope for children and families affected by childhood cancer.

This is what Niu Niu says :

“I am incredibly excited to have the opportunity to perform 8 movements from 8 great concertos with Maestro Chan Tze Law and the Metropolitan Festival Orchestra. I am thankful to the VIVA Foundation for Children with Cancer for presenting this meaningful concert. 

It will not only be an unprecedented challenge for me but will also be a dream-come true moment to be able to perform all these 8 favourite concerto movements in one concert. These 8 concertos span over the past two centuries of music history with 8 different music styles and characters by the composers. By juxtaposing them in the same concert, it will be an experience that you’ve never encountered before, where we can all experience the intense conversation between different cultures across the globe. As it will be a fundraising concert for children with cancer, this concert will also show music can serve a greater purpose in encouraging anyone to overcome any 
hardship in life with positivity. 

It’ll be an unforgettable evening concert where the East and the West come together as one, and where we all bond together to ‘Share Music and Share Love’.”


Programme
Favourite Movements from Great Concertos

1. Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky
Piano Concerto No. 1 in B-Flat Minor, Op. 23
First Movement

2. Frédéric Chopin
Piano Concerto No. 1 in E minor, Op. 11
Second Movement

3. Sergei Rachmaninov
Piano Concerto No. 2 in C minor, Op. 18
Third Movement

INTERMISSON
(20 minutes)

4. He Zhanhao & Chen Gang

Violin: Niu Niu (Debut)

5. Franz Liszt

First Movement

mapped.title: When We Stand Together
mapped.description:
Cancer tried to steal the rhythm. We’re bringing the beat back. 14 voices sing with love from the heart. With the Philharmonic Wind Orchestra, we turn up the volume on hope — through the tunes that make us feel alive. This isn’t just a concert. It’s proof that when we stand together, we are unstoppable.Funds received from donations and tickets sold go towards the Singapore Cancer Society's Cancer Care Fund, providing vital aid to low-income families battling cancer, including:

- Temporary financial aid and medical supplies, including transportation, milk, diapers, and medical equipment.

- One-time financial assistance within 6 months of diagnosis (with possibility to re-apply for additional support if needed). Support for CHAS card holders (Blue or Orange), Public Assistance, or Special Grant Card recipients

- Interim financial support to help patients tide through while waiting for financial aid approval from government or public agencies.

STAND TOGETHER. KEEP HOLDING ON.

mapped.venue: University Cultural Centre (Ho Bee Auditorium), National University of Singapore
mapped.description:
LIVE，现场感，是豆豆生活中的关键词，他想捕捉和还原「当时的感觉」。所以他一直在记录着每个具体的瞬间—不是概括地说某件事，而是生动细致地保留那个画面：眼神、表情、动作、场景...…. 

这些瞬间，有愉快的，也有尴尬的，有误会，有受伤，会无奈也会紧张，各式各样，豆豆全都记录了下来。 

这就是为什么，大家会说豆豆的脱口秀「肢体语言丰富、鲜活生动」，因为他不仅想让你听到，他还想让你看到，体验到：我们的生活有多厉害。 

豆豆2025-2026脱口秀专场《算你厉害》全球巡演已经启动，欢迎来现场体验。 

温馨提示：本场演出为中文演出。

mapped.title: DIFFERENT: Drift
mapped.venueName: Flock at Kampong Java - The Hall
mapped.venue: Flock at Kampong Java - The Hall
mapped.description:
DIFFERENT: Drift
Some concerts happen to you. This one you drift through.

DIFFERENT returns for its third edition at Flock at Kampong Java with a programme that spans eight centuries and refuses to stay in any one of them for long. From Hildegard von Bingen's 12th-century mystical chant to Aphex Twin. From Bach to Brian Eno. From the stillness of Satie to Philip Glass in a new arrangement that pulses like something alive.

Works by J.S. Bach, Saint-Saëns, Fauré, Satie, Philip Glass, and Peter Gregson are drawn into a shared atmosphere of tenderness and suspended time — held together, and gently taken apart, by composer and sound artist Mervin Wong. Using original field recordings, electronic ambient works, and live spatial audio mixing, Mervin threads through and within the programme, quietly reshaping the experience as it unfolds.

Classical music, but not as you know it.

DIFFERENT: Drift is presented by Tangent Moves.

Experience as it unfolds.

mapped.title: 回响 Echoes of Us
mapped.description:
Echoes of Us
Every voice you’ve heard has a story you don’t know.
Seven local singer-songwriters. Seven real lives behind the music. What happens when the stage lights go off? What breaks them,and what keeps them going? On a road with no guaranteed destination, what makes them choose to keep singing?
Inspired by true stories, Echoes is an intimate live experience that takes you behind the music and into the hearts of the people who make it.
When their voices go out into the world — what it stirs in you, are the echoes of us.

mapped.title: The Legend Continues - Engelbert Humperdinck - The Celebration Tour 2026
mapped.description:
Engelbert Humperdinck The Celebration Tour 2026 Lights Up Singapore!

“The Legend Continues - Engelbert Humperdinck - The Celebration Tour 2026”, the new Tour is coming to Singapore.

The legendary music icon, currently on tour and renowned for his velvety voice and timeless ballads, has captivated audiences worldwide for over six decades. Engelbert Humperdinck is best known for his iconic hit Release Me, which topped charts in 11 countries and remained in the UK Top 50 for an impressive 56 weeks. His illustrious catalogue includes enduring classics such as Am I That Easy to Forget, Quando Quando Quando, A Man Without Love, The Last Waltz, and Spanish Eyes.

More recently, his 1968 hit A Man Without Love has enjoyed a resurgence, reaching new audiences after being featured in Marvel’s Moon Knight starring Oscar Isaac, as well as in the action film Bullet Train starring Brad Pitt.

With over 150 million records sold worldwide and a remarkable string of No. 1 hits—including the legendary 1967 smash Release Me—Engelbert Humperdinck remains one of the world’s most beloved performers. A recent Grammy nominee and recipient of numerous accolades, including stars on the Hollywood and Las Vegas Walks of Fame, he continues to be celebrated for his rich, velvety voice and charismatic stage presence.

“I’ve always loved coming to Asia—the energy and warmth of the audiences are truly special,” he shared. “I’m thrilled to bring these songs to my fans in various part of Asia especially Singapore this September.”

“The Legend Continues - Engelbert Humperdinck - The Celebration Tour 2026” is set to be a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity for fans to enjoy his timeless music live. The tour not only marks his triumphant return to Southeast Asia, but also celebrates his latest studio work, with songs from All About Love reinterpreting classics from the Great American Songbook. Promoters say audiences should expect a grand show befitting the legend’s illustrious career.

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | National Theatre Live
The Fifth Step (Screening) – NC16
mapped.description:
Written by David Ireland 
Directed by Finn den Hertog 

★★★★★ ‘Raw, excruciating and often alarmingly funny’ – The Daily Mail   
★★★★ ‘Two actors at the top of their game’ – Evening Standard   
★★★★ ‘Hilarious two-hander’ – The Guardian    

After years in the 12-step programme of Alcoholics Anonymous, James becomes a sponsor to newcomer Luka. The pair bond over black coffee, trade stories and build a fragile friendship out of their shared experiences. But as Luka approaches step five—the moment of confession—dangerous truths emerge, threatening the trust on which both of their recoveries depend. 

Finn den Hertog directs the provocative and entertaining production filmed live from London’s West End.

mapped.title: The Grass That Flowers
mapped.description:
Beneath the ocean’s waves lies a secret world waiting to be discovered. The Grass That Flowers invites children into the amazing world of the humble seagrass meadow—a wondrous underwater community teeming with life.

Where marine science meets the art of dance, this fantastical performance reimagines familiar sea creatures as magical water spirits. Meet the mischievous sea gremlins who bully minuscule fish; the generous eight-armed weaver god who knits protective blankets of seagrass for juvenile fish and more! Mayhem ensues when human disruptions turns the seagrass world upside down. All sea deities, kind or mean, will have to band together and fight for their community’s survival.

This performance is brought to life by a dynamic cast of professional dancers from ARTS FISSION and children performers. The show translates complex ecological concepts—such as conservation, ecosystem, and anthropogenic impact—into a magical and accessible learning experience for children.

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | The Studios
Fragments of Tuah
by Mark Teh & Five Arts Centre (Malaysia)
mapped.description:
“indelibly evocative and brimming with sensoriality” – Critics Republic

BOH Cameronian Arts Awards 2026 – Best Original Script; Best Director; Best Set and/or Visual Media; Best Music & Sound Design, overall Best of 2025

What does loyalty mean today? What gaps have shaped our understanding of the past? And who decides which myths are worth remembering? 

Fragments of Tuah is a documentary theatre work set to original music that revisits, dissects, and reimagines the enduring mythical figure of Hang Tuah—a purported 15th-century warrior often deployed as an emblem of unquestioning allegiance to the state and her ruler. His legend and memory continue to echo across time, shaping the narratives of loyalty, identity, and power in Malaysia even to this day. 

Over three years, Five Arts Centre members Mark Teh, Faiq Syazwan Kuhiri, Syamsul Azhar together with close collaborator Wong Tay Sy researched the different faces, facets and phases of this fabled figure, and in the process confronted what all these incarnations of Hang Tuah might mean for the historical and contemporary landscapes within and beyond Malaysia. 

The audience is invited into a space of reflection where the fragments of Tuah are pieced together not to restore a whole, but to explore the gaps, contradictions, and questions that linger. Rather than offering a single truth or linear biography, Fragments of Tuah is a layered collage of perspectives and materials—drawn from archival texts, schoolbooks, songs, state slogans, invented memories, and historical erasures. 

Performers / Musicians: Faiq Syazwan Kuhiri, OJ Law, Shariman Shuhaime 
Director: Mark Teh 
Production Designer: Wong Tay Sy 
Lighting Designer: Syamsul Azhar 
Multimedia Designers: Syamsul Azhar, Bryan Chang 
Stage Manager: Armanzaki Amirolzakri 
Producer: June Tan 
Assistant Producer / Production Manager: Hoe Hui Ting 
Executive Producer: Five Arts Centre

There will be a post-show dialogue after the performances on 24 & 25 Jul, Fri & Sat, 8pm.

As part of the WROC World Tour,Paul and his musicians will bring the songs from his latest album,*WROC* (*Washington's Rules of Civility*),to the stage alongside fan favourites from his acclaimed solo career,Racer X,and Mr. Big. mapped.title: The Parting Glass
mapped.description:
Here’s to us, to love, and to all that could have been.

Jim is the life of the party. He’s the joker, the storyteller, the one who always knows what to say. But when his eyes meet Sarah’s for the first time, something shifts. For the first time, words fail him. No matter how hard he tries to compose himself and make a good impression, he comes across as flustered and clumsy. But that’s okay, because Sarah finds him cute. It might even be love at first sight.

It’s Jim’s 30th birthday—a night for celebration and laughter, held at his favourite bar in town. But something doesn’t feel right. Jim has always felt out of place, like there’s a cloud in his mind that is hard to clear. Today, he wants to tell you his story.

Directed by Samantha Scott-Blackhall, written by Chris O’Connor, and adapted by Krish Natarajan, The Parting Glass is an immersive theatrical experience that invites you into Jim’s world—at live music bar The Blackbird. From the team behind Third Man Syndrome and Every Brilliant Thing comes a poignant exploration of mental health and masculinity that poses the question: how well do we ever really know someone—and what are we missing when we don’t ask?

Step in, join the party, and raise a glass to Jim.

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | A Date With Friends
Back to Golden Hits: Songs of
Sam Hui & Jacky Cheung
《两代歌神：许冠杰 · 张学友金曲相约》
mapped.description:
Sam Hui and Jacky Cheung are two of Cantopop’s most beloved voices, whose songs have accompanied generations through life’s many moments. From Sam Hui’s witty, relatable classics to Jacky Cheung’s heartfelt ballads, these two icons have formed an enduring soundtrack that continues to resonate with audiences today. 

Join Wysom Wong and Marcus Lee for a special evening celebrating these legendary artists through their most memorable hits, including From The Heart of a Loafer, the theme song from The Private Eyes, Kiss Goodbye, My Heart is Left in Pieces, and many more. These timeless classics will be reimagined in a fresh live performance, awakening nostalgia and the connections between people. So gather your friends and relive these evergreen golden hits together!

mapped.title: GRAVITY [G]
mapped.description:
Three legendary voices. One iconic stage. One unforgettable theatrical event. 

Following a sell-out run across the UK, including a triumphant performance at the prestigious Theatre Royal Drury Lane, GRAVITY comes to Marina Bay Sands, Singapore for an exclusive series of performances. West End royalty Kerry Ellis, Louise Dearman, and Rachel Tucker unite for GRAVITY - a spectacular celebration of the songs, roles, and moments that defined a generation of musical theatre. 

Kerry Ellis was the West End's first British Elphaba, Rachel Tucker became the longest-running Elphaba in the show’s history, and Louise Dearman made theatrical history as the only actress ever to play both Elphaba and Glinda in London’s West End production. Together, these three extraordinary performers have left an indelible mark on one of the world’s most beloved musicals - and now unite on stage for a concert unlike any other. 

Expect an electrifying evening of soaring anthems, powerhouse vocals, career-defining performances, and unforgettable surprises, as three of the most celebrated leading ladies of modern musical theatre take flight together on one stage. With songs from Wicked and beyond, GRAVITY promises a thrilling celebration of friendship, legacy, and the extraordinary music that changed their lives forever. 

This is a once-in-a-lifetime concert experience not to be missed.

mapped.title: Chinese Children Drama - Chinese Proverbs-Ye Zi Gao’s Journey of Growth，华语儿童剧《成语寓言剧之叶子高成长记》
mapped.venueName: Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre Auditorium, Level 9 新加坡华族文化中心表演厅, 九楼
mapped.venue: Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre Auditorium, Level 9 新加坡华族文化中心表演厅, 九楼
mapped.description:
A long long time ago，there was a scholar named Ye Zigao. Every day,he dream about becoming the top scholar in the imperial examination and rising to fame and fortune. However，he was very lazy and unwilling to study hard . He was not only late for school，he also slept in class. In the end，he never even graduated. 《得过且过》 

One day，he fell asleep under a tree and dreamed that he had become the top scholar. As he does not know how to do anything，people accused him of being a fraud and were about to behead him. Just as the blade was about to fall，he woke up only to realise that it was all a dream. 《南柯一梦》 

Ye Zigao then made up his mind to study diligently. On his way home,he found a bundle of silver. Ye Zigao decided to return it to its owner,but since it was already late，he chose to hide it first and look for the owner the following day. However,he couldn’t find a safe enough place to hide it. Finally，he came up with a “brilliant” idea，he buried the silver in his own yard. Still worried，he placed a sign over the spot that read: 

“No three hundred taels of silver are buried here.” 《此地无银三百两》 

In a while later，Ye Zigao’s father fell ill from anger over his son’s laziness. A neighbor recommended a good doctor named Mr Wang,and told Ye Zigao to invite him to treat his father. 

However，Ye Zigao mistakenly brought back two other doctors one surnamed Wang (a different Chinese character ) and one surnamed Huang who turned out to be veterinarians《悔之晚矣》

mapped.title: Gran Turismo World Series 2026 | Round 3 – Singapore [G]
mapped.description:
The Gran Turismo World Series global esports tournament makes its highly anticipated debut in Singapore with Round 3 of the 2026 Championship on Saturday, 3 October. Held as part of the Grand Prix™ Season Singapore (GPSS) festivities, the event arrives as the city builds momentum ahead of the race week.

Hosted in English by UK TV Presenter Jules Hardy, along with commentators Tom Brooks and Jimmy Broadbent, the race action will take place live on stage. Twelve racing seats are lined up with all of the ultra-realistic race action visible to the audience on a large screen above the drivers.
 
Hosted at the iconic Sands Theatre at Marina Bay Sands, the event will kick off with a special Singapore Regional Gran Turismo Cup support race featuring top competitors from across the region. The fastest racers from Hong Kong, Indonesia, Korea, Malaysia, Taiwan, Thailand and Singapore will go head-to-head after qualifying through a dedicated Singapore Regional Time Trial Challenge.
 
The action then continues with the Manufacturers Cup, where 12 drivers from the Americas Region will battle for valuable World Series points in a final push before the World Finals in Tokyo this December.
 
Closing the evening, the spotlight turns to the Nations Cup, featuring 12 of the world’s top drivers go wheel-to-wheel for crucial championship points ahead of the season finale.
 
Experience the thrilling live spectacle at Sands Theatre at Marina Bay Sands, as the world’s best Gran Turismo racers battle and bring the excitement of global motorsport to the heart of Singapore. 


Programme information:

Guests may enter and exit the venue, outside of temporary lockout periods which may apply during parts of the event.

3pm: Door Opens

3.15pm - 3.30pm: Meet & Greet with GT's top drivers for Signature Ticket-holders

4pm: Singapore Regional Gran Turismo Cup (exhibition race)

5pm: Manufacturers Cup

6.30pm: Intermission (The Theatre Concession Stand will be available for the purchase of refreshments.) 

8pm: Nations Cup starts

9.30pm: End of Event

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | National Theatre Live
Hamlet (Screening) – PG
mapped.description:
By William Shakespeare 
Directed by Robert Hastie 

‘This is exactly the kind of Hamlet you would hope to see’ – Broadway World 
‘A beautifully choreographed production’ – The Guardian 
‘A compelling reading of Shakespeare’s play’ – The Independent 

Trapped between duty and doubt, surrounded by power and privilege, young Prince Hamlet dares to ask the ultimate question—you know the one. 

National Theatre Deputy Artistic Director Robert Hastie (Standing at the Sky’s Edge, Operation Mincemeat) directs this sharp, stylish and darkly funny reimagining. 

Filmed live in London for screens across the globe.

mapped.title: HANS ZIMMER LIVE  - THE NEXT LEVEL IN SINGAPORE
mapped.description:
Multiple Academy Award® and Grammy Award®-winning composer Hans Zimmer will bring his groundbreaking live production Hans Zimmer Live – The Next Level World Tour 2026 to Singapore. Following the global success of Hans Zimmer Live, the legendary film composer returns with an all-new production that pushes the boundaries of live music through electronic sound design, powerful orchestration, spectacular lighting, and a striking new stage concept.  

Presented by AEG Presents Asia, Semmel Concerts and RCI Global, the Singapore show will take place on October 7, 2026 (Wed) at the Singapore Indoor Stadium. This will mark Hans Zimmer’s return to Singapore, since his last performance in Singapore since 2019.

The Hans Zimmer Live – The Next Level World Tour will also travel across multiple cities in Asia, including Yokohama, Kaohsiung, Taipei, Beijing, Hangzhou, Chengdu, and Guangzhou.

Hans Zimmer says: “I want fans to feel like they’re at a rave party or riding a roller coaster. For me, every show is a new voyage of discovery. The greatest challenge is the status quo — you have to do everything in your power to leave it behind and find and create something new.” 

On this tour, Zimmer sets new standards with electronic sound elements, impressive lighting design and a striking new stage concept, continuing the success story of his previous live productions in spectacular fashion. Through fresh arrangements of music from Pirates of the Caribbean, The Lion King, The Dark Knight, Interstellar and Dune, alongside new selections from Dune: Part Two and F1®: The Movie, The Next Level brings Zimmer’s cinematic worlds to life in a completely new way. The show stands for Zimmer’s relentless drive to push artistic boundaries, explore new paths and continually evolve his sound.

mapped.title: HAPPY MONDAYS Live in Singapore
mapped.description:
UK Alt-Rock Icons Happy Mondays to perform in Singapore for the very first time!

Get ready for a night of euphoric grooves, timeless anthems, and pure Madchester energy as Happy Mondays arrive in Singapore on 15 September 2026 at Foo Chow Hall! Presented by LAMC, the British icons bring their celebrated live show to Singapore for an unforgettable evening of alternative rock, funk-infused rhythms, acid house vibes, and dancefloor chaos.

Since forming in 1980, Happy Mondays have stood as one of the UK’s most groundbreaking bands, redefining alternative music with their fearless blend of indie rock, psychedelia, funk, and club culture. Led by the unmistakable Shaun Ryder and featuring fan-favourite force of nature Bez, the band became a cornerstone of the legendary Madchester era.

Their platinum-selling masterpiece Pills ’n’ Thrills and Bellyaches propelled them to international acclaim, delivering era-defining classics including “Step On,” “Kinky Afro,” and a catalogue of tracks that helped shape British music history.

Fresh off years of acclaimed tours and festival appearances across the UK and beyond, Happy Mondays continue to prove why their music and their live performances remain as exhilarating, unpredictable, and influential as ever.

Don’t miss your chance to experience the sound, spirit, and chaos of Happy Mondays live in Singapore this September!

mapped.title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone™ In Concert
mapped.venueName: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.venue: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.description:
Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone™ in Concert with Resound Collective Orchestra at Star Theatre as part of the Harry Potter™ Film Concert Series. The concert will feature an orchestra performing live, to picture, every note from Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone. Audiences will be able to relive the magic of the entire film in high-definition on a big screen while hearing John Williams’ unforgettable score live. Relive the film that started it all. Watch the wand choose the wizard, a troll run amok and magic mirrors in high-definition while a live orchestra performs John Williams’ iconic score. Don’t miss this once-in-a-lifetime event as Harry, Ron, Hermione and all your favourite characters return to the screen and enchant the world all over again. 

All characters and elements © & ™ Warner Bros. Entertainment Inc. Publishing Rights © JKR.

Brought To You By CineConcerts.

These presentations of Harry Potter in Concert will be conducted by Benjamin Pope.

Benjamin Pope is an internationally-acclaimed conductor, renowned for his work in live-to-projection film and symphonic concerts, as well as major theatrical productions. He is proud to be associated with CineConcerts, and has an extensive repertoire that includes the complete Harry Potter in Concert series, Gladiator, Elf in Concert (European premiere), and many other blockbuster and silent film titles.

He has led productions and concerts in many of the world’s most prestigious venues, including the Vienna Staatsoper, Berlin Philharmonie, Royal Albert Hall, Royal Opera House Covent Garden, Teatro Colón (Buenos Aires), and La Fenice (Venice), as well as earning awards for sold-out arena performances. His orchestral collaborations include the Royal Philharmonic Orchestra, Philharmonia, Berlin Staatskapelle, Dresden Staatskapelle & Philharmonic, Tokyo Philharmonic, Hallé, Royal Scottish National Orchestra, City of Birmingham Symphony Orchestra, Luxembourg Philharmonic, Hong Kong Sinfonietta, and many other leading orchestras across Europe, Asia, the Americas, and Australia. He has also toured internationally with the Royal Philharmonic Orchestra and Royal Ballet among others.

Critically-acclaimed in conducting for dance, Benjamin is Principal Conductor for Matthew Bourne’s New Adventures, including the iconic Swan Lake. He was appointed Music Director of the Royal Ballet of Flanders in 2011 and later served as Music Consultant to Hong Kong Ballet. His work as a composer, arranger, and vocalist spans concert, theatre, ballet, radio, film, and television.

As a prize-winning graduate of Oxford University and the Royal College of Music himself, he is committed to mentoring future generations of musicians, and is a guest tutor on the Conducting Masters and Composition for Screen programmes at London’s Royal College of Music

mapped.title: Hayato Sumino in Concert
mapped.description:
International Piano Phenomenon 

Experience the extraordinary artistry of Hayato Sumino, a pianist and composer redefining classical music with dazzling virtuosity, fearless imagination, and a fresh contemporary voice. 

Standing at the forefront of a new generation of musicians, he seamlessly blends technical brilliance with bold improvisation and imaginative arrangements. His digital presence, built under the name “Cateen,” has earned him over 1.56 million YouTube subscribers and more than 200 million views across his platforms. 

A Forbes 30 Under 30 Japan honouree, Steinway & Sons Artist, and ambassador for Apple Music Classical and CASIO, Sumino has performed with leading orchestras including the NHK Symphony Orchestra, BBC Philharmonic, Chicago Symphony Orchestra, and the New Japan Philharmonic under Joe Hisaishi. 

Returning to Singapore after his sold-out 2024 appearance, Sumino presents Chopin Orbit—a richly imagined programme centred on Chopin and set in dialogue with works by Adès, Saint-Saëns and Stravinsky. From the dramatic sweep of the Piano Sonata No. 2 and the brilliance of the “Winter Wind” Étude, to the haunting allure of Danse Macabre and the fiery virtuosity of The Firebird, the programme traverses a wide spectrum of colour and intensity. 

Woven through with Sumino’s own compositions, re-compositions, and improvisations—including Lydian Harp, White Keys, and Imaginary Polonaise—the recital unfolds as a vivid journey between tradition and reinvention—spontaneous, compelling, and not to be missed.

mapped.title: Batu Bo Makan Gegat (The Impossible)
mapped.venueName: Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre Auditorium, Level 9 新加坡华族文化中心表演厅, 九楼
mapped.venue: Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre Auditorium, Level 9 新加坡华族文化中心表演厅, 九楼
mapped.description:
"Batu Bo Makan Gegat” - The Impossible

Set in modern-day Singapore, “Batu Bo Makan Gegat” takes its title from the playful and ironic line of a traditional Peranakan saying “Jat Berejat, Batu Bo Makan Gegat”—suggesting something so absurd, so impossible, it could never happen.

For 50 proud years, Bibik Gan Geok Neo, a tough and spirited nyonya now in her late 80s, has run her family’s old school Peranakan restaurant, “OhPohChok” with fierce dedication. By her side are her daughter Ong Swee Lee and her loyal son-in-law Tan Hock Chye. Now in their 60s, both are weary but dutiful in keeping Bibik’s vision alive.

Jessica Tan Lee Lin, their only daughter, is a young professional. A young lawyer basking in the career climb and mentally finds running a restaurant far too tiring. Her boyfriend Joseph Chan Chee Teck is a comfortable distraction.

Life has its takes and turns. By her side, Bibik Geok Neo holds her deepest secrets and dreams, her passion and desires, only with her faithful majie of more than 50 years, Kam Jie, and her sister Gan Chin Neo.

What unfolds is a delightfully humorous, touching, and deeply human journey of love, family, identity, and the age-old tug-of-war between tradition and progress.

Some things cannot be eaten away. Not time. Not tradition, Not love.

Legacies lie in wait for the right hands, the right heart with the courage to turn the key.

50 years of memories, of hands that stirred before sunrise, or recipes whispered never written. To Bibik Geok Neo, it is everything.

Come step into time with us and allow Gunong Sayang (1910) Ltd present our Wayang Peranakan 2026.

mapped.title: JAN SCHULMEISTER PIANO RECITAL SINGAPORE 2026
mapped.description:
"BBC Music Award-winner Jan Schulmeister brings the PETROF P237 Monsoon to life in a visionary recital where sound becomes colour and music becomes light.

At just 20 years old, Czech pianist Jan Schulmeister has emerged as one of Europe’s most compelling young artistic voices — a musician celebrated for his authenticity, imagination, and remarkable international momentum. As the sixth-generation descendant of the legendary Černý–Schulmeister musical dynasty, whose heritage reaches back to the era of Antonín Dvořák, Jan represents a living continuation of the rich Czech and Central European piano tradition.

A prodigy who began his musical journey at the age of five, Jan is currently refining his artistry at the Janáček Academy of Performing Arts in Brno and the Hochschule für Musik in Hannover. His rise accelerated in 2026 following the release of his debut recording with Supraphon, which received exceptional critical acclaim including a prestigious 5-star review from BBC Music Magazine and recognition through the BBC Music Awards. Despite his young age, Jan has already released multiple studio albums and performed with distinguished ensembles including the Prague Radio Symphony and Brno Philharmonic.

For this special Singapore recital, Jan performs on the magnificent PETROF P237 Monsoon — the flagship concert grand of PETROF, renowned for its warm European singing tone, extraordinary colour palette, and deeply expressive character.

More than a traditional piano recital, this programme invites audiences into a world where music unfolds like shifting light and changing landscapes of emotion.

The evening opens with Mozart’s Fantasy in D minor, K.397, a work suspended between improvisation and architecture. Beginning in darkness and introspection before opening into moments of brilliance and grace, Mozart creates a dramatic musical journey that foreshadows the Romantic spirit.

From there, Jan enters the deeply emotional universe of Sergei Rachmaninoff through selections from Morceaux de fantaisie, Op. 3. Written when the composer was still a teenager, these miniatures already reveal extraordinary emotional maturity. The audience will experience shifting moods ranging from intimate lyricism and melancholy to grand resonance and poetic introspection, including echoes of the iconic spirit associated with the celebrated “Bells of Moscow”.

The programme continues with Tchaikovsky’s Dumka in C minor, a work that captures the duality of Slavic expression — moments of quiet reflection transformed into energetic dance and passion. Rich with contrast and emotional warmth, it reveals the composer’s gift for storytelling through sound.

After intermission, the recital moves into the distinctive voice of Leoš Janáček with his powerful Sonata 1.X.1905 “From the Street.” Inspired by historical and political events, this rare masterpiece channels grief, resistance, and human intensity through Janáček’s uniquely expressive musical language. Raw, direct, and deeply moving, it offers one of the most emotionally charged moments of the evening.

The recital concludes with selections from Alexander Scriabin’s 24 Preludes, Op.11 — music that transforms piano sound into something almost visionary and transcendent. Delicate whispers dissolve into radiant bursts of colour and ecstatic energy, revealing Scriabin’s fascination with connecting sound, emotion, light, and the infinite.

Across this carefully curated programme, Jan Schulmeister traces a journey through the Slavic soul and beyond — from Classical clarity to Romantic passion, from earthly memory to cosmic imagination.

This is not simply a concert.

It is an immersive artistic experience where tradition meets youthful brilliance, where the piano becomes a storyteller, and where every sound reveals a new shade of colour.

Join us in Singapore for an unforgettable evening with one of Europe’s most exciting emerging pianists and experience the expressive beauty of the PETROF P237 Monsoon in full concert glory.

Approximate performance duration: 75 minutes (excluding 15-minute intermission). Programme subject to change at the artist’s discretion."

Celebrating 55 years in music, Jerry & The NeuFaces take audiences on a journey through the band’s storied career. Accompanied by rarely seen photos and videos, the concert traces their rise from the local club circuit of the 1970s to international tours across Europe and Asia following their breakthrough in the 1980s and 1990s after signing a recording contract with Polygram Records.

Audiences can expect favourites like Gadis Manis and covers of songs such as Tom Jones’s Delilah, Engelbert Humperdinck’s Quando Quando Quando, Lionel Richie’s Easy and more. mapped.title: Jesus Christ Superstar [Advisory16]
mapped.description:
AN ALBUM THAT INSPIRED A REVOLUTION.

A REVELATION THAT CHANGED THE WORLD.

A REINVENTION FOR THIS MILLENNIUM.

Timothy Sheader (Crazy for You, Into the Woods) directs this mesmerising new production of the iconic global phenomenon, JESUS CHRIST SUPERSTAR. Originally staged for London’s Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre, this reimagined production won the 2017 Olivier Award for Best Musical Revival, garnering unprecedented reviews and accolades.

Choreographed by Drew McOnie (King Kong, Strictly Ballroom), with designs by Tom Scutt, lighting design by Lee Curran, sound design by Nick Lidster, and music supervision by Tom Deering. The production features lyrics and music by Emmy, GRAMMY, Oscar, and Tony winners Tim Rice and Andrew Lloyd Webber, JESUS CHRIST SUPERSTAR is set against the backdrop of an extraordinary series of events during the final weeks in the life of Jesus Christ, as seen through the eyes of Judas. Reflecting the rock roots that defined a generation, the legendary score includes ‘I Don’t Know How to Love Him’, ‘Gethsemane’ and ‘Superstar’.

“GORGEOUS, THRILLING, HEAVENLY MUSICAL.” - The Guardian.

“Jesus Christ Superstar” is a theatrical production presented for entertainment purposes only. It is not intended to be a biblically accurate representation of the life of Jesus.

mapped.title: Keichan - Piano Asia Tour [ROOM]: Singapore

mapped.venue: University Cultural Centre Ho Bee Auditorium, NUS
mapped.description:
Experience the electrifying artistry of Keichan (けいちゃん) — Japan’s sensational pianist and YouTube phenomenon — in a one-night-only live concert that promises to captivate audiences with a blend of technical brilliance, heartfelt emotion, and genre-defying performances.

Gaining fame from his moving rendition of The Sound of Silence, Keichan has since gained overwhelming support from around the world, with over 1.4 million YouTube subscribers and more than 600 million total views. His spontaneous street performances across Japan have become viral for their raw expressiveness and unfiltered connection with audiences of all ages. Keichan is known for blending classical pieces, anime hits, and modern tunes with his own soulful improvisations. His debut album『殻落箱』(Kararohako) and his three subsequent albums all received critical acclaim, solidifying his place in the contemporary music world.

Fresh from his sold out concert in Singapore in 2025, Keichan has also performed in Japan, South Korea and Taiwan. He has also performed on morning news programs, composed film soundtracks, and appeared in films and on stage, all while being an active piano performer. Notably, he was responsible for performing the main theme of Detective Conan: One-eyed Flashback, the number one film at the Japanese box office in 2025.

This is not just a concert — it’s a celebration of musical freedom, cultural exchange, and the unifying power of live performance. Whether you’re a classical connoisseur, anime enthusiast, or music lover of any genre, Keichan’s vibrant and emotive style will surely leave you inspired.

Programme:
Keichan is curating an exciting programme specially for his Singapore concert — a musical journey where classical melodies blend seamlessly with the vibrant tunes of anime soundtracks and J-Pop favourites and even local cultural songs. The full setlist will be announced soon! Don’t miss this chance to witness one of Japan’s most exciting musical talents live on stage!

mapped.title: From Prelude to Flame • Kevin Chen | Singapore Debut
mapped.description:
Fresh from winning the Silver Medal at the most recent Chopin Competition, pianist Kevin Chen makes his Singapore debut in a programme of exceptional sweep and ambition — one that moves from inward reflection to elemental force.

The recital opens with Frédéric Chopin’s 24 Preludes, Op. 28, a cornerstone of the piano repertoire and a work that distils an entire emotional universe into a compact cycle. By turns intimate, volatile, luminous, and austere, the Preludes demand not only virtuosity but imagination, restraint, and narrative instinct —qualities that have already marked Chen out as a compelling Chopin interpreter.

From there, the programme traces a line of increasing intensity through the visionary sound-world of Alexander Scriabin. The Prelude in B major, Op. 11 No. 11 offers a moment of radiant calm before the Fantasy, Op. 28 expands into sweeping gestures and harmonic daring. The journey culminates in Vers la flamme, Op. 72, Scriabin’s hypnotic late masterpiece — an inexorable ascent toward ecstatic release.

The final chapter belongs to Franz Liszt, whose music fuses virtuosity with transcendence. Les jeux d’eaux à la Villa d’Este shimmers with colour and spiritual luminosity, while the solo-piano transcription of Les Preludes transforms Liszt’s symphonic poem into a grand, fate-driven narrative for the keyboard.

Spanning introspection, mysticism, and elemental drama, this Singapore debut recital introduces Kevin Chen as an artist of breadth, courage, and imagination —one equally at home in poetic concentration and high-voltage intensity. A striking first appearance from a pianist poised at the beginning of an international career.

PROGRAMME

CHOPIN – 24 Preludes, Op. 28
SCRIABIN – Prelude in B major, Op. 11 No. 11
SCRIABIN – Fantasy, Op. 28
SCRIABIN – Vers la flamme, Op. 72
LISZT – Les jeux d’eaux à la Villa d’Este, No. 4 from Années de pèlerinage III, S. 163
LISZT – Les préludes (Symphonic poem), S. 97 (solo piano transcription)

(Programme subject to change at the artist’s discretion)

mapped.title: Legally Blonde – The Musical

The international West End and Broadway smash hit musical comedy Legally Blonde – The Musical is strutting into Singapore this July with a bold, high-energy new production from Singapore Repertory Theatre.

Unapologetically stylish and powered by confidence, Elle Woods is ready to take law school by storm - proving that ambition and heart can take you anywhere. What begins as a quest for love quickly becomes something bigger: a joyful journey of self-belief, friendship and truly owning who you are.

This is Legally Blonde with its wit turned up, in a fresh reimagining that's sleek and glamorous. Expect show-stopping songs, big laughs, and a feel-good celebration with a whole lot of pink power.

Celebrated for her performances as Éponine (Les Misérables) and Eliza (Hamilton),West End star Nathania Ong will take centre stage as a fearless new Elle Woods, bringing fresh attitude and unstoppable star power to this iconic role.

Please note that Nathan Hartono will not be performing the role of Warner in the Preview performances on 29 and 30 July. The role will be performed by an alternate cast member.

mapped.title: Chasing Light 《追》: Ding Yi x Dick Lee 70
mapped.description:
In celebration of Dick Lee’s 70th birthday,Chasing Light 《追》: Ding Yi x Dick Lee 70 reimagines the Mandarin and Cantopop classics written by one of Singapore’s most iconic creative voices,Dick Lee.

Many of these songs have lived in the hearts of audiences across Asia for decades,popularised by legendary artists such as Leslie Cheung,Sandy Lam,Andy Lau,Jacky Cheung,Anita Mui,Leon Lai and Aaron Kwok. While these melodies are instantly recognisable,not everyone knows them as part of Dick Lee’s remarkable songwriting legacy.

Presented by Ding Yi Music Company,the concert features vocalists Jessie Yeong,Lennerd Lim,Skye Sirena and Wysom Wong performing beloved classics such as Chase 《追》,Love is Eternal《爱是永恒》,North South West East《北南西东》and Dawn 《破晓》. Through new arrangements by Singapore composer Sulwyn Lok,Taiwan composer Hannah Hsieh,and Hong Kong composer Law Kin Pong and others,these classics are reimagined through the rich colours of contemporary Chinese music.

Under the baton of Resident conductor Wong De Li,Dedric and directed by Jet Ho,the concert brings together voice,chamber music and theatrical storytelling to offer a fresh perspective on songs that have shaped generations of listeners.

Chasing Light 《追》: Ding Yi x Dick Lee 70 invites audiences to rediscover familiar melodies through a new musical lens,revealing the artistry of Dick Lee’s song writing and the emotional depth of contemporary Chinese chamber music.

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | da:ns focus - Connect Asia Now
Sounding Light
by Cloud Gate Dance Theatre of Taiwan
mapped.description:
Sound and dance merge with one another; the body emancipates itself and becomes an instrument. – Stuttgarter Zeitung

Cloud Gate leaves you sitting breathlessly on the edge of your chair. – San Jose Mercury News

This is a company that you will enjoy, fall in love with, and want to see again. – Ballet-Dance Magazine

Internationally acclaimed and beloved worldwide, Cloud Gate Dance Theatre returns to Singapore with another stunning tapestry of movement, inspired by the sounds, shapes and fluidity of nature.

Created during the pandemic, Sounding Light begins from a renewed act of listening.

Inspired by the forest—its insects, birds, and the wind moving through trees—this work choreographed by Artistic Director Cheng Tsung-lung reflects on the shifting (im)balance between human civilisation and the natural world.

The choreography traces the passage of a single day. Sunlight moves, clouds gather, and shadows of leaves drift across space. Time is shaped through changing light, rhythm, and atmosphere.

Sound is created live on stage. The dancers become both movers and musicians, using breath, voice, finger snaps, and clapping in collaboration with composers Lim Giong and Chang Shiuan.

Movement and sound intertwine. Gestures echo birds and insects; bodies turn into instruments. Together, they evoke wind, rain, and flowing water—forming a world that is both seen and heard.

Premiered internationally at Kunstfest Weimar and HELLERAU – European Center for the Arts Dresden in 2024, the work has since been widely acclaimed and continues to tour internationally.

This programme is presented as part of da:ns focus – Connect Asia Now (CAN), a weekend that spotlights contemporary dance works created by or in collaboration with Asian artists, focusing on distinct voices and creative impulses from the region.

mapped.title: Lisa Ono Precious Music Journey Live in Singapore
mapped.description:
2026 Lisa Ono Precious Music Journey Live in Singapore 

✿ Experience the return of the “Queen of Bossa Nova” to the Esplanade Concert Hall this August. 

After Japanese-Brazilian singer Lisa Ono (小野リサ )’s sold-out show in Singapore back to 2024, her new album titled "Sue Ann — Homage to Antonio Carlos Jobim" is set to be released as a tribute to the creator of bossa nova. 

Her performances at major venues worldwide have been praised as “healing & power” (SBS Australia).  “You could be pretty confident you were hearing the best Bossa nova of our time,” said ABC Australia after a concert at Sydney Opera House. 

This evening, join us to enjoy Lisa’s latest album in a live program of timeless love and new standard of power, from one of the most amazing women of our time. 

✿ Who is Lisa Ono?  Lisa Ono began singing and playing the guitar at age 15, debuting as a professional Bossa Nova singer in 1989. Her natural voice, rhythmic guitar playing and her charming smile led her to huge success and popularised Bossa Nova in Asia.   Lisa Ono has performed with many acclaimed musicians such as the legendary Antonio Carlos Jobim and the Jazz Samba giant Joao Donato. Lisa has been performing enthusiastically in New York, Brazil and across Asia.   Lisa Ono's album Dream (1999), sold more than two hundred thousand copies in Japan, and since then she has established a more determined position in the Japanese Bossa Nova community. Her top hits include Fly me to the Moon, Weiny Weiny, La Vie En Rose, Samba de Verão and more.

mapped.title: Bryan Soo & Zack Teo / The Ballades: A Dialogue in Four Tales
mapped.description:
Praised for his musicality and artistic sensitivity, Singaporean pianist Bryan Soo has been described by Prof. Grzegorz Niemczuk—one of Poland’s leading interpreters of Frédéric Chopin—as a musician whose playing “flows as if united with his sensitive soul,” and by Régulo Martínez-Antón as one who “plays with sensitivity and passion.” A concert artist, producer, and educator, Bryan performs internationally across Asia, Europe, and the United States. He is the founder and artistic director of BespokeArts, a curatorial performance platform dedicated to presenting distinctive concert experiences.

Joining him for this recital is pianist Zack Teo, known for his poetic sensibility, vocal approach to phrasing, and refined touch at the keyboard. Originally aspiring to be a voice major, his affinity for singing continues to shape his pianism, resulting in performances that breathe and sing. 
At the heart of the programme are Chopin’s four Ballades—works that transformed the piano into a vehicle of narrative imagination. Rich in drama, lyricism, and structural innovation, each Ballade unfolds like a musical tale, weaving together moments of poetry, turbulence, and reflection. Shared between the two pianists, these masterpieces form a dialogue across four distinct musical stories.

The programme concludes with the Fantasy in B minor, Op. 28 by Alexander Scriabin, a work of sweeping emotional intensity and visionary character. Bridging late Romanticism with Scriabin’s emerging mystical language, the Fantasy offers a fitting culmination to an evening devoted to imagination, storytelling, and the boundless expressive possibilities of the piano.

FEATURING
Bryan Soo, piano*
Zack Teo, piano†
⸻

CHOPIN –

mapped.venue: The Lobby Lounge, Frasers House, a Luxury Collection Hotel
mapped.description:
ALL THAT JAZZ AT THE LOBBY LOUNGE🎙️
Introducing All That Jazz at The Lobby Lounge, a month-long weekend series featuring internationally acclaimed jazz artists interpreting timeless classics from the genre’s great masters. Each evening brings soulful vocals and a world-class jazz band in an intimate lounge setting, paired with fine wines and bespoke cocktails in a relaxed, elegant atmosphere.

🎙️All That Jazz July Lineup🎙️

17 & 18 July - Michelle SgP
A Singaporean vocalist, pianist, and producer, Michelle blends jazz, soul, and contemporary influences. Her debut album, Multiplicity, featured 68 international contributors and earned multiple VIMA Music Award nominations. A long-standing vocal lecturer at LASALLE College of the Arts, she has performed alongside acclaimed musicians including Kenny Werner and Marcus Printup.

24 & 25 - James Flynn
James Flynn is an international jazz vocalist and band leader with over three decades of experience across Europe, Asia, and Australia. Originally from Scotland and based in Western Australia, he works primarily within jazz performance traditions. His career includes performances with Ray Charles and Demis Roussos, featured solo work with the West Australian Symphony Orchestra, and appearances at the Singapore International Jazz Festival, Sing Jazz, and the Dave Koz Cruise with The Summer Horns ensemble. 

31 July - Reggie Pryor
Singapore-based baritone Reggie Pryor delivers a versatile repertoire spanning jazz standards, musical theatre, and classical crossover. A founding member of the award-winning vocal group Regu A, he brings an extensive background in international choral performance to his solo work. His debut album, Love Anthology, highlights his range and deep appreciation for classic jazz vocal traditions.


🎙️All That Jazz August Lineup🎙️

1 Aug - Reggie Pryor
Reggie Pryor is a baritone vocalist whose repertoire spans jazz standards, musical theatre and classical crossover. A founding member of the award-winning vocal group Regu A, he brings an extensive background in international choral performance to his solo work. His debut album, Love Anthology, reflects his versatility and deep appreciation for classic jazz vocal traditions.

7 & 8 Aug - Jacintha
Jacintha is a jazz vocalist known for her warm, intimate style and understated phrasing. She gained international recognition through her recordings with Groove Note Records, including Lush Life, Jacintha Is Her Name, The Girl from Bossa Nova and Jacintha Goes to Hollywood. Her interpretations of jazz standards, bossa nova and film classics are distinguished by elegance, restraint and emotional depth.

14 & 15 Aug - Namie Rasman
Jazz vocalist, composer and educator Namie Rasman brings a contemporary perspective to the jazz tradition. A graduate of LASALLE College of the Arts and a Top 5 finalist at the 2019 Thailand Jazz Competition, she is recognised for her thoughtful musicianship and refined approach to jazz. Her performances blend timeless standards with modern influences, delivered with intimacy and nuance.

21 & 22 Aug - James Flynn
James Flynn is an international jazz vocalist and band leader with more than three decades of experience across Europe, Asia and Australia. Originally from Scotland and now based in Western Australia, his career includes performances with Ray Charles and Demis Roussos, featured appearances with the West Australian Symphony Orchestra, and engagements at the Singapore International Jazz Festival, Sing Jazz and the Dave Koz Cruise with The Summer Horns ensemble.

28 & 29 Aug - Wendy Tan
Wendy Tan is the lead vocalist of The Wild Marigolds, known for her expressive phrasing and understated style. Bringing a fresh perspective to jazz standards and soulful classics, she combines musical sensitivity with an engaging, unforced stage presence. Her thoughtful interpretations and refined musicianship make every performance warm, personal and memorable.

⸻

Seats are limited. Reserve now for a month of world-class live jazz in one of the city’s most elegant settings.

mapped.title: The Art of Wealth
mapped.venueName: The Mind Synergy Centre @ Tai Seng, #05-03
mapped.venue: The Mind Synergy Centre @ Tai Seng, #05-03
mapped.description:
Not everything needs to be solved by thinking.
Some things… are seen more clearly when expressed.

This is not a typical art session.
There are no techniques to master,
no right or wrong outcome to achieve.
Instead, this is a guided space
to slow down, observe, and express —
through colour, form, and whatever naturally emerges.
As you create, you may begin to notice:

  • how you approach uncertainty

  • what you hold on to, and what you avoid

  • the quiet patterns that shape your inner world

Without needing to analyse or explain,
what appears on the paper often reveals more than expected.

What you’ll experience

  • A calm, structured space for self-expression

  • Light guidance to support awareness and reflection

  • A different way to process thoughts — without overthinking

  • A guided drawing experience to express, reflect, and bring home your own meaningful creation

All materials are provided.
No prior art experience is needed.

mapped.title: Batik Painting with Kamal Dollah

An Authentic Batik Experience!
Discover the beauty of Batik—a cornerstone of Southeast Asian art and heritage—at Singapore’s dedicated batik-making facility.
Step inside a genuine working studio where the craft is practiced in its truest form and find inspiration in the professional masterpieces on display.
Guided by renowned artist Kamal Dollah, you will go beyond the basics to understand the soul of this craft. This 2.5-hour workshop offers a rare, hands-on opportunity to create your own color-fast, wearable artwork. Working within an environment designed specifically for the batik process, you are guaranteed an experience that is as educational as it is creative.

mapped.title: Beans
mapped.description:
Beans follows two Lance Corporals decommissioning a military ration bunker 20 metres underneath London as the world ends. They're stuck in their old office they've been clearing out, and they only have 1 can of beans left.

About Bowtie Productions
Founded in 2021, Bowtie Productions (Yes, it is spelt that way) has been bringing new voices to the stage all across the world. Starting in Singapore and then expanding operations to the United Kingdom in 2024, BTP has organized shows of all sizes and genres, from the humble bar show, to Netflix recordings in theatres, to wrestling shows in a Thai discotheque, all the way to the stages of the most prestigious comedy festivals.

With over a decade of experience in live entertainment and management, we aim to run productions that allow performers to do what they do best, without having to deal with any of the fuss or admin. That's our job after all, to be your producers and managers, so that they can do their jobs: being the best entertainers they can be.

About Benedict Lim
Benedict Lim was born and raised in Kota Kinabalu, Sabah, and has been based in Singapore since 2013. Their work spans fiction, theatre, film and live performance, moving between Singapore and the U.K. They hold space for the liminal. They also hold space equally for fried chicken and black coffee, which he thinks cancel each other out.
As a performer, they’ve been in productions such as PACE (2026, narrative feature), Park Life (2025, short film), The Critic (2023, play), Brain Things (2023, play). Also, their producing credits include: The Asian Comedy Showcase (Edinburgh Fringe 2024-2025), Off With Your Head (Edinburgh Fringe 2024-2025)
As a dramaturg, he has worked with Sam See on And I Can't Feel At Home In This World Any More (Edinburgh Fringe 2024).

About Benjamin Soon
Benjamin Soon is a 21-year-old dancer, actor, and circus practitioner who lives for the thrill of performance. He started his journey at the age of 7 and has been going strong ever since.
As a dancer, he was the runner-up in the 2022 East Side Session. As an actor, he was the lead in ’The Trip: What Happened in Larspura’, appearing both in the campaign film and its accompanying immersive theatre experience.
Most recently, he performed with Sangnam and Bornfire Circus in Flipside 2026 at Esplanade. Then, he’ll be joining the esteemed Phare Circus for a summer intensive programme at Siem Reap. 
Beyond that, Benjamin is also pursuing a Diploma in Performance at the LASALLE College of the Arts.

mapped.title: This Is Home - Guitar & Cajon Playing For Beginners @ Tampines Plaza 2
mapped.description:
This is Home is a 60-minute beginner-friendly workshop that invites participants to celebrate Singapore’s spirit through the beloved National Day song “Home” by Kit Chan. Using guitar and cajón, participants will learn simple chords and rhythms before coming together in an uplifting group jam.

Blending music-making with a short reflection on Singapore’s history, the session fosters joy, unity, and national pride. All instruments are provided, making it accessible to youths, seniors, and underserved communities alike.

mapped.title: Art of Caricature with Cartoon.SG
mapped.description:
Discover the hilarious and engaging art of caricature drawing with Kamal Dollah, founder of www.cartoon.sg and Kamal Arts (www.kamal.sg).

This two-hour programme introduces the artform and systematically guides you through the essential techniques and observational skills needed to capture personality and likeness with a playful, exaggerated twist. Whether you're a budding artist or hobbyist, acquire a fun new skill, with the perfect opportunity to learn from a master.

The ability to draw caricatures by raw observation and by hand is still a rare and remarkable skill that embodies personal perception, wit, and spontaneity — qualities that no AI algorithm can fully replicate. It reflects not just technical prowess but a deep understanding of human expression, allowing for a level of satire, exaggeration, and charm that resonates uniquely with viewers.

mapped.title: Chinese Penjing in Singapore
mapped.venueName: City Sprouts Punggol Greenhouse Plot A13
mapped.venue: City Sprouts Punggol Greenhouse Plot A13
mapped.description:
Discover the timeless art of bonsai and its journey from Japan to Singapore’s Garden City. In this 2.5-hour immersive cultural experience, you’ll explore how bonsai traditions have travelled across Asia, blending Japanese precision, Chinese penjing influence, and Singapore’s tropical horticultural heritage. Guided by local bonsai artisans from Crafted Identity, you’ll learn the history, symbolism, and techniques behind bonsai artistry — then create your own miniature tree to take home. This is more than a craft session — it’s a heritage journey that connects art, nature, and culture in the heart of Singapore.

What You’ll Learn from bonsai styling techniques:

  1. Tree Design: Discover the attributes that define a beautiful bonsai and explore various design styles to create your unique miniature tree.
  2. Basic Techniques: Learn fundamental styling, wiring, and shaping techniques to bring your bonsai vision to life.
  3. Container and Soil: Understand the significance of selecting appropriate containers and soil mixes to ensure your bonsai thrives.
  4. Care and Maintenance: Gain insights into the specific care requirements of mini bonsai, including watering, pruning, and repotting.

 
Workshop Details:
• Duration: Approximately 2.5 hours

Materials Provided:

• Pre-bonsai tropical tree (10-15cm tall)
• Bonsai pot
• Bonsai soil mix
• Crafting wire

Instructor Guidance: Our experienced instructor will provide step-by-step assistance, ensuring you leave with your own bonsai creation.

Crafted Identity is a boutique plant-craft studio and lifestyle brand in Singapore dedicated to bringing nature, culture, and artistry into everyday living. Founded by Adrian Tan, the brand operates out of its greenhouse plot at City Sprouts Punggol, where visitors can explore a curated range of handcrafted botanical creations and join immersive workshops.

Workshop Location:
City Sprouts Punggol Greenhouse Plot A13
50 Punggol East, Singapore 828826
 
Our Workshop Environment
Crafted Identity at 50 Punggol East City Sprouts Punggol Plot A13 exudes a rustic, serene charm—an inviting, plant-filled studio nestled within the lively grounds of City Sprouts’ urban farm. Housed in a fan-ventilated greenhouse, the space feels like a lush living atelier, where botanical craft, community spirit, and creative calm come together in a tranquil, nature-infused setting.
 
Driving Directions:
From Punggol East Road, turn left onto Tebing Lane and proceed to the carpark at the extreme left to locate City Sprouts Punggol Greenhouse. (At Punggol Container Park; we are near Wao Fitness)

Walking: Riviera LRT → City Sprouts Punggol Greenhouse
Start at Riviera LRT Station (PE4)
Destination: City Sprouts (Punggol) Greenhouse Plot A13

mapped.title: Streets and Places of SG 2: A City Tour 2026
mapped.description:
Streets and Places of Singapore (total of two volumes) is a collection of writings that explores the stories, memories, and lives connected to Singapore’s streets and places, weaving together a tapestry of the nation’s past, present, and future.
　　In this special tour, TrendLit Publishing joins hands with veteran historian and curator Mr. Lee Kok Leong to lead participants on an immersive walk through some of the streets and stories featured in the collection. This unique experience blends history, literary narrative, and personal reflection—an invitation to step into the very streets where these memories unfolded.

About the Tour Guide: Lee Kok Leong has a strong interest in Cantonese culture. His book Guangdong Majie (广东妈姐) and Breaking the Waves (大眼鸡·越洋人) were shortlisted as the best books in 2016 and 2018, respectively. His research was used to set up the Kwong Wai Shiu Hospital Heritage Gallery and the Kwong Wai Siew Peck San Theng Heritage Gallery. He is the co-author of the book Will Singapore still exist in 100 years? (100年后新加坡还存在吗?) published in 2023.

mapped.title: Creative Stone Seal Carving Workshop
mapped.description:
About the Workshop
In this hands-on session, participants will be introduced to the traditional art of Chinese seal carving, reimagined with modern tools and accessible design motifs. The workshop includes sketching out ideas, learning carving techniques, and printing the finished seal on paper. Each participant will create a unique, hand-carved artwork to take home.

This session focuses on creativity, craftsmanship, and the quiet joy of working with your hands. No prior art experience is needed—just a willingness to try something new in a relaxed, guided setting. All workshop materials will be provided, including carving tools and materials.
It’s a calm, inspiring experience perfect for anyone curious about traditional art forms with a modern twist.

To make your experience even more enjoyable, we offer complimentary drinks, including packet drinks, instant tea, and coffee, at our cosy studio.

Workshop Duration: 2 Hours

About the Instructor
Jean Toh is the founder of Baisimu, a Singapore-based creative studio specialising in handmade stone seals and rubber stamps that blend traditional Chinese artistry with contemporary design. She has conducted workshops with institutions such as the Esplanade's Huayi Festival, the National Heritage Board, and the Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre.
Her solo exhibition, Inches of Singapore, explored local culture through seal art. Jean’s work has been featured in The Straits Times, Lianhe Zaobao, and Hello Singapore, celebrating her efforts to keep Chinese heritage arts alive. Her stamp designs often feature nostalgic local elements like Singlish and traditional snacks, making heritage relatable to modern audiences.

About Artify Studio
Established in 2014, Artify Studio offers a range of art services from children and adult art classes, corporate team-building sessions to community outreach programmes.

mapped.title: Cyanotype Lantern-making Workshop
mapped.venueName: Objectifs Centre for Photography and Film
mapped.venue: Objectifs Centre for Photography and Film
mapped.description:
Celebrate the Mid-Autumn Festival—also known as the Mooncake Festival or Moon Festival—with a creative twist at our Cyanotype Lantern Making Workshop! Held during this cherished time of reunion and reflection, this hands-on art workshop invites families and friends to craft beautiful, personalized lanterns using the unique cyanotype printing process.
Workshop Highlights:

  • Create one-of-a-kind lanterns using cyanotype, a magical UV light-sensitive printing technique that produces striking blue-and-white designs.

  • Experiment with natural materials and everyday objects to compose your own custom prints before transforming them into lanterns.

  • Take part in an evening walk around Objectifs Centre, where participants can showcase their glowing creations and experience the joy of Mid-Autumn traditions.

  • Learn about the cultural significance of the Mid-Autumn Festival and connect with its themes of family, unity, and creativity.

Perfect for families, and creative adults, this lantern-making workshop is suitable for ages 12 and up. No prior experience required—just bring your imagination!
Celebrate this Mid-Autumn Festival in Singapore with a memorable art activity that blends heritage, creativity, and fun under the full moon.

mapped.title: Discover Dizi
mapped.description:
The Dizi (笛子), a traditional Chinese bamboo flute, is an important part of Singapore’s Chinese musical heritage. This hands-on workshop introduces participants to the history, cultural significance, and unique playing techniques of the Dizi. Participants will experience the sound world of the instrument through live demonstrations, storytelling, and guided practice.

In line with SG Culture Pass objectives, the workshop will also incorporate knowledge transfer and appreciation of local arts, including sharing about Singapore’s Chinese music scene, local Chinese orchestras, prominent Singaporean musicians, and opportunities to engage with local arts activities.

By the end of the session, participants will be able to produce basic notes, play simple rhythms, and appreciate the role of the Dizi in Singapore’s cultural tapestry.

mapped.title: DJ Workshop with Ministry of DJs - Pro DJ School
mapped.venueName: Ministry of DJs - Pro DJ School
mapped.venue: Ministry of DJs - Pro DJ School
mapped.description:
This introductory DJ workshop offers participants an immersive, hands-on journey into the art and culture of DJing. Designed as a 60-minute experience, the session not only covers the technical fundamentals of mixing, but also explores the rich history of Singapore’s nightlife and the evolution of its DJ heritage.
Participants will gain insight into how DJ culture shaped iconic eras of Singapore’s club scene — from the golden days of Centro, Ministry of Sound, Zirca, dbl O and Bang Bang — and how local talents rose to international recognition. The workshop highlights the influence and success of Singapore DJs who have made their mark regionally and globally, contributing to the vibrant identity of the nation’s nightlife landscape.

On the technical front, attendees will be introduced to industry-standard equipment, including the CDJ-3000/CDJ-3000X and DJM-A9 setups used by top-tier DJs worldwide. Through a live demonstration followed by guided practical application, participants will learn essential skills such as cueing, beatmatching, track transitions and basic mixing techniques using professional DJ software and hardware.

The workshop is led by veterans of Singapore’s club scene: Kennerve (ex Centro / Ministry of Sound / Zirca Singapore), Dalley Sani (ex Ministry of Sound / Zirca Singapore), Effen (ex Ministry of Sound / Zirca Singapore) and GemStarr (ex dbl O / Zirca / Bang Bang Singapore). Collectively bringing over 25 years of experience each, they share firsthand stories, industry insights, and deep-rooted knowledge of Singapore’s DJ culture and nightlife heritage.

Ideal for schools, community groups, and corporate events, this interactive session is beginner-friendly and requires no prior experience. Participants will be organised into two small groups of five (maximum 10 participants), each guided by a dedicated instructor to ensure focused attention, meaningful engagement, and effective learning.

mapped.title: Everyone Can Take Wow Pictures (Safra Punggol)
mapped.description:
Everyone Can Take Wow Pictures is a beginner-friendly mobile photography workshop that focuses on seeing rather than equipment.
Using only a smartphone, participants will learn the fundamentals of lighting, framing, and simple editing techniques to create WOW pictures.

The session is taught by Tan Chin Hock, award-winning photographer and inspiring founder of the social enterprise Holdinghands Studio. Chin Hock has taught over 1,000 learners, with an average 4.9-star Google rating, and is known for his engaging, practical, and confidence-building teaching style.

Through guided examples and hands-on practice, participants will learn how intentional observation and simple creative choices can create compelling images.

No prior photography experience is required, and no technical knowledge is assumed.

The emphasis is on practical skills that participants can immediately apply in their daily lives using the phone they already own. It also introduces participants to local art, encouraging a deeper appreciation of the local creative scene.

LEARNING OUTCOMES
By the end of the workshop, participants will be able to:
-Use their mobile phone more intentionally to capture clear, well-composed photographs
-Apply basic principles of lighting and framing
-Perform simple edits on mobile to enhance mood and clarity without over-processing
-Develop greater confidence in expressing how they see the world through images

mapped.title: Experiencing Nyonya Kebaya Fashion
mapped.description:
Immerse yourself in the rich tradition of Peranakan culture with this hands-on workshop dedicated to the iconic Nyonya Kebaya. Participants will explore the fascinating history and significance of the Kebaya, discover ways to wear it, and enjoy the unique experience of pairing it with the sarong. Topics include the essence of Nyonya Kebaya and Peranakan culture, personalising the Nyonya Kebaya through Body Proportion Analysis, Fit-and-Flatter Strategies, Colour Profiling and Sulam Design.
 
This workshop will be facilitated by Mr. Raymond Wong who has embroidered almost every Kebaya worn by the female cast members of the popular Emerald Hill drama series. He was awarded the Stewards of Intangible Cultural Heritage Award 2023 by the National Heritage Board.
 
Join us for an unforgettable cultural experience to learn the art of wearing the Nyonya Kebaya and gain a deeper appreciation for the heritage it represents.

mapped.title: Wheels on the Bus - The Musical
mapped.description:
Inspired by the ever-popular song of the same name, ‘Wheels on the Bus – The Musical’ introduces small theatregoers to a high energy interactive show that takes the audience on a journey through bus driver training school where the young ‘cadets’ must learn their right from their left and how to count to 10, not to mention some groovy dance moves before they can graduate and officially drive the bus! Through the use of song and dance to engage the young audience, the children are taught things such as letters, numbers, and shapes.

Each child will receive a bag of “toys” (included in ticket) which help them interact with the actors on stage through each activity and song – and the kids get to take this “toolkit” with them so they can continue the fun at home!
 
Songs, laughs and fun for children aged 2 to 6 and their grown-ups, in the much-loved show that’s wowed audiences in the USA and Singapore!
 
Show Objectives
Here at Freddo Children’s Theatre, we believe in the magic of live theatre to spark imagination and joy in our littlest audience members. Our primary goal is to provide a joyful, immersive, and highly interactive theatrical experience that introduces toddlers and preschoolers to the magic of the performing arts. We aim to:-

  • Engage and Entertain: Keep young attention spans captivated through high-energy musical numbers, vibrant visuals, and continuous audience participation.

  • Encourage Active Participation: By inviting our young cadets to sing, dance, and solve problems alongside the cast.

  • Create Shared Memories: Provide a fun, laugh-filled environment where children and their grown-ups can bond over a shared love of music and play.

 
What Your Child Will 'Practice and Play'
Wheels on the Bus – The Musical is as educational as it is entertaining! Disguised as a fun-filled bus driver training school, the show helps our young cadets develop crucial early-learning skills to graduate. By the end of the show, children will have practiced:-
 

  • Early Literacy & Numbers: Cadets will practice their ABC and counting from 1 to 10 as they navigate their way through training.

  • Directional Awareness: Through interactive games, children will learn to distinguish their left from their right.

  • Basic Geometry: Catchy songs and visual aids are used to help kids identify basic letters and shapes.

  • Social Skills: Turn-taking, listening and engaging as part of an audience.

  • Movement Coordination: We get everyone up on their feet to learn some "groovy dance moves," promoting physical coordination, balance, and rhythm – and fun!!



“We had loads of fun at the Wheels on the Bus Children's Musical! Presented by Freddo Children's Theatre, the musical … had us singing and dancing from start to finish.” Sunny City Kids, December 2022 Singapore

"Any parent would have heard the wheels on the bus a million times! But this is definitely the only one that makes you look forward to it. This 45 minute bus ride came with a goodie bag that adds on to the interactivity of the theatre experience. We were 100% ENTERTAINED. This was wheely amazing!" Fiona, December 2022 Singapore
