mapped.title: FUCHUAN & SINGAPORE: VOYAGES THAT SHAPED A NATION 福船与新加坡的故事
mapped.description:
Overview
Experience Fu Ning Boat—An Intangible Cultural Heritage Masterpiece
Fuchuan & Singapore: Voyages that Shaped a Nation is a heritage experience centred on Fu Ning, one of the world’s largest traditional wooden sailing vessels and a living representation of UNESCO-recognised shipbuilding craftsmanship.
The exhibition traces the journeys of early Chinese migrants who travelled across the seas on vessels like the Fuchuan, shaping the foundations of modern Singapore. Through artefacts and showcase of traditional craftsmanship, it brings to life the human stories, maritime heritage, and cultural exchanges that defined these voyages.
Positioned at the intersection of history, culture, and design, the experience offers visitors a meaningful connection to Singapore’s origins—told through the lens of the sea and the enduring legacy of the Fuchuan.
《福船与新加坡的故事》以世界上最大的传统木帆船之一“福宁号”为核心，呈现一段融合历史与文化的沉浸式体验。福宁号亦是联合国教科文组织认可的传统造船技艺的活态体现。
展览回溯早期华人移民乘坐福船渡海来到新加坡的历程，展现这些航程如何塑造今日的新加坡。通过叙事、文物与传统工艺展示，呈现人与海洋、文化与迁徙之间的深刻联系。
该体验融合历史、文化与设计，让观众从海洋视角重新认识新加坡的起源，以及福船所承载的时代记忆与文化价值。

mapped.title: Gateway Kids Club – July Storytelling 2026
mapped.description:
Together We Can – Stories in July
“Our ability to reach unity in diversity will be the beauty and the test of our civilization.” – Mahatma Gandhi
This month at Gateway Kids Club,we celebrate teamwork,unity and diversity! Through the stories,kids will learn what it means to accept each other’s differences,and learn how to foster a more inclusive and united society. Join our storytellers for a month of wonder-filled adventures together!

NEW SESSIONS ADDED WEEKLY
Registration for each storytelling session will open two weeks in advance.

  • 4th July, 10:00 am – The Chief’s Daughter by Storyteller Shien
    Long ago on Temasek island, boys were chosen to be warriors—but the Chief’s Daughter wants to be one!

  • 4th July, 11:00 am – 红头巾· Samsui Women by Storyteller William · 故事人伟光
    红头巾是从中国来新加坡工作的女性。她们戴红色头巾，穿深色衣服，在烈日下辛勤劳作，为新加坡建设作出重要贡献。· The Samsui women were Chinese migrant workers, known for the red headscarves they wore. They laboured tirelessly under the sun, helping to build early Singapore.

  • 11th July, 10:00 am – The Great Giggle Festival Rescue by Storyteller Misha
    When the Giggle Rocket stops working, Zorp the alien sets off on an adventure across Sparkle Galaxy to save the Giggle Festival!

  • 11th July, 11:00 am – 彩虹恐龙和我们不一样吗？· Is That Rainbowsaurus So Different From Us? by Storyteller Ying Er · 故事人瑛铒
    小小彩虹恐龙搬进新社区. 其他恐龙都躲着她，因为它们从没见过像她这样的人。她真的那么不一样吗？· The little Rainbowsaurus moves into a new neighbourhood, but the other saures avoid her—they’ve never seen anyone like her! Is she really that different?

  • 18th July, 10:00 am – The Proud Butterfly by Storyteller Nat
    In a beautiful garden, Betty Butterfly learns how to be a gracious neighbour.

  • 25th July, 10:00 am – Together, we CAN! by Storyteller Nat
    Kenny the soda can is excited for a new round of recycling, but finds himself in the wrong bin. Can he and his new friends sort things out in time?

 
Register now and enjoy these stories for FREE!

About Gateway Kids Club
Gateway Kids Club is a free programme held at Gateway Theatre every Saturday. You can also join the Arts & Crafts session or Free Play activities after the storytelling session! Each month,activities are focused on a different theme.

More information on Gateway Kids Club available here.

To stay updated on our programmes,follow us on Facebook or Instagram!

mapped.title: Gateway Kids Club – August Storytelling 2026
mapped.description:
🎵 This is home, truly, where I know I must be 🎵
It’s Singapore’s birthday month, and we’re celebrating it at Gateway Kids Club with a month of stories about our beloved country. From legends of our past, to tales about the things that make us uniquely Singapore, join our storytellers on an exciting adventure of understanding this Little Red Dot that we call home!

NEW SESSIONS ADDED WEEKLY
Registration for each storytelling session will open two weeks in advance.

  • 1st August, 10:00 am – Breakfast of Tengkus by Storyteller Nat
    The Tengku’s chef, Ah Kun, is challenged to create a dish that the Tengku will never tire of eating. What will he whip up?

  • 1st August, 11:00 am – 河水山· Bukit Ho Swee by Storyteller William · 故事人伟光
    你知道新加坡的政府组屋是怎么来的吗？来了解 1961年发生的一场大火，看看它如何改变人们的生活，并塑造现代新加坡。· Ever wondered how HDB flats came about in Singapore? Come hear about a great fire that happened in 1961 and how it changed the way Singaporeans lived.

  • 8th August, 10:00 am – Sang Nila Utama and The Raging Seas by Storyteller Misha
    How did we earn our name, ‘Singapura’?  Come hear the legend of Prince Sang Nila Utama to find out!

  • 8th August, 11:00 am – 巴当的传奇· The Legend of Badang by Storyteller William · 故事人伟光
    听说过大力士巴当的故事吗？快来听有关新加坡的传奇吧！· Have you ever heard of the mighty Badang? Come and hear this legend from Singapore’s past!

  • 15th August, 10:00 am – Karang Guni Boy & the Wonders of Singapore’s Rainforest by Storyteller Tia
    From giving old things new life to uncovering the unique creatures that call Singapore home, find out what treasures can be found here!

  • 22nd August, 10:00 am – A Kaya-ful Adventure by Storyteller Marissa
    Kaia the Kaya goes on a journey through the hawker centre, discovering delicious cuisines that are uniquely Singaporean!

  • 29th August, 10:00 am – The Secret of Kusu by Storyteller Angel from Zip Zap Zoom Collective
    Do you know the legend of Kusu Island? What if there was even more to the story? Would you like to hear it?

Register now and enjoy these stories for FREE!

mapped.title: Guided Autobiography
mapped.description:
Our lives are stories that we continually revise, edit, plot and reshape in response to events that are often beyond our control. Experiences of living with mental health conditions, whether directly or as a caregiver, or both, often interrupt the life stories that we have told ourselves, and leave us searching for new ways of knowing. 

Guided Autobiography is a form of writing practice with therapeutic effects originally developed by psychiatrist Jim Birren at the University of Southern California, and which is now practiced worldwide. It draws upon tacit narrative knowledge we all possess in order to unsettle the stories we have become part of, to begin to confront unfinished business, and to rediscover strengths and values that come from our earlier experiences that can guide us in the future.
This programme will cover six sessions in a supportive and sharing environment. Each week, there will be a warm up with writing activities and sharing experiences of writing for the first hour. Each group member will write a short autobiographical fragment in response to a theme, and share what they have written by reading aloud during the second half of the session. Each group member makes a commitment to write regularly and attend each session.

Details

Time: 10am - 12.30pm (2.5 hours)
Venue: The Collective, L1, The Foundry 11 Prinsep Link

  • Week 1: Sat, 15 August - Introductions

  • Week 2: Sat, 22 August - Branching Points

    • Finding out the branching points in our lives, and the events and circumstances that caused them.

  • Week 3: Sat, 29 August - Family

    • Exploring who we consider as family and their influence on us.

  • Week 4: Sat, 5 September - Beginnings

    • Focusing on mental health, writing about beginnings of challenges we’ve faced, and how they may result in changes in life.

  • Week 5: Sat, 12 September - Living Through

    • Progressing to the “middles”, writing about vulnerabilities and also strengths, about supports and absences.

  • Week 6: Sat, 19 September - Living On

    • Closing with writing about the future, exploring hopes and aspirations.


To ensure an intimate experience with maximum individual attention, this programme is limited to just 5 participants. We therefore ask for a commitment to all six sessions so you can get the full benefit of the writing process

The Facilitator

Philip Holden taught literary and narrative studies at NUS over a twenty-year period. In addition to an ongoing commitment to historical research and creative writing, Philip is now a Registered Clinical Counsellor in British Columbia, Canada, and serves as editor for The Tapestry Project, working in a space in which writing and mental health meet. This Guided Autobiography group is an expression of his ongoing interest in the therapeutic power of storytelling.

mapped.title: Stained Glass Soldering
mapped.description:
🌟 Workshop Overview
Step into the timeless art of stained glass, a craft that has illuminated cathedrals, temples, and homes for over a thousand years. In this workshop, you’ll not only create your own colorful glass artwork but also learn about the rich cultural history of Stained Glass in Singapore and artistic principles behind glass soldering as an expressive medium.
By the end, you’ll have a completed stained glass piece that reflects your creativity, ready to display in your homes!

Reservation Procedure

  • Slots are open 1-2wks prior for booking
  • Important Note: Upon purchasing the ticket, kindly verify the reservation with the operator directly via WhatsApp (+65 9070 2238). Failure to do so may result in a void ticket.
    • Kindly obtain final confirmation with the operator directly. Booking is not considered complete without final confirmation code from operator.
    • Please provide your e-ticket's bar code when making a reservation.
  • Bookings must be made at least 2 days in advance
  • Bookings are final and are not eligible for rescheduling.

Cancellation policy
No cancellations or refunds.

What to Expect
1. Welcome & Introduction (5mins)
We’ll begin with a warm welcome and a short talk on the story of stained glass: its origins, cultural significance and modern revival.
2. Stained Glass in Local Context (25mins)
Showcasing stained glass around Singapore, Local Artists and Singapore's historic ties to stained glass
3. Choose Your Design (5mins)
Before choosing your pattern, we’ll explore each piece and take a look at the finished pieces to choose from.
Although these patterns are fixed, some provide an opportunity to customize as your heart desires, creating a design that is from the heart, one of a kind!
4. Learn the Art of Soldering (15mins)
Your instructor will introduce soldering techniques, rooted in the Tiffany method developed in the late 19th century:

  • Understanding the role of copper foil, flux, and solder.
  • Practicing steady, even soldering lines that both strengthen and beautify your design.
  • Learning about safety and care with hot tools.

You’ll discover how this technique revolutionized glass art by allowing for finer, more intricate designs.
4. Create Your Piece (60-90 mins)
Now it’s your turn to bring your design to life!
You’ll solder and assemble your glass pieces, guided step-by-step by your instructor. As you work, we’ll discuss how artists use texture and linework to give character and emotional depth to their work.
5. Finishing Touches & Show and Tell (10mins)
After soldering, you’ll clean and polish your piece, then attach a hanging chain.
We’ll end with a short “gallery walk,” celebrating everyone’s completed artworks. 
 --- 
All Materials Provided

  • Pre-cut, pre-foiled stained glass kit (choose 1 of 4 designs - depending on stock availability)
  • Soldering iron & supplies
  • Chain for hanging
  • Cleaning materials

mapped.title: Heritage in a Cup

Celebrate Singapore’s rich culture through clay!

INTERNATIONAL AUTOMODIFIED (IAM) SINGAPORE

The world's fastest growing car tuning & lifestyle show series is set to hold its Singapore round in Singapore Expo, for the 2026 season.

International Automodified is the number 1 car show franchise in the region. It aims to provide a high quality and professional platform for automotive hobbyists/enthusiasts, automotive products manufacturers, dealers and automotive service providers to converge and exchange the latest trends and technology in the automotive dress-up and tuning industry.

mapped.title: JASS Spotlight Series: JUNK
mapped.description:
Experience the future of jazz in Singapore with the JASS Spotlight Series, a monthly live showcase celebrating outstanding youth jazz talent at the iconic The Jazz Loft @ Blu Jaz. Each edition spotlights a rising star in an intimate setting, championing the next generation of jazz excellence. 

This April, catch JUNK, a Singapore-based jazz-funk ensemble led by classical-contemporary pianist Amery Reuben. Blending funk, Latin rhythms, New Orleans second-line grooves, progressive rock and jazz fusion, JUNK delivers high-energy, groove-driven performances that are both rhythmically tight and irresistibly danceable.

The band performs original compositions alongside bold reimagining’s inspired by contemporary jazz powerhouses such as Snarky Puppy, Chick Corea and The Funky Knuckles. Having appeared at venues and festivals including Esplanade’s Jazz in July, JUNK has quickly built a reputation as one of Singapore’s most exciting jazz-funk outfits.

JUNK
Amery Reuben – Piano
Avery Chan – Guitar
Thomas Low – Guitar
Doran Oon – Bass
Tristan Seow – Drums

Get ready for an evening of funk, groove and fearless improvisation!
Jazz Association (Singapore) is a registered charity with IPC status dedicated to promoting the participation, engagement, and excellence of jazz in Singapore. 
 
This event is eligible for SG Culture Pass, attendees may use SG Culture Pass credits.

mapped.title: Roll with History: A Popiah Journey Through Joo Chiat
mapped.description:
Step into the world of popiah — Singapore’s most beloved spring roll!
Join this immersive tour at Kway Guan Huat Joo Chiat Popiah, the birthplace of traditional popiah skins since 1938. In this 1-hour experience, participants will:
🥢 Watch the live art of paper-thin popiah skin-making, spun on a searing hot griddle.
🌿 Learn about Joo Chiat’s Peranakan heritage and how food connects generations.
👨‍🍳 Wrap your own popiah using heritage recipes — a hands-on, delicious adventure!

SYNOPSIS
In 1935,the Straits Museum of Malaya launches an exclusive exhibition featuring the infamous Monkey Paw,known to many as a cursed object that grants its user’s wishes,with unfortunately deadly and ironic consequences. During a time of great economic uncertainty,many wish to get their hands on the mysterious Monkey Paw as a means to satisfy their own desires.
PRODUCTION CREDITS
 
Producer: AGAM Theatre Lab
Presented by: AGAM Theatre Lab
Directors: Akhilesh and Vinith Kumar
Playwrights: Nallu Dhinkharan and Sabarna
Production & Stage Manager: Gomathi

​
Cast : Shaikh Yasin,Shobha Thiban,Krishmita Shiv Ram,Karls Karthikeyan,Nivedha Nagarajan,SRS Maheshwaran,Sabarees Kumar

AGAM Theatre Lab (AGAM)
​
AGAM Theatre Lab (AGAM) is a young Singaporean Tamil theatre company dedicated to creating a sustainable platform for Tamil theatre within Singapore's vibrant arts landscape. Established in January 2019,AGAM has grown exponentially,producing trailblazing works that continually invigorate and enhance the presence of Tamil theatre in the local arts scene. Accorded with charity status and an accredited Institute of Public Character (IPC),AGAM became the first Tamil theatre company recognised under the Major company scheme under the National Arts Council. Over the past years,AGAM has not only produced traditional Tamil theatre works but has also innovated new forms of production and direction,pushing the boundaries of Tamil theatrical expression. The company has undertaken various initiatives to nurture and support young and aspiring artists,fostering the growth and development of talents within the community. Its work is characterised by its commitment to nurturing emerging talents,supporting new writing,and creating platforms for underrepresented voices. At the core of AGAM's work is the aspiration to create professional plays of the highest quality for contemporary audiences. Despite being a relatively young theatre company,AGAM's core team possesses a wealth of experience spanning organisational management and theatre-making,driving its remarkable success over the past five years. AGAM has successfully collaborated with various arts organisations,including Esplanade – Theatres on the Bay,and has received recognition for its contributions,including the ST Life! Theatre Awards.

mapped.title: Les Musicables and Friends: When SG Was Young
mapped.description:
The 60s and 70s in Singapore were a time of upheaval and a time of change. From self-government to independence to nation-building, Singapore had to navigate what it meant to stand on its own two feet complete with its quirks and idiosyncrasies as an island-state.

With the help of suggestions from the audience, join Les Musicables as well as some friends from the local improv scene, as we reimagine what it was like to live, work, play, grow-up, and hope for a better future, in a time when the Singapore of today would not have been imaginable. Through the power of musical theatre, build the world of 60s and 70s Singapore together with our improvisers, as we come up with story, music, lyrics, and dance moves all on the spot.

mapped.title: Mazu Heritage Tour: Goddess of the Sea An ICH Journey Through Singapores Maritime Past
mapped.description:
Discover the fascinating world of Mazu (妈祖), the Goddess of the Sea, worshipped for protecting sailors and guiding fishermen safely home. Today, she remains a symbol of protection, resilience and the enduring bond between people and the sea. Begin your journey at the Thian Hock Keng Temple, where early seafarers prayed for safe voyages. This temple is a masterpiece of traditional Hokkien architecture. The final stop is the breathtaking Fu Ning, the world's largest traditional chinese wooden sailing boat docked at Raffles Marina. Step on board this majestic vessel, a floating altar, here, Mazu’s spirit watches over all who continue to seek the sea. This isn’t just a heritage tour — it’s a journey through myths and belief, inviting you to experience Singapore as its early seafarers did and rediscover the sea’s sacred beauty.
*The tour requires a minimum of 10 participants to proceed.
探索妈祖（妈祖）的神奇世界。妈祖被尊奉为海上守护神，守护航海者，并引领渔民平安归来。时至今日，她依然象征着守护、坚韧，以及人与大海之间深厚而持久的联系。
旅程从天福宫（Thian Hock Keng Temple）开始，早期的航海者曾在此祈求航行平安。这座庙宇是传统福建建筑的杰作。
最后一站是停泊在莱佛士码头（Raffles Marina）的壮观福宁号（Fu Ning），世界上最大的传统木制帆船。登上这艘宏伟的船只，如同踏上一座漂浮的祭坛，在这里，妈祖守护着所有继续与大海相伴的人们。
这不仅是一场文化遗产之旅，更是一段穿越神话与信仰的旅程，让您以早期航海者的视角体验新加坡，并重新感受大海神圣而迷人的魅力。
本行程需至少10位参与者方可成团。

mapped.title: Ocean of Songs 2026
mapped.description:
Program description
Metro Philharmonic Society (MPS) proudly presents its long-waited concert - Ocean of Songs 2026
The highlight of this concert,Ocean of Songs 2026,is the long choral work “The Peacock’s East-Southerly Flight” by Leong Yoon Pin,the founding composer of the Metro Philharmonic Society. We have specially invited Ng Eng Kee to conduct “The Peacock’s East-Southerly Flight” which is the first long narrative poem in the history of Chinese literature. This folk song not only tells a sad and moving love tragedy,but also deeply reveals the contradictions of the feudal society through its exquisite artistic expression.
MPS will also present a selection of songs with the theme,water. There are “The Blue Danube” (a classical waltz),“Bengawan Solo” (an Indonesian folk song) and “Zai Shui Yi Fang” (a popular Chinese song). Besides these songs,there is also a classic Chinese song from a Tang poem,“Fu De Gu Yuan Cao Song Bie” (a farewell song).
In the spirit of co-operation and exchange,Xiyao Culture Association and Chordinate Singers were invited to perform on the same stage and participate in the singing performance of “The Peacock’s East-Southerly Flight”.
MPS also invited soprano soloist,Teng Xiang Ting and tenor soloist,Jonathan Charles Tay to perform for the night. They are both local professional classical singers who actively perform and collaborate with many local arts organisations to share the beauty and diversity within classical vocal music.
Let us come together to enjoy the performance and have a lovely evening.
 
节目简介
 
星市音乐会荣幸呈现期待已久的音乐会——“歌声的海洋2026”。
 
今晚，“歌声的海洋2026”的亮点是星市音乐会创始作曲家梁荣平创作的长篇合唱作品《孔雀东南飞》。星市特邀黄荣基指挥演绎《孔雀东南飞》，它也是中国文学史上第一首长篇叙事诗。这首民歌不仅讲述了一个悲怆动人的爱情悲剧，更以其精湛的艺术表现力，深刻揭示了封建社会的矛盾。
 
星市音乐会还将呈现一系列以水为主题的歌曲，包括圆舞曲《蓝色的多瑙河》、印尼民歌《梭罗河》和华语流行歌曲《在水一方》。除了这些歌曲之外，还有一首改编自唐诗的经典中国歌曲《賦得古原草送别》，这是一首送别歌曲。
 
本着合作交流的精神，喜耀文化学会和合悦唱团也受邀同台演出，共同参与《孔雀东南飞》的演唱。
 
星市音乐会还邀请了女高音独唱家丁湘庭和男高音独唱家郑渊智为当晚献艺。他们都是本地专业的古典声乐家，积极与众多本地艺术团体合作演出，分享古典声乐的魅力与多样性。
 
让我们齐聚一堂，欣赏这精彩演出，共度美好的夜晚。

mapped.title: Our Stories, Our City
mapped.venueName: The Mind Synergy Centre @ Tai Seng, #05-03
mapped.venue: The Mind Synergy Centre @ Tai Seng, #05-03
mapped.description:
Our Stories, Our City
Night Time Stories of Grit, Growth and Glory

In Singapore, we pass thousands of people every day.
On the MRT. In hawker centres. In office lifts. Along HDB corridors.
But behind every familiar face is a story you’ve never heard.
A story of sacrifice.
Of survival.
Of reinvention.
Of courage quietly carried through everyday life.
Our Stories, Our City is an intimate live storytelling experience where ordinary Singaporeans step forward to share the defining moments that shaped who they are today.
From heartbreak to hope, career failures to second chances, kampung memories to modern-day struggles — each storyteller brings a raw, honest piece of Singapore life onto the stage.
This is not a polished performance.
It is real people. Real voices. Real stories.
Set against the warmth of a night-time gathering, the experience unfolds through a curated lineup of storytellers from different walks of life — each sharing deeply personal journeys of grit, growth, humour, identity and triumph.
Between stories, audiences are invited to pause, reflect, laugh, connect, and perhaps see a little of themselves in someone else’s journey.
And when the evening opens up…
The mic opens too.
Audience members will have the opportunity to step forward and share their own spontaneous stories — transforming the night into a co-created experience shaped by the people in the room.
Because the most powerful stories are not found in headlines.
They are found in us.
Whether you come to listen, reflect, connect, or bravely share your own story, Our Stories, Our City promises an unforgettable evening of humanity, vulnerability and Singapore spirit.
Come as a listener.
Leave feeling seen.
🎟️ Limited seats available.
🎤 Storyteller slots are open to members of the public.
📍 Join us for a night where Singapore stories come alive"

SYNOPSIS

A provocative psychological drama by NAC’s cultural medallion recipient Haresh Sharma that explores fear, prejudice, and the invisible walls we build around ourselves. Set in a tense domestic space, the play uses the presence of a lizard as a metaphor for suspicion and xenophobia, slowly revealing how paranoia can distort relationships and expose buried insecurities. First staged by The Necessary Stage, the work is a sharp commentary on identity, otherness, and the fragile line between perceived threat and imagined danger.

mapped.title: Raffles Heritage Sunset Tour 2026
mapped.description:
Step into the world of Sir Stamford Raffles with An Evening at the Lighthouse: Sunset, Dinner & Boat. A guided experience set against the golden coastline of western Singapore.

Hosted at Raffles Marina, the evening traces Raffles’ vision of transforming Singapore into a strategic maritime trading port. Through intimate storytelling and conversations at Discovery Pub, uncover how early trade routes, seafaring communities, and colonial ambition shaped the nation’s beginnings.

Climb to the top of the lighthouse for exclusive access to its highest point, where sweeping panoramic views and a stunning sunset await. Discover how navigational beacons like this bring to life Sir Stamford Raffles’ vision of Singapore as a key maritime hub.

The journey continues board Fu Ning, the world’s largest traditional Chinese wooden sailing boat. Guests will have the opportunity to explore the vessel and capture memorable photos on board as the sun sets over the ocean.

The evening concludes with a relaxed waterfront dinner at the marina’s bistro, offering time to unwind and reflect on Singapore’s transformation from a trading outpost to a global port city.

This programme offers a rare opportunity to experience heritage, maritime history and dining in one memorable evening by the sea.

The tour requires a minimum of 10 participants to proceed.

  • 📖 Raffles Heritage Storytelling at Discovery Pub – Enjoy light refreshments while discovering the vision of Sir Stamford Raffles, and how Singapore’s early maritime roots shaped its development into a global trading hub.

  • 📸 Photo Opportunities on Board – Capture stunning photos aboard this magnificent vessel.

  • ⚓ Exclusive Visit to Fu Ning – Explore the world’s largest traditional Chinese wooden sailing boat. Learn about its rich maritime heritage craftsmanship inspired by the legendary treasure ships of the famed explorer Zheng He.

  • 🌅 Lighthouse Sunset Experience – Climb to the top of the Raffles Marina Lighthouse and enjoy breathtaking panoramic sunset views over the sea from its highest point.

  • 🍽 Dinner at Bistro – Relax and enjoy a delightful dinner at the marina’s bistro.

mapped.title: Art of Tessellation Design Workshop
mapped.description: This workshop introduces participants to the fascinating art of tessellation design where repeating geometric patterns are created. Through show and tell for participants to appreciate the tessellation design being used in many architectures, cultures, including Singapore Peranakan tiles. This workshop will guide participants to do a simple tessellation design through drawing and paper folding. By professional and experienced artist Mr. Paul Lee.

mapped.title: Crafting Moments: The Art of Letterpress
mapped.description:

During this intimate session, you'll be walked through the different machines and techniques that make our work unique. From our iconic letterpress to foil stamping, embossing, die-cutting, and more, our team will explain the artistry and craftsmanship behind every print. What’s Included:

Book your visit today and see where the magic happens!

mapped.title: The Art & Soul of Beaded Shoes
mapped.description:
Hidden in every glittering bead and careful stitch of a Kasut Manik is a love letter from the past - a story of culture, craftsmanship, and quiet resilience.

Worn proudly by the Peranakan ladies, these beaded shoes are more than just beautiful accessories; they are heirlooms of identity, crafted with patience, adorned with symbolism, and passed down through generations.

Each pair is a canvas of delicate artistry of vibrant birds, blooming peonies, and mythical creatures, all meticulously formed using tiny glass beads and fine thread, often by hand over many painstaking hours. In a world that moves fast, the Kasut Manik invites us to slow down, to be mindful, to admire, and to connect with heritage in a tangible, heartfelt way, one bead at a time.

When we honour these shoes, we honour the women who made them — their artistry, their stories, and their silent strength. In each pair lives the spirit of sustainability too: did you know these shoes can be"re-shoed"up to three times, allowing the beaded upper to continue shining while the soles are renewed? It is a sustainable practice long before sustainability was a buzzword.

Discover the art and soul of Kasut Manik alongside Mr. Raymond Wong, one of the Stewards of Intangible Cultural Heritage Award 2023 by the National Heritage Board. Mr. Wong has also embroidered almost every Kebaya worn by the female cast members of the popular Emerald Hill drama series.

Participants will learn the cultural significance of beaded shoes, recognise what makes each pair unique in design and symbolism. Gain hands-on experience with materials, beadwork and stitching techniques by making a mini beaded art. Explore how to extend the concept of beaded artistry beyond shoes such as bags, and even modern accessories. Topics include Cultural Significance of Kasut Manik, Beadwork Materials, Stitching and Modernisation.

mapped.title: Peranakan Tiles Relief Painting Workshop with a Clay & Puffy Paint Twist,
mapped.description:
PERANAKAN TILES RELIEF ART WORKSHOP WITH A TWIST
"Bringing Heritage to Life with Clay & Puffy Paint"

WORKSHOP OVERVIEW
This hands-on session reimagines iconic Peranakan tiles through a tactile twist – participants will create their own relief-style art pieces on either canvas or ceramic tiles, using air-dry clay and puffy paint to replicate the intricate, raised patterns of traditional Peranakan craftsmanship. The workshop explores the cultural significance of Peranakan tile motifs while teaching practical techniques for building texture and layering vibrant, heritage-inspired colors.

WORKSHOP DETAILS
* Duration: 3 hours (10:00 AM – 1:00 PM / 2:00 PM – 5:00 PM; select slot upon booking)
* Venue: THEARTHAUS - #02-09 Katong Square
* Class Size: Max 5 participants per session (to ensure personalized guidance)

Target Audience
* All ages 10 and above (family and intergenerational sessions encouraged)
* Heritage enthusiasts, craft lovers, and anyone interested in Singapore’s cultural art forms

Materials Provided:
◦ Choice of 30cm x 40cm / 15cm x 15cm canvas or ceramic tile base
◦ Air-dry clay, puffy paint sets (heritage color palette), sculpting tools
◦ Reference guides of classic Peranakan tile motifs
◦ Protective aprons and work surfaces

WHAT YOU’LL LEARN
1. Brief introduction to Peranakan culture and tile design history
2. Techniques to shape clay into raised patterns
3. How to use puffy paint for added texture and color depth
4. Tips for sealing and preserving your finished piece

WORKSHOP FLOW
* Introduction : Overview of Peranakan tile culture and showcase of finished pieces.
* Design Planning : Participants select base and design, with guidance on motifs and colors.
* Clay Relief Creation : Sculpt clay motifs on base; allow drying or curing.
* Painting & Detailing: Apply acrylics and puffy paint for bold, textured finish.
* Finishing & Sharing: Seal or air-dry completed pieces.

mapped.title: Clay Relief Canvas Painting Workshop : Singapores Mosaic Playgrounds - A Heritage Journey
mapped.description:
CLAY RELIEF CANVAS PAINTING WORKSHOP
"Singapore’s Mosaic Playgrounds – A Heritage Journey"

WORKSHOP OVERVIEW
Singapore’s iconic mosaic playgrounds are more than just childhood landmarks—they’re tangible pieces of the nation’s urban heritage, designed in the 1970s–80s by pioneers like Khor Ean Ghee to reflect local culture and creativity. This workshop invites participants to transform these beloved spaces into vibrant clay relief canvas paintings, celebrating how playground design has shaped Singapore’s social and cultural fabric.

WORKSHOP DETAILS
* Duration: 3 hours (10:00 AM – 1:00 PM / 2:00 PM – 5:00 PM; select slot upon booking)
* Venue: THEARTHAUS - #02-09 Katong Square
* Class Size: Max 5 participants per session (to ensure personalized guidance)

Target Audience
* All ages 10 and above (family and intergenerational sessions encouraged)
* Singapore residents seeking to reconnect with local memories, heritage enthusiasts, and art lovers

Materials Provided:
◦ Choice of 30cm x 40cm canvas
◦ Air-dry clay, acrylic paint sets (heritage color palette), sculpting tools
◦ Reference guides of classic Peranakan tile motifs
◦ Protective aprons and work surfaces

WORKSHOP FLOW
* Singapore’s Playground Heritage Story: Learn about mosaic playgrounds' role in public housing and community building, explore iconic sites, and discover how mosaic art ties to Singapore’s multicultural heritage.
* Design & Sketching: Choose an existing playground subject or design a new local culture-inspired piece, with guidance on translating 2D patterns to 3D relief.
* Clay Relief Creation: Sculpt clay structures and details to replicate mosaic textures, incorporating local symbols; intergenerational sharing of memories encouraged.
* Painting & Detailing: Apply bold colors, use puffy paint to mimic mosaic tiles, and add accents inspired by national symbols or local elements.
* Heritage Sharing & Finishing: Share artworks and stories, receive a trail guide, and seal pieces to take home as an urban heritage tribute.

mapped.title: Jazzing Around at The Capitol Jazz Club
mapped.description:
⭐ Step beyond the velvet rope and immerse yourself in the timeless elegance of Jazzing Around at The Capitol Jazz Club, an intimate listening experience set within one of Singapore’s most iconic art deco landmarks

Highlights
🎷 Live jazz performances by a curated lineup of world-class artists
🏛️ A hidden listening room nestled inside the historic Capitol Theatre
🥃 A speakeasy-style atmosphere filled with soulful melodies and quiet glamour
🎶 An intimate setting where every note, pause, and rhythm takes center stage
✨ A journey through jazz’s rich heritage and vibrant future

July Artists Lineup

24 & 25 July - Charlene Su 
A Voice of Elegant Soul 
Singaporean vocalist and songwriter Charlene Su has cultivated a style defined by elegance, warmth, and emotional honesty over more than fifteen years on stage. A SOTA alumna and finalist on The Final One (Season 2), Charlene’s musical identity was shaped by a deep appreciation for the Great American Songbook and a passion for expressive interpretation. Today, she is recognized as one of Singapore’s most versatile musical voices, known as much for her viral original pop and R&B releases as her ability to breathe new life into timeless classics.

July Special Show

17 & 18 July - Simone Prattico Trio – Special Show
Rhythms Without Borders
Led by Italian drummer and composer Simone Prattico, this international trio features pianist Daniel Gassin and Brazilian percussionist Acelino de Paula. Blending contemporary jazz with Afro-Brazilian rhythms, their music is driven by groove, improvisation, and dynamic musical interaction. Drawing on extensive international performance experience across Europe, the Americas, Asia, and beyond, the trio brings together diverse musical influences in a performance shaped by rhythmic exploration, spontaneity, and cross-cultural exchange.

30 & 31 July & 1 Aug - Jacob Khalil – Special Show
The Contemporary Songsmith
New York–based singer, pianist, and songwriter Jacob Khalil blends jazz, contemporary pop, and soul through expressive vocals, thoughtful songwriting, and elegant piano performance. A regular presence on New York’s live music scene, including appearances at Birdland Jazz Club, Khalil is known for his fresh interpretations of both original songs and contemporary favourites. Combining jazz-influenced phrasing with a strong sense of storytelling, he delivers a performance that is intimate, melodic, and deeply engaging.

August Artists Lineup

1 August - Jacob Khalil
The Contemporary Songsmith
Jacob Khalil is a Palestinian-American singer, pianist, and songwriter based in New York City. Known for blending jazz and contemporary pop, he has appeared on Saturday Night Live alongside Sam Smith, performed at Birdland, and built a following through soulful originals and reinterpretations of modern and jazz standards.

7 & 8 August - Eugene Pao & Jeremy Monteiro Quartet | Special Show
40 Years of Friendship, Music and Jazz Adventure
In 1986, Jeremy Monteiro first heard Eugene Pao play at The Jazz Club in Hong Kong and was astonished to discover jazz guitar playing of such world-class depth, elegance and fire right here in Asia. That meeting began a friendship and musical collaboration that has now spanned 40 years. Since then, Eugene has worked with jazz greats including Randy Brecker, Eddie Gomez and Steve Gadd, while Jeremy has recorded and performed with artists such as Ernie Watts, Charlie Haden and Al Foster. On 7 & 8 August, Eugene and Jeremy reunite at the Capitol Jazz Club, joined by Ben Poh on bass and Hong Chanut Techatananan on drums. Expect an evening of swing, lyricism, virtuosity and spontaneous musical magic from four master musicians.

14 August - Alemay Fernandez
The Soulful Storyteller
Alemay Fernandez is a Singaporean jazz vocalist acclaimed for her commanding presence and versatility across jazz and soul. With a career spanning over two decades, she has collaborated with numerous international artists and performed with leading jazz orchestras. Known for her refined, expressive delivery, she brings depth, poise, and authority to every performance.

15 August - Vanessa Shavonne
The Velvet Voice of Jazz
Vanessa Shavonne brings a warm, soulful voice and a thoughtful approach to interpretation. Drawing from both jazz and soul traditions, she moves gracefully between standards and contemporary repertoire, shaping each song with subtle phrasing, musical sensitivity, and a natural connection with the audience.

20, 21 & 22 August - Natasha Oong | Special Show
The Enchanting Songstress
Thai jazz vocalist Natasha Oong is known for her clear tone, elegant phrasing, and understated musicality. A former lead vocalist of the jazz ensemble Mellow Motif, she has built a reputation across Asia for performances that balance precision with warmth, particularly in jazz and Brazilian repertoire.

28 & 29 August - The Carpio Brothers | Special Show
Jazz Beyond the Standard

Internationally acclaimed Hong Kong jazz musicians Bernard and Chris Carpio are joined by a rhythm section for an evening of straight-ahead jazz and groove-led jazz funk. With Bernard on saxophone and Chris on piano and keyboards, the group brings together swing, groove and expressive improvisation, reflecting a musical partnership developed over years of performing together.

Description
Jazzing Around at The Capitol Jazz Club is more than an evening out—it’s a refined escape into the soul of live jazz. Set within the art deco grandeur of The Capitol, this intimate listening room blends architectural elegance with the raw, expressive energy of a classic jazz speakeasy. As conversations soften and glasses clink, a hush falls and the music takes over, creating moments that feel both spontaneous and timeless. From storied classics to forward-looking sounds, each performance is thoughtfully curated to captivate discerning listeners and curious newcomers alike. Get your tickets now!

mapped.title: Translating Culture to the Page - A Fiction Masterclass with Prasanthi Ram
mapped.description:
Translating Culture to the Page
A Masterclass with Prasanthi Ram

How do you translate a culture that your reader may not share or fully understand? How do you stay true to lived experience without oversimplifying it for outsiders or conforming entirely to gatekeepers' expectations? How do you balance faithful representation with critique? What happens if you yourself are an outsider looking in?

In this masterclass, participants will read excerpts from local texts including Balli Kaur Jaswal’s Sugarbread and Fairoz Ahmad’s Neverness, that engage with notions of culture, identity and community and work through a series of generative exercises designed to give them more confidence in translating culture to the page.

Note: There is a selection process for this Masterclass. Participants are expected to submit a 250-500 word writing sample from a fictional scene that engages with culture in some way. The masterclass will be tailored to these submissions. Participants will also receive brief feedback on their submission. Please email your samples to programmes@singlitstation.com by 15 July 2026. Selected participants will be informed by 22 July 2026, and will receive a separate link to make payment. 

About Prasanthi Ram
Prasanthi Ram is a writer, lecturer and editor. Her debut Nine Yard Sarees (2023) won the Singapore Literature Prize for English Fiction in 2024, and was shortlisted for SLP's Best Debut and Singapore Book Awards' Best Literary Work. In 2025, she was selected as a writer-in-residence under the NAC-NLB Writers' Lab, where she began developing her sophomore novel. Her writing can be found in the Best New Singaporean Short Stories series (2022; 2025) and Making Kin: Ecofeminist Essays (2021) among others. Currently, she teaches academic writing at Nanyang Technological University, and is the co-founder and fiction editor of Mahogany Journal that spotlights South Asian writers born or based in Singapore. 

Event Details
📆 Date: 2 August 2026, Saturday
🕒 Time: 1pm - 4pm
📍 Location: Sing Lit Station, 22 Dickson Road #02-01, S209506

Ticketing Information
General Admission: $60
Student Concession: $35

mapped.title: Let's Explore: A Heritage Trail around Temasek Shophouse
mapped.description:
Let's Explore: A Heritage Trail Around Temasek Shophouse

Overview
A community-rooted heritage trail that uncovers Dhoby Ghaut-Bras Basah’s history through stories,local voices and shared connections.
What if the streets you walk every day held stories you'd never heard?
The "Let's Explore: A Heritage Trail Around Temasek Shophouse" is a 2.5-hour walking journey through the Orchard–Dhoby Ghaut–Bras Basah district,one of Singapore's richest heritage corridors,where the past and present meet in surprising,meaningful ways.
This isn't your typical guided tour. You'll be accompanied by a Community Host with a personal connection to the area,and a Facilitator who brings heart and authenticity to every stop. Expect honest storytelling,not a polished script and come ready to see the familiar with fresh eyes.

What you'll experience:
Discover how Temasek Shophouse honours its history as a conserved building while serving as a hub for social good today

  • Uncover lesser-known stories behind familiar landmarks in the district

  • Hear personal anecdotes that connect heritage themes to present-day community and social impact

  • Leave with a deeper appreciation of how Singapore's past continues to shape the communities we live in

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | da:ns focus - Connect Asia Now
Still Lives: Singapore
by Daniel Kok and Luke George, featuring RUDRA
Special iteration developed with Esplanade
mapped.description:
Part performance, part living sculpture, Still Lives transforms a fleeting moment into a shared space for reflection, revealing how personal memories, social relationships and cultural histories become entangled. Presented previously in cities such as Venice, Melbourne and Glasgow, Still Lives now arrives in Singapore.

As a special edition developed with Esplanade – Theatres on the Bay, Still Lives: Singapore features RUDRA, Singapore’s pioneering Vedic metal band, whose journey ran alongside the nation’s rapid economic and cultural transformation since the 1990s.

Members and instruments of RUDRA are bound and suspended within intricate rope structures in the Singtel Waterfront Theatre at Esplanade, creating a visually arresting landscape through which audiences can reflect on the stories that shape Singapore’s cultural identity—particularly those that have existed beyond the mainstream.

This programme is presented as part of da:ns focus – Connect Asia Now (CAN), a weekend that spotlights contemporary dance works created by or in collaboration with Asian artists, focusing on distinct voices and creative impulses from the region.

Advisory:
•    This work contains consensual tying & partial human suspension.
•    This work features rope suspension as an artistic and visual element. It is presented in a non-sexual context and does not contain nudity.
•   Please dress comfortably. Audience members are free to move around the space and may choose to sit (various seating options available) or stand during the performance.

mapped.title:
One Love, A Legacy 一缕柔情千秋夜
Chinese Theatre Circle 敦煌剧坊
In Collaboration with Esplanade – Theatres on the Bay 滨海艺术中心联合制作
mapped.description:
Interweaving dramatic storytelling, multimedia art and Cantonese opera excerpts, this theatrical production celebrates the extraordinary four-decade journey of Chinese Theatre Circle and its founders, pioneering Cantonese opera couple Joanna Wong and the late Leslie Wong.

After decades of preserving and promoting the art of Cantonese opera, Singapore traditional arts organisation Chinese Theatre Circle (CTC) celebrates their 45th anniversary in 2026. Spearheaded by pioneering Cantonese opera couple Joanna Wong and the late Leslie Wong, CTC has played a significant role keeping traditional arts alive in Singapore and shaping our multicultural identity while establishing standards for artistic achievement in Chinese opera. Joanna is Singapore’s best-known Cantonese opera artist, known globally for her skills and as a teacher. Her husband Leslie was a lyricist and opera composer who was learned in Chinese classics, and whose work has been performed by CTC around the world.  

In commemoration of this anniversary milestone, the company is thrilled to present an original English and Cantonese theatrical production One Love, A Legacy.

Following CTC’s spirit of cultural innovation, One Love, A Legacy deviates from previous Chinese opera productions and is created through artistic collaboration between Joanna Wong, homegrown Cantonese opera artists Christopher Choo and Jaron Leong, plus award-winning artist Ming Wong. Heartfelt, earnest and celebratory in nature, this contemporary production intertwines Joanna and Leslie’s love story with the evolution and achievements of CTC, featuring a cast comprising CTC’s artists, Joanna’s former students as well as guest artists from China’s Hong Dou Cantonese Opera Troupe. Skillfully blending theatre, Cantonese opera and multimedia, One Love, A Legacy reflects on the personal sacrifices, collective aspirations, and enduring passion of traditional opera artists in Singapore.

The show is presented in collaboration with the Esplanade – Theatres on the Bay, with support from the National Arts Council, the Sustain the Arts (stART) Fund, and the Cultural Matching Fund.

mapped.title: Singapore Ballet Masterpieces
mapped.description:
Discover Singapore Ballet Masterpieces — a landmark anniversary production honouring the company’s artistic identity, evolution, and enduring legacy. 

This year’s edition is especially meaningful as we celebrate Artistic Director Janek Schergen’s 20th year with Singapore Ballet, a milestone that reflects two decades of vision, leadership, and transformative artistry. 

In this commemorative programme, audiences will experience a powerful triple bill featuring three signature works that define the breadth and brilliance of our repertoire: 

Choo‑San Goh’s Schubert Symphony — a sculpted, neoclassical masterpiece of musicality and precision; 

Nils Christe’s Purcell Pieces — a lyrical, evocative work rich with elegance and emotional resonance; 

Tim Rushton’s Quiver — a dynamic, contemporary creation pulsing with rhythm, energy, and bold choreographic language. 

Singapore Ballet Masterpieces invites you to journey through the company’s artistic spirit — past, present, and future — in an evening that celebrates excellence, innovation, and the dancers who bring these works to life.

mapped.title: Maya: The Illusions of Mind
mapped.description:
What if the very things we chase for happiness are the source of our suffering?

MAYA – The Illusions of Mind is a thought-provoking solo Bharatanatyam production that explores the illusions we weave around ourselves—our desires, expectations, fears, and attachments. Through a powerful progression of dance, music, and storytelling, the work follows an inward journey through the rhythms of everyday life, the pursuit of fulfillment, and the unseen forces that shape our perceptions of ourselves and the world around us.

Rooted in the classical Bharatanatyam tradition yet deeply relevant to modern life, MAYA invites audiences to question what is real, what is imagined, and how the mind constructs the narratives by which we live. As the performance unfolds, familiar certainties begin to blur, revealing a landscape where longing, identity, and reality are not always what they seem.

Through expressive storytelling, intricate rhythm, and evocative music, MAYA offers a compelling exploration of the human condition—leaving audiences to ponder whether true freedom lies in obtaining what we seek, or in seeing beyond the illusion itself.

Presented by Saravanan Swedha, MAYA – The Illusions of Mind is conceived, choreographed, and artistically mentored by Guru Meera Balasubramanian, and brought to life with the accompaniment of Guru Meera Balasubramanian (Nattuvangam), Dr Lavanya Balachandran (Vocal), Doraiswamy Rajagopal (Mridangam), Pavan Sughosh (Violin), and Raghavendran Rajasekaran “Ragha” (Flute).

mapped.title: McLaren Racing Live - In Conversation with Oscar Piastri and Zak Brown [G]
mapped.description:
McLaren Racing arrives at Sands Theatre, Marina Bay Sands in Singapore, for a one-night-only event, with McLaren Mastercard Formula 1™ driver Oscar Piastri and CEO Zak Brown in conversation with a special guest host. 

It began with Bruce McLaren himself, a racing driver to his core who dared to build his own car, and in doing so, created one of the most enduring legacies in motorsport. 

For over 60 years, McLaren Racing has captivated the world – the only team to have ever completed The Triple Crown: the Monaco Grand Prix, the Indianapolis 500 and the 24 Hours of Le Mans. From Emerson Fittipaldi to James Hunt, Niki Lauda to Alain Prost, Ayrton Senna to Mika Häkkinen and Lewis Hamilton – generations of icons and champions have shaped McLaren Racing into the team they are today. A team with over 200 Grand Prix victories and 13 Constructors’ Championships to their name, and an unrivalled driver combination featuring Formula 1™ World Driver’s Champion Lando Norris and 9-time Grand Prix winner Oscar Piastri.   

Hear about a 2026 season which has already thrown everything at the team, as they fight for their third consecutive constructors title, and as Piastri chases the crown of 2026 Formula 1™ World Champion. 

With new engines and new aero, this is the most sweeping reset of the Formula in a decade, and McLaren are fighting to retain their primacy. Hear the inside line on the highs, the setbacks, and everything between. 

Join Oscar Piastri and Zak Brown for an exclusive unmissable insight into McLaren Racing, past, present and future.

mapped.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio Orchestra Concert - Singapore
mapped.description:
Experience the magnificent orchestral concert of music from ATLUS’s award-winning RPG Metaphor: ReFantazio,live for the first time outside of Japan! 

Following the success of the sold-out 2025 premiere in Japan,the official concert tour of the Metaphor: ReFantazio embarks on a world tour spanning 3 continents,6 countries and 8 cities across the globe. 

Step into the breathtaking world of Metaphor: ReFantazio,embark on an epic journey across the vast continents of a world inhabited by "humans." Gather trusted allies,enter the tournament for the throne,and find a way to lift the deadly curse to save your dear childhood friend,the Prince of Euchronia. 

This concert brought to life through the masterfully crafted soundtrack by Shoji Meguro,the legendary composer behind the "PERSONA" series. Experience every unforgettable track from the game,including "Ode to Heroes","King of Destruction",and "Prelude to a Heroic Tale",performed live by a magnificent 45-piece orchestra,a powerful choir,and exceptional vocalists. Immerse yourself completely with game cutscenes synced in real-time on a massive screen above the stage,and don't miss out on exclusive event-only merchandise available only at this concert!

mapped.title: Mitski
mapped.venueName: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.venue: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.description:
Mitski is "the most alluring and enigmatic musician in indie rock" (Rolling Stone) who “keeps shifting in all these really brilliant and wonderful ways” (NPR Music). Throughout her career, sheʼs released seven albums, four of which are certified gold. Her song, “My Love Mine All Mine,” was a global sensation, and is four-times platinum. Mitskiʼs varied collaborations with other artists include two co-writes on the most recent Florence and the Machine album, Everybody Scream, and with David Byrne and Son Lux for the Oscar nominated “This Is A Life.” She is currently writing the music and lyrics for the musical adaptation of The Queenʼs Gambit.

“A mesmerizing study in movement” (Variety), Mitskiʼs live show has also been described as “highly stylized, brilliantly realized, super cool” (Philadelphia Inquirer) and “unusual, enigmatic and utterly compelling” (The Guardian). Her first concert film, Mitski: The Land, was played in over 600 cinemas across 30 countries in October 2025, cementing her as “the rare indie star who has earned the big-screen treatment” (Uproxx). It was released in conjunction with The Land: The Live Album.

mapped.title: MICO Live in Singapore
mapped.description:
Global Alt-Pop Sensation MICO Set for First-Ever Singapore Show with the Running From a Feeling Tour!

Filipino-Canadian singer-songwriter MICO brings his highly anticipated Running From a Feeling Tour to Singapore on 19 November 2026 at Capitol Theatre. Presented by LAMC, the breakout alt-pop artist lands for his first-ever Singapore show fresh off sold-out international tours, bringing his emotionally charged mix of soaring hooks, pop-punk nostalgia, and deeply personal storytelling to one of his biggest regional shows yet.

From viral online beginnings to becoming one of alt-pop’s fastest-rising new artists, MICO (Miguel Veloso) has built a world that resonates deeply with a generation raised on playlists, late-night spirals, and big feelings. Drawing from influences like Fall Out Boy,5 Seconds of Summer, and The All-American Rejects, his music combines nostalgic alternative sounds with a fresh, genre-blurring edge.

Armed with breakout tracks like “HOMESICK,” “DREAMBOY,” “cut my hair,” plus music from his acclaimed debut album When the lights turn on, MICO’s live show promises soaring choruses, emotional storytelling, and the kind of cathartic energy that turns strangers into a room full of people singing the same feelings back at the stage.
Singapore, don’t miss your chance to experience one of alternative pop’s most exciting new names live this November!

mapped.title: MILLI JAA EHH! ASIA TOUR 2026 FIRST IN SINGAPORE
mapped.description:
From the Coachella main stage to Korea’s iconic hip-hop competition show Show Me The Money Season 12, MILLI has brought her fearless energy to the global stage, earning worldwide attention through her explosive performances and distinctive artistry.

Fresh from her standout "MSG" performance on Show Me The Money Season 12 and the viral TikTok moment of "SAAK" featuring Jungkook, Thai rap sensation MILLI is set to make her first-ever headline debut in Singapore.

For one night only, Foo Chow Building transforms into the ultimate JAA EHH! Party Experience, where fans can sing, dance, and celebrate together with MILLI as she performs her biggest hits, fan-favourite tracks, and viral anthems live on stage.

From MSG to SAAK, get ready to experience the energy, attitude, and unapologetic spirit that have made MILLI one of Asia’s most exciting young artists.

One stage. One night. One JAA EHH! Party. Singapore, are you ready?

mapped.title: Minsoo Sohn Piano Recital
mapped.description:
An extraordinary one-night journey through Bach’s 24 preludes and fugues

Internationally acclaimed pianist Minsoo Sohn takes the stage in Singapore this October for a rare, one-night-only performance of The Well-Tempered Clavier, Book I.

Comprising 24 preludes and fugues across all major and minor keys, Johann Sebastian Bach’s landmark work sits at the foundation of Western keyboard music. Yet its scale and complexity mean it is seldom performed complete in a single sitting. Heard in full, it becomes an immersive journey—each piece revealing its own colour, character, and intricate design.

Renowned for his precision and architectural clarity, Sohn brings exceptional authority to this repertoire. His recording of Bach’s Goldberg Variations was named among the year’s best by The New York Times, and his acclaimed Beethoven sonata cycle affirms his place among today’s leading pianists.

Beyond the stage, Sohn is also the mentor of Yunchan Lim, winner of the 2022 Van Cliburn International Piano Competition—underscoring his influence on a new generation of artists.

This is a rare chance to experience one of Bach’s masterpieces as a complete, living whole—an evening of focus, beauty, and quiet intensity that unfolds with remarkable clarity and grace. 

“Mr. Sohn’s playing was beautifully articulated, with clarity and a sense of structure that never faltered.” – The New York Times

“A performance of uncommon intelligence and poetic restraint.” – The Boston Globe

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | The Studios
MONSTRESS
by Claire Teo & ART:DIS (Arts and Disability)
Singapore
mapped.description:
What do you see? 
What do you choose to see? 

Mei Ling, a blind girl, moves into the apartment beside Melissa and Sam, a single mother and her son, who is autistic.  

As their lives brush against one another, they navigate the tensions of adapting to neighbourhood norms, schooling pressures, transport systems and public spaces, where social expectations are rigid, repeated, and rarely questioned. They start to find an unexpected friendship in each other. 

But what happens at home, stays at home, doesn’t it? 

Behind, between, and beyond the walls of their adjacent HDB flats, pressure builds. The walls seem to be pressing in. Corridors carry them forward, only to bring them back to where they first began. There is movement. But no exit. No reprieve.  

By the time something is named,  
And help comes running, 
It may already be too late. 
Not all monsters arrive fully formed. 
Some are made. 

Written by visually-impaired theatre maker Claire Teo, who draws from lived experience and observation to examine how harm is enabled and overlooked within the systems we trust, MONSTRESS is directed by Yvan Karlsson and performed by Claire Teo, Oon Shu An, Syarifuddin Sahari, and Sindhura Kalidas.

The play confronts what we choose not to see in everyday life. It exposes the behaviours we justify, the silences we keep, and the lines we draw between ourselves and others. Unsettlingly familiar, MONSTRESS becomes a mirror, one that dares us to look away. 

Moving seamlessly through text, movement, sound, and image, MONSTRESS asks: Why are certain voices dismissed over others? And can we become a community that is ready to offer support, before it is too late? 

Production credits
Playwright: Claire Teo  
Director: Yvan Karlsson 
Dramaturg: Charlene Rajendran  
Cast: Claire Teo, Oon Shu An, Syarifuddin Sahari, Sindhura Kalidas

mapped.title: In The Mood For Love in Concert《花样年华》电影音乐会 [G]
mapped.description:
Get ready to fall in love with In The Mood For Love in Concert, filmmaker Wong Kar Wai’s masterpiece brought to life in stunning full HD on the big screen, accompanied by a symphony orchestra performing the soundtrack live in perfect sync. Renowned for its poetic storytelling and lush visuals, In The Mood For Love is widely regarded as one of the most elegantly beautiful films ever made. With an emotionally rich score composed by Shigeru Umebayashi, this fully restored classic promises to be an immersive cinematic experience like no other. 

First released in 2000, In The Mood For Love tells the tender, bittersweet story of a man (Tony Leung Chiu-wai) and a woman (Maggie Cheung) who discover their spouses are having an affair together. As they grapple with their own loneliness, they slowly form a deep connection that is both restrained and profoundly moving. 

Premiering at the Cannes Film Festival, the film received universal acclaim, earning a nomination for the prestigious Palme d’Or and winning Tony Leung the Best Actor award — the first for a Hong Kong actor. Today, it remains a landmark of Asian cinema, frequently listed among the greatest films of all time.  

The Singapore season will be conducted by homegrown, renowned conductor Joshua Tan, who won the 2nd Prize at the 2008 Dimitri Mitropoulos International Competition. 

©2000 BLOCK 2 PICTURES INC.   ©2019 JET TONE CONTENTS INC. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED.

mapped.title: Moonlit City
mapped.description:
Before the Legend, There Was a Promise.

Every great city has its myths, but Singapore has a heartbeat.

Moonlit City is the sweeping English-language musical debut that pulls back the curtain on the private world of Lee Kuan Yew and Kwa Geok Choo. Beyond the iron will and the political podiums lies a breathtaking secret: a story of two brilliant minds who fell in love—and stayed in love—while forging a nation from the sea.
Guided by the hauntingly beautiful melodies of acclaimed composer Tan Kah Beng, this enhanced production reimagines the 2025 Mandarin sensation for a global stage. For the first time, experience the "Moonlit" letters, the whispered dreams, and the quiet power of the woman who was his equal in every way.
Witness the love story that built a home. Witness the soul of Singapore.

mapped.title: Moulin Rouge! The Musical [Rating: TBA]
mapped.description:
Pop the champagne, Moulin Rouge! The Musical, the winner of 10 Tony Awards®, including Best Musical is coming to Singapore!

Enter a world of splendour, of eye-popping excess, of glitz, grandeur and glory! A world where Bohemians and aristocrats rub elbows and revel in electrifying enchantment. Welcome to Moulin Rouge! The Musical!

Baz Luhrmann’s revolutionary film comes to life onstage, remixed in a new musical mash-up extravaganza. Directed by Tony Award® winner Alex Timbers, Moulin Rouge! The Musical is a theatrical celebration of Truth, Beauty, Freedom, and above all - Love.

With a book by Tony Award® winner John Logan; music supervision, orchestrations, and arrangements by Tony Award® winner Justin Levine; and choreography by Tony Award® winner Sonya Tayeh, Moulin Rouge! is more than a musical - it is a state of mind.

A global phenomenon that has dazzled more than 12 million people, Moulin Rouge! The Musical will have its Southeast Asia premiere at Sands Theatre, Marina Bay Sands featuring a world-class international cast!

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | The Studios
MULTIPLE BAD THINGS
by Back to Back Theatre (Australia)
mapped.description:
★★★★★
“Expansive and razor sharp…a chilling reminder of the ways power disguises itself for its own ends” – The Guardian

International Ibsen Award 2022
Golden Lion Award, Venice Biennale 2024

Set in a placeless warehouse, three employees approach a possibly pointless task. As the employees struggle to work together, they grapple with questions of inclusion and identity. 

They are forced to test the limits of their bodies, their cooperation and their capacity to care. Initial politeness descends into bad behaviour and sly victimisation. Reality distorts, and language flips from being playful and hilarious, to deadly serious and menacing. The conflicts are familiar, continuing to exist in the reality we live in today. Are you complicit?

The witching hour is here. Who will be the scapegoat?

Back to Back Theatre has received international acclaim for crafting work that defies expectations, and has consistently devised with an ensemble of actors who identify as having an intellectual disability or as neurodivergent. They are the first Australian company to win the Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement in Theatre in 2024, and were also a recipient of the International Ibsen Award in 2022.

In the making of this work, Back to Back Theatre invites new voices into the devising room. Zoë Barry’s score is assembled from collected field recordings of bad things. Anna Cordingley’s design demands the actors’ physical participation to reach its manifestation.

MULTIPLE BAD THINGS is theatre. It is not real. But in a world where self-righteously indignant voices so often drown out the most disenfranchised and vulnerable, this theatre sometimes feels real.

Welcome to the workplace at the end of the world. They’re only saying what you’ve already been thinking.

Devisors: Bron Batten, Breanna Deleo, Natasha Jynel, Simon Laherty, Sarah Mainwaring, Ben Oakes, Scott Price, Tamara Searle, Ingrid Voorendt 
Directors: Tamara Searle, Ingrid Voorendt

MULTIPLE BAD THINGS has been co-commissioned by Kunstenfestivaldesarts (Brussels), The Keir Foundation, The Anthony Costa Foundation, Geelong Arts Centre and Back to Back Theatre’s New Work Donor Circle, with development support from Festival d’Automne (Paris), Malthouse Theatre, Une Parkinson Foundation, Sidney Myer Fund and Give Where You Live.

mapped.title: From My Life
mapped.description:
From My Life is a story about Lynnette Seah’s journey — one shaped by discipline, love, and a lifelong devotion to music. Her musical path began at the age of four with piano lessons from her mother, a pianist who later introduced her to violin at six under the guidance of Goh Soon Tioe. Practice was a daily ritual; only after completing an hour each on piano and violin would she earn her dinner. These moments often continued in the kitchen, where her mother taught her to cook, turning food into both reward and an expression of care.

These early experiences laid the foundation for Lynnette’s artistry, instilling in her a deep sense of dedication and resilience. The works presented in this recital are drawn from some of her favourite violin and piano repertoire, each piece reflecting a chapter of her personal and musical life.

More than a retrospective, From My Life is also a statement of conviction — that age does not define one’s ability to perform, grow, or continue pursuing excellence. Through this concert, Lynnette Seah affirms that passion for music endures beyond time, and that artistry evolves with experience.

Joined by fellow musicians and collaborators, this recital is both a celebration of her journey and an invitation for audiences to share in the moments that have shaped her life in music.

mapped.title: 万紫千红之《潮韵蝶恋·梁祝情深》Nam Hwa Blaze: Butterfly Lovers
mapped.venueName: Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre Auditorium, Level 9 新加坡华族文化中心表演厅, 九楼
mapped.venue: Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre Auditorium, Level 9 新加坡华族文化中心表演厅, 九楼
mapped.description:
《万紫千红》 是南华潮剧社的年度制作系列。每一年都会围绕一个主题框架呈现精选的潮剧折子与艺术作品。该系列旨在传承文化遗产、推动艺术卓越，并为观众提供一个在当代语境中持续欣赏传统戏曲的平台。
  
万紫千红之《潮韵蝶恋·梁祝情深》演出以最核心的视觉符号，精准截取了《梁祝》中最具代表性的四大华章。

“情深”则呼应了从“相送”到“化蝶”的情感递进。

观众将完整经历从长亭的依依不舍《十八相送》；

到得知真相后的满腔欢喜《回十八》；

再到生离死别的悲恸《楼台会》；

以及梁山伯相思成疾、含恨而终的悲壮篇章《山伯临终》；

最后升华为绝美永恒的奇幻结局《化蝶》。

用潮剧独特的韵味，将祝英台的深情委婉，

梁山伯的淳厚儒雅表现得淋漓尽致。

mapped.venueName: Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre Auditorium, Level 9 新加坡华族文化中心表演厅, 九楼
mapped.venue: Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre Auditorium, Level 9 新加坡华族文化中心表演厅, 九楼
mapped.description:
Presented by Nam Hwa Opera, this one-day showcase brings to life the legendary tale of Sun Wukong’s Havoc in Heaven through dynamic Peking opera performance and interactive engagement.

Audiences will witness the Monkey King’s defiance against the Heavenly Court, his dazzling battles with celestial warriors, and his triumphant return to Flower-Fruit Mountain as the Great Sage Equal to Heaven.

Blending heritage, spectacle, and theatrical energy, the programme offers an engaging cultural experience for audiences of all ages while celebrating the enduring vitality of Peking opera in a contemporary setting.

mapped.title: NieR:Orchestra Concert 12026 [YoRHa]
mapped.venueName: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.venue: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.description:
A brand-new orchestra concert for the NieR series!

AWR Music Productions and SQUARE ENIX are proud to present NieR:Orchestra Concert 12026 [ YoRHa ]! From the team that shattered the paradigm with NieR:Orchestra Concert 12024 [ the end of data ], experience the music and vision anew. 

Conducted by Eric Roth, this concert will feature an immersive performance which brings together the power of the Resound Collective Orchestra and the ACJC Alumni Choir, the intensity of original NieR vocalists J’Nique Nicole and Emi Evans, stunning visuals on a giant screen, and a new narrated story, inviting you into an expanded musical world of the NieR series.

The concert features recorded recitations by Yui Ishikawa (No.2), Reina Tanaka (No.4), Mio Hanana (No.21), and Chihira Mochida (No.16), presenting episodes related to the “Pearl Harbor Descent Operation,” the prequel story to NieR:Automata. The story unfolds, richly integrated with the music. 

Director Yoko Taro and composer Keiichi Okabe will be in attendance at this concert, providing a rare opportunity to celebrate the music and artistry of NieR alongside its creators.
Join us for this awesome new experience of the expanding universe of NieR, live in concert.

VIP Meet & Greet
A limited number of VIP Meet & Greet tickets will be available. 
VIP tickets grant admission to a meet & greet event after the concert with conductor Eric Roth, director Yoko Taro, composer Keiichi Okabe, and vocalists Emi Evans and J'Nique Nicole, including an autograph and photo opportunity. 
VIP guests may check in at the Front Lobby to exchange for wristbands. Please note that guests must bring their own item for autograph signing, limited to one item per person.

mapped.title:
Dance Appreciation Series 
Introduction to The Nutcracker  
Presented by Singapore Ballet
mapped.description:
Back by popular demand, Introduction to The Nutcracker returns four years after its sold out run in 2022. An all-time audience favourite, this magical one-hour production is specially created for young audiences. Beautifully condensed from the beloved classical ballet, it offers the perfect introduction to the world of dance.

Follow Clara on an enchanting adventure filled with bravery, wonder and imagination, and meet iconic characters audiences adore—from the elegant Snow Queen to the dazzling Sugar Plum Fairy and her Cavalier.

Come dressed up in costumes and join a special post-performance meet-and-greet with the cast—snap photos and say hello to the dancers. Make it a day to remember and experience the magic of ballet up close through dance and storytelling.

mapped.title: The Nutcracker by State Ballet of Georgia [G]
mapped.description:
This festive season, experience The Nutcracker as it was meant to be seen – sumptuous, classical, filled with wonder, set to Tchaikovsky’s cherished and timeless score. Returning after a sold‑out season of Swan Lake, the State Ballet of Georgia brings ballet’s most beloved Christmas tradition to Sands Theatre this November, in a production adored by families and treasured across generations.

It is Christmas Eve and young Clara is gifted a beautifully crafted nutcracker doll. As night falls, her world transforms into something extraordinary – a Christmas tree that towers sky‑high, toy soldiers who spring to life, and a daring battle with the Mouse King. Snowflakes swirl across the stage, carrying Clara and her Nutcracker Prince into an enchanted winter kingdom.

Their journey continues to the Kingdom of Sweets, with the majestic Sugar Plum Fairy and a celebration of classical dance. Each scene unfolds to Tchaikovsky’s timeless score, music that has become inseparable from the season itself.

With a touring company of 60 and widely regarded as one of the world’s top ten classical ballet companies, the State Ballet of Georgia presents a large-scale production combining refined classical technique, traditional staging, and exceptional dancing. Perfect for audiences of all ages, critics have praised its “refined classical elegance” and described the production as “a visually enchanting interpretation of a timeless holiday ballet”.

mapped.title: Onam Nite 26
mapped.description:
Onam Nite is the signature annual musical and cultural extravaganza presented by the Singapore Malayalee Association in collaboration with Esplanade – Theatres on the Bay. Recognised as one of Singapore’s premier celebrations of Kerala’s rich cultural heritage, the event brings together world-class entertainment featuring celebrated artists from the Malayalam film and performing arts industry.

This year’s Onam Nite promises an unforgettable evening of music, dance and comedy with an exceptional lineup of renowned performers from South India. The musical programme features legendary playback singers Sithara Krishnakumar and Unni Menon, whose timeless melodies have captivated audiences for decades. The stage will also come alive with spectacular dance performances by acclaimed actresses and dancers Asha Sharath, Bhavana and Swasika. Round off the evening with a laughter-filled set by celebrated comedian Kottayam Nazeer and his team, sure to delight audiences of all ages.

Combining outstanding live performances with the festive spirit of Onam, Onam Nite continues to be a highly anticipated cultural event that strengthens community bonds while showcasing the finest talents from the Malayalam entertainment industry. It offers an evening of memorable entertainment, celebrating music, dance, humour and the vibrant traditions of Kerala in the heart of Singapore.

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | The Studios
Walking Tour: Memory Palace – The Worlds & Words of Bukit Larangan
By Ng Yi-Sheng
mapped.venueName: Fort Canning Hill (Meeting point: Entrance of National Museum of Singapore)
mapped.venue: Fort Canning Hill (Meeting point: Entrance of National Museum of Singapore)
mapped.description:
This walk—conducted by two-time Singapore Literature Prize winner Ng Yi-Sheng—will explore the lasting cultural legacy of precolonial Singapore. Visiting multiple sites in the vicinity of Fort Canning Hill, Yi-Sheng will reveal how the 14th-century kingdom lives on in the form of folk memories, rituals, monuments, theatre, and fictions.

Inspired by the research behind his mythic historical novel, Utama, which re-imagines the rise of Sang Nila Utama, Yi-Sheng ponders the contradictory stories around the lineage of the legendary founder of the island of Temasek, and the clash of empires in that era which shaped the Malay Archipelago we know today. Historical fact is layered with myth and re-invention, and spiced up with imagination.

Amongst other locations, the walk will take you to the National Archives, the Sang Nila Utama Garden, the Keramat Iskandar Shah, and the Archaeological Dig Exhibition. At each stop, Yi-Sheng will share personal memories and titbits of Singaporean cultural history, while also inviting you to share your own recollections.

This is a companion programme to Fragments of Tuah (24 – 26 Jul 2026) at Esplanade – Theatres on the Bay. The Studios companion programmes are an invitation to deepen engagement with the artistic works and conversations raised in the season.
