PEPPA PIG Fun Day Out! arrives at the Esplanade Theatre on 5 & 6 September 2026 for an oink-tastic live theatre experience packed with music,laughter and fun!

Join Peppa,George,Mummy Pig,Daddy Pig and friends as they head to the zoo and the beach for a day filled with songs,dancing,muddy puddles and plenty of surprises. Feed the penguins,build sandcastles and sing along in this interactive live show designed especially for young audiences.

Filled with laughter,snorts and unforgettable family fun,PEPPA PIG Fun Day Out! is the perfect first theatre experience for little ones.

mapped.title: THE JOURNEY CONTINUES - PETERPAN
mapped.description:
One Night.
One Stage.
One Iconic Band.

Relive the music that defined a generation — Peterpan returns to Singapore with The Journey Continues, a landmark live concert experience at Arena @ EXPO on Sunday, 4 October 2026.

Formed in 2000, Peterpan is one of Southeast Asia's most beloved and enduring bands, known for anthems that have moved millions across Indonesia, Malaysia, and Singapore. Their music — deeply emotive, powerfully melodic, and impossible to forget — has been the soundtrack to countless lives across the region for over two decades.

From the heartbreaking "Semua Tentang Kita" to the timeless "Ada Apa Denganmu,"
"Menghapus Jejakmu," "Ku Katakan Dengan Indah," and "Bintang di Surga," this night promises an extraordinary journey through over twenty years of music that has shaped a generation.

The Journey Continues is more than a concert — it is a reunion, a homecoming, and a celebration of everything this band means to their fans. One stage. Five thousand voices. Endless memories.

Don't miss your chance to be part of this unforgettable night.

mapped.title: Cosmopolitan Polo 2026
mapped.description:
The largest and most anticipated polo event of the year returns to the prestigious Singapore Polo Club this August. Once again, the public is invited to experience the speed, precision, and heart-racing intensity of polo up close.

Building on the success of its debut edition, the second Cosmopolitan Polo raises the bar with a bold fusion of high-octane sport, contemporary luxury, and vibrant cultural flair. Presented in a dynamic arena format, the action unfolds on a more intimate field, delivering fast-paced, adrenaline-charged chukkas that bring spectators right to the edge of play.

A highlight of the day will be the thrilling showdowns between competing teams in a series of exhilarating matches. Set against the striking Thomson–Novena skyline, the Club grounds transform into a stylish social destination. As dusk falls, the atmosphere transitions into a lively After-Party, where the vibrant energy of the crowd sets the stage for music, celebration, and dancing under the stars.

Beyond the matches, guests can immerse themselves in a curated lifestyle experience — from up-close encounters with polo ponies and player meet-and-greets to gourmet dining concepts and engaging equestrian showcases designed for both seasoned enthusiasts and first-time spectators.

Cosmopolitan Polo 2026 is a celebration of sport, sophistication, and community spirit.

Join us on 29 August 2026 — where elegance meets adrenaline.

mapped.title: Pub Choir - Just Think About It World Tour
mapped.description:
There are many facts in the known universe. For instance, did you know a giraffe is 30 times more likely to be struck by lightning? I guess because they are so tall?

Another fact is that Pub Choir is the world’s biggest, best, funniest, hottest music lesson. This fact has been verified by independent* sources (*we paid a guy on fiverr to say it).

An actually true fact is that Pub Choir is an opt-in experience. You don’t HAVE to buy a ticket and you are allowed to rot at home like you always do. But you PROBABLY should come and have a sing with a few thousand strangers. You would DEFINITELY feel better if you did. And hasn’t your sister-in-law been asking for years to go with you? It couldn’t be that bad could it?!? Everybody keeps bloody going on about it, maybe it’s worth a look. Anyway, just think about it.

What I'm saying is that Pub Choir will be touring across the world this year and Astrid is brewing some truly great arrangements, which I can claim with some authority, because I am her. We'll start in July in Australia, then head to NZ, Asia, Europe and the UK! The tour is called ‘Just Think About It’. And if you could, that'd be great.

You’ll learn one song in 3 part harmony at the show. That’s it, that’s the whole promise! Just think about it! Please come and have a sing! No worries if not!

This show is 18+ Please be aware that this event will be filmed & photographed. By purchasing a ticket and attending this event, you are giving your consent to be filmed & photographed during this event.

mapped.title: Resonance Across Borders - SJ60 Concert
mapped.venueName: Drama Centre Theatre, National Library 100 Victoria St, #03-01, Singapore 188064
mapped.venue: Drama Centre Theatre, National Library 100 Victoria St, #03-01, Singapore 188064
mapped.description:
Resonance Across Borders – SJ60 Concert is a special Singapore–Japan collaboration concert presented in celebration of the 60th anniversary of diplomatic relations between Singapore and Japan (SJ60).

Bringing together leading artistes from both countries, the concert features internationally acclaimed Shamisen virtuoso Hiromitsu Agatsuma, renowned pianist and music director Takuro Iga, Tokyo-born, Singapore-raised singer-songwriter Sachiyo, and Singaporean artistes Nathan Hartono, Samuel Wong and Govin Tan. Together, they create a unique musical experience where the musical cultures of Singapore and Japan meet, interact and resonate.

Rather than a programme of individual performances, the artistes come together to collaboratively reinterpret representative works from both countries through fresh arrangements and interpretations. Spanning a wide range of musical styles — from traditional music to contemporary popular music, film music and anime-inspired repertoire — the concert explores new artistic possibilities through cross-cultural collaboration.

Widely recognised for bringing the tradition of the Shamisen to international audiences through innovative collaborations that transcend genres and musical boundaries, Hiromitsu Agatsuma joins fellow artistes from Singapore and Japan in an extraordinary musical dialogue that celebrates both tradition and innovation.

Presented as part of SJ60, Resonance Across Borders offers audiences a rare opportunity to experience Singaporean and Japanese artistes creating music together on one stage. Through familiar melodies, new arrangements and artistic exchange, the concert celebrates sixty years of friendship between the two nations while looking towards the future of cultural collaboration.

Don't miss this unique SJ60 collaboration as acclaimed artistes from Singapore and Japan come together to create an unforgettable musical experience that transcends borders, cultures and genres.

mapped.title: The Radicalisation of Mrs. Mary Lim-Rodrigues
mapped.venueName: The Ngee Ann Kongsi Theatre @ Wild Rice, Funan
mapped.venue: The Ngee Ann Kongsi Theatre @ Wild Rice, Funan
mapped.description:
“Solidarity… is a radical posture.” – Paulo Freire

School principal Mrs. Mary Lim-Rodrigues is a Nice Lady™. A paragon of good intentions, she believes that every decision she makes is for the best. Except she presides over a sharp-edged system that makes life feel impossible for students like the brilliant but troubled Shan, whose tender young self thrives in the margins.

One horrible day, a devastating incident binds these two lives. Suddenly, Mary is confronted by the fine line between help and harm, and her moral faculties are put to the test. What happens when she can no longer rely on the way things have always been done? Can she be good at her job, and also a good person? And is there a right way to live, learn and die?

Powerful and thought-provoking in equal measure, Michelle Tan’s play is a story about rupture, irreversible loss, and what it takes to fix the things and people broken by all the good intentions in the world. Then, it is a call for revolution.

Written by Michelle Tan
Directed by Edith Podesta
Starring Janice Koh, Ryan Ang, Izzul Irfan, Koh Wan Ching & Suhaili Safari

Content Warning: References to self-harm and suicide

First commissioned by Singapore Repertory Theatre, The Radicalisation of Mrs. Mary Lim-Rodrigues was developed at Centre 42, followed by a work-in-progress reading as part of the 2024 Singapore International Festival of Arts.

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | A Date With Friends 
Hidup Ini: A Journey in Song with Friends
by Rahimah Rahim
mapped.description:
Hidup Ini: A Journey in Song with Friends is an intimate concert celebrating Rahimah Rahim’s 70th birthday year and six decades as one of Singapore's most enduring voices, woven into the fabric of the nation's cultural landscape and entertainment industry.

From her earliest days as a child performer to her place among the nation's most cherished entertainers, the concert traces the arc of a remarkable journey through the songs that shaped it—from timeless favourites such as Gadis dan Bunga to newer releases like Hidup Ini.

Joining Rahimah on stage are the longtime collaborators and dear friends who have shared that journey with her, making it a warm and reflective evening of music, memories and friendship.

mapped.title: Tunggu Sekejap: The P.Ramlee Suite
mapped.venueName: The Ngee Ann Kongsi Theatre @ Wild Rice, Funan
mapped.venue: The Ngee Ann Kongsi Theatre @ Wild Rice, Funan
mapped.description:
Back by popular demand,Tunggu Sekejap: The P. Ramlee Suite is Julian Wong’s deeply personal response to the life and legacy of a cultural giant whose music has enchanted generations.  

In this heartfelt and inspiring tribute to P. Ramlee,Wong – one of Singapore’s most outstanding music arrangers and directors – reshapes beloved classics like Azizah,Getaran Jiwa and Tunggu Sekejap into a three-movement orchestral work that feels both affectionate and alive. Within the intimate setting of The Ngee Ann Kongsi Theatre,the music is performed with warmth,joy and virtuosic grandeur by re: mix,a 30-member chamber orchestra under the assured direction of maestro Foo Say Ming. 

Throughout the show,Wong steps out from behind the music to reflect on how these songs entered his life and why they continue to speak to him. In a playful and poignant theatrical conceit blending memory,imagination and homage,he finds himself in conversation with P. Ramlee,brought to life by Fadhli Ramlee. What emerges is an affecting portrait of an artist who made music for his time that has since proved to be timeless. 

Reuniting the creative team led by Ivan Heng that so memorably staged Don’t Call Him Mr. Mari Kita,this extraordinary concert is an invitation to listen closely,and to hear P. Ramlee anew. 

Written & Arranged by Julian Wong 
Directed by Ivan Heng 
Performed by re: mix & Foo Say Ming

mapped.title: In Relation《不着一字》
mapped.description:
Harmony begins in the space between us, where difference becomes understanding. 

Inspired by the way of Li, "In Relation" reflects on respect not as formality, but as feeling given shape, a quiet sensitivity made visible through gesture, memory, and attention. 

It moves through the ways we live with one another: to inherit the past without being bound by it, to hold difference without erasing it, and to recognize how the smallest gestures of regard can open into something larger than ourselves. Community emerges here as a living weave, where many colourful threads move as one. 

Rooted in Singapore’s living cultural landscape, this performance brings music, voice, and artistic expression into shared breath. Traditions enter like living currents, each carrying its own pulse, distinct yet moving in relation. Each response becomes part of a larger unfolding. Silence gathers into sound, and sound opens into connection as ancient melodies encounter contemporary voices. Heritage is no longer held behind glass, but allowed to breathe, shift, and converse in the hands and hearts of today, where difference becomes opening rather than boundary, and harmony arises not from similarity, but from attention, patience, and mutual regard. 

Between one voice and another, between what has been and what is still becoming, we rediscover the delicate thread that connects us, where to be in relation is to make space, listen deeply, and journey together in our own voices toward a shared horizon. 

Presented by Siong Leng  
Supported by National Arts Council  

*A post-show talk will be held after the performance.

The “Reminiscence III” concert is the third installment of the Association’s pop concert series, dedicated to showcasing the versatility and expressive depth of the traditional Guzheng. Centered on the theme of nostalgia, the concert brings together a collection of timeless classics that evoke cherished memories and emotional connections.

Featuring a thoughtfully curated program, the performance spans Chinese pop songs, iconic theme songs from Chinese films and television dramas, as well as beloved game and animation soundtrack. Designed to appeal to a broad audience, the repertoire highlights familiar melodies that have remained meaningful across generations.

The concert is presented in three chapters—Chinese Pop Songs, Chinese Movie and Drama Series Theme Songs, and Game & Animation Soundtracks—each focusing on music that is at least a decade old, celebrating enduring works that continue to resonate with listeners today.

The Legend of the Monkey King Returns begins with Sun Wukong trapped under the Five Finger Mountain, having lost his powers. The story follows his journey from a stubborn and rebellious monkey to becoming the legendary Great Sage. mapped.title: REVELATION
mapped.description:
《启示录》是一部以圣经中最后一部书卷为灵感而创作的宏伟中文神曲，由三百人组成的大型合唱团与管弦乐团联合呈献。全曲分为上下两部，从地上教会延展至天上异象，呈现审判、盼望与最终胜利的末世启示之旅。

第一部以写给七个教会的书信展开，呼唤信徒持守真理、警醒、坚定，用生动的音乐刻画教会的呼声与追求。

第二部则透过使徒约翰所见的天上异象，展现神最终的完全得胜、新天新地的降临，以及神永恒同在的荣耀；《启示录》中的七个祝福，成为信徒永恒的盼望与喜乐。

为了营造天地呼应的戏剧张力，作品采用了 “三维音响设计”：

独唱加上羽管键琴伴奏与大提琴独奏象征书卷作者“约翰”的宣告；小组合唱加上管风琴伴奏与弦乐四重奏象征天上长老与上帝使者的颂赞；大合唱加上管弦乐团与钢琴伴奏象征众教会与天使天军的回应。

mapped.title: Swaranjali: Remembering Pandit Rajan Mishra
mapped.description:
Presenting a showcase of the unique repertoire of the Banaras Gharana, in tribute to late Pandit Rajan Mishra in the year of his 75th birth anniversary. The concert will be presented by the legendary vocal maestro’s sons, Pandit Ritesh Mishra and Pandit Rajnish Mishra. This will be an evening of khayal performance that will bring alive the unique style and compositions of a rich and storied gharana of Hindustani classical music.

Recipients of the Padma Bhushan award, Pandit Rajan Mishra along with his brother Pandit Sajan Mishra achieved global renown for introducing the world to the long form khayal compositions of the Banaras Gharana. Hailing from an ancient city and a 300 year old musical lineage that already boasted some of the greatest names in Hindustani classical music, the brothers carved a unique space for themselves as the most recognized vocal duo in Hindustani classical music, with a style that was marked by a deep, resonant vocal quality, intricate improvisations, and a seamless blend of voices that made it often difficult for listeners to distinguish where one brother's singing ended and the other's began.

Pandit Ritesh Mishra and Pandit Rajnish Mishra are globally established artists in their own right, and have been conferred the Sangeet Samriddhi Samman and the Sangeet Natak Akademi awards. They are carrying forward not just the legacy and repertoire of the Banaras Gharana but also the unique approach to jugalbandi (duet) performance of their father and uncle.

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | The Studios
Blind Runner
by Amir Reza Koohestani & Mehr Theatre Group (Iran)
mapped.description:
“mesmerising…stunningly performed…deeply moving” – The Guardian​

Obie Awards 2025 –  Special Citation
Biennale Teatro Venice 2024

Once a week, a husband comes to visit his wife, a political prisoner. 

Spied on by cameras and under constant surveillance, their conversations become increasingly fraught and distant, leading to incomprehension and a crippling inability to put into words the harshness of daily life.  

At his wife's insistence, the husband agrees to guide a young blind woman through a race in Paris. They have to find a common rhythm; over the course of their training sessions, both get to know each other. 

Once the competition ends, another race emerges. He now has to help her get to England by crossing the Channel Tunnel, covering 38 kilometers in just a few hours so as to avoid being hit by the first train in the morning. Between the confines of the prison and the sense of freedom afforded by the race, emerges a poetic vision of the struggle, of mutual aid, and of the freedom towards which we rush headlong into. 

A 2023 co-production with several key international festivals—Kunstenfestivaldearts (Brussels), Festival d’Automne à Paris, Noorderzon Festival of Performing Arts & Society (Netherlands), Berliner Festspiele, and Under the Radar Festival (New York)—Blind Runner deftly and gracefully interweaves the destinies of three individuals, each grappling with oppression in their own ways. 

Iran’s Amir Reza Koohestani and Mehr Theatre Group return to Singapore for the first time since 2014, where their presentation of Amid The Clouds at Singapore International Festival of Arts (SIFA) was praised by The Business Times for being an “austere and restrained piece … steeped in poignancy and meaning.”

True to his stripped-down approach for the stage, Iranian director Amir Reza Koohestani now deploys video to multiply the possibilities of the narrative, encouraging the spectator's gaze to roam freely from the performers' bodies to their filmed faces. In so doing, he has once again created an undoubtedly striking and moving show. 

Key Production Credits 
Text and direction: Amir Reza Koohestani 
Dramaturgy: Samaneh Ahmadian 
Director’s assistant: Dariush Faezi 
Lights and scenography: Éric Soyer 
Video: Yasi Moradi, Benjamin Kreig 
Music: Philip Hohenwarter, Matthias Peyker 
Costume design: Negar Nobakht Foghani 
Performers: Ainaz Azarhoush, Mohmammad Reza Hosseinzadeh 
English translation and surtitle adaptation: Massoumeh Lahidji 
Surtitles operator: Negar Nobakht Foghani 
Production, administration, and promotion: Pierre Reis 

Blind Runner is supported by The Institut Français and The Ministry of Culture – DRAC Île-de-France.

mapped.title: BHSO 40th Anniversary Gala Concert: Saint-Saëns’ Organ Symphony
mapped.description:
BHSO 40th Anniversary Gala Concert: Saint-Saëns’ Organ Symphony

Celebrate a landmark year with the Braddell Heights Symphony Orchestra as it marks its 40th Anniversary with a gala concert honouring four decades of music-making as one of Singapore’s oldest symphony orchestras.

The programme reaffirms the orchestra’s commitment to Singapore music, opening with Seem Jia Ang’s “The Journey Ahead” and Tan Chan Boon’s Symphonic Poem “In Memory of Franz Schubert,” Op. 29. The evening also features Chen Yi’s Percussion Concerto, performed by Cheong Kah Yiong.

The celebration culminates in Camille Saint-Saëns’ Symphony No. 3 in C Minor, “Organ Symphony,” featuring organ soloist Loraine Muthiah. Marking the 140th anniversary of this monumental masterpiece, its majestic sonorities and triumphant finale provide a fitting conclusion to this special occasion.

mapped.title: Sam Lee「One Day」 World Tour - Singapore 李圣杰「One Day 直到那一天」演唱会 — 新加坡站
mapped.description:
华语乐坛实力派情歌王李圣杰，将带着「One Day 直到那一天」演唱会正式来到新加坡，以一整晚的经典情歌，陪伴歌迷重返那些关于青春、爱情与回忆的重要时刻。 

出道 27 年，李圣杰凭藉极具辨识度的深情嗓音，以及细腻动人的演唱风格，唱红无数脍炙人口的经典作品。《痴心绝对》、《手放开》、《你那么爱她》、《最近》、《眼底星空》等歌曲，多年来陪伴无数人走过爱情中的遗憾、等待、放手与成长，也成为许多人青春岁月中不可取代的情感记忆。 

他的歌声之所以动人，不只是因为旋律朗朗上口，更因为每一首歌都像是一段真实人生的缩影。那些关于爱情里的错过、遗憾与成全，都透过李圣杰温柔而深刻的演唱，被完整唱进人心。 

「One Day 直到那一天」作为李圣杰全新巡演篇章，无论在舞台视觉、音乐编排或情感铺陈上都全面升级，并以更完整的演出内容，串联他 27 年来的音乐历程与经典作品。 

演唱会将透过不同时期的代表歌曲，带领歌迷重返那些熟悉的青春记忆。从年少时对爱情的执着与勇敢，到历经岁月后对遗憾、成长与放下的理解，每一段人生情感，都将在这次演出中被重新唱响。

本次巡演也融合了「FACE」系列作品的音乐概念，呈现李圣杰一路走来在音乐与人生中的转变与蜕变，并以更成熟细腻的歌声，重新诠释那些陪伴无数人成长的经典情歌。

「One Day 直到那一天」不只是一场演唱会，更像是一场与回忆久别重逢的旅程。当熟悉旋律再次响起，每一首歌都将唤醒不同人生阶段的故事与情绪。无论是曾经在《痴心绝对》中学会执着，还是在《手放开》里理解放手，每一位走进演唱会现场的歌迷，都能在歌声中找到属于自己的情感共鸣。 

2026 年，李圣杰将与新加坡歌迷相聚，用最熟悉的情歌旋律，陪大家一起唱回青春，也唱进人生新的篇章。

mapped.title: Sampan Rides
mapped.venueName: The Shoppes at Marina Bay Sands, Basement 2, The Canal
mapped.venue: The Shoppes at Marina Bay Sands, Basement 2, The Canal
mapped.description:
Enjoy a leisurely sail through Singapore’s colourful past to its glittering present in the heart of Marina Bay Sands. Hop onto a beautifully crafted Sampan boat, inspired by the small wooden skiffs that once filled the Singapore River at the height of its trading activity. Simply relax as you’re gently paddled down the Canal by our very own Sampan rowers.​

Make a wish as you circle the Rain Oculus, an eco-friendly art installation comprising a 22m-wide acrylic bowl that collects the rainwater that fills the Canal. Or feel the rush with a close-up view of the hourly water cascade — an impressive 22,000 litres of water are released per minute from a skylight two storeys overhead.​ ​

Explore our tours where you can learn more about our design and sustainability features, or join in for some musical fun on the water.​

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | Raga
Sanjya Chhaya
by WeCanDoIt
mapped.description:
Retirement, as often told, is when life slows down. Sanjya and Chhaya clearly did not get the memo. 

In their early sixties, this energetic couple run a Happiness Centre, a free community initiative where people drop in with problems, worries, dilemmas and occasionally the expectation of miracles. 

Having built successful careers and raised a family, Sanjya and Chhaya have embraced a retirement filled—not with routine and predictability—but with purpose, laughter and meaningful human connections. Their days are busier than ever, their opinions sharper and their zest for life completely intact. 

But when family expectations, generational differences and competing ideas of responsibility begin to collide, the couple find themselves navigating a question that many families quietly wrestle with: once duties to others are fulfilled, is it finally possible to live life on one's own terms? 

Warm, witty and deeply relatable, Sanjya Chhaya is a celebration of friendship, companionship, individuality and the enduring desire to remain useful and joyfully alive. Packed with humour, heart and moments of poignant reflection, the play shines a spotlight on a generation that refuses to be defined by age. 

Presented in Hindi with English surtitles, Sanjya Chhaya is a charming reminder that retirement is not the end of the story, it may just be the beginning of life's most liberating chapter. 

Because life doesn't go to bed when you retire... and neither should you. 

There will be a post-show dialogue after each performance.

mapped.title:
An Esplanade Production
PLAYtime! 2026
The Amazing Sarong
mapped.description:
Isn’t a sarong just a big piece of cloth? Not for Nora and Adi! When their mother hands them her sarong, the siblings discover that this seemingly ordinary fabric can transform into anything their imagination allows.

From sails to secret hideouts, costumes to capes, the sarong takes them on a playful adventure filled with surprises, laughter and new encounters. What amazing things will Nora and Adi discover next?

Join us for a delightful performance that brings this beloved story to life on stage. Together, children and their grown-ups can celebrate the joy of play, the strength of family bonds, and the extraordinary possibilities hidden in everyday things.

Adapted from the book The Amazing Sarong by Quek Hong Shin, published by Epigram Books.

PIP says
This is a performance that encourages children to see the extraordinary in the everyday. As Nora and Adi discover new adventures with the sarong, children learn to notice and care for the feelings of others, building empathy and positive connections.

mapped.title: The Script “Man In The Arena” Tour
mapped.description:
Irish pop-rock band, The Script are set to return to Singapore on 31 March 2027 at the Singapore Indoor Stadium as part of their “Man In The Arena” Tour.

With over 11 billion streams, 12 million album sales worldwide, six UK No.1 albums and a catalogue of era-defining hits, The Script continues to captivate audiences across generations with their heartfelt lyrics, anthemic sound and electrifying live performances. Fans can look forward to an unforgettable night featuring beloved hits such as “Breakeven,” “The Man Who Can’t Be Moved,” “Hall of Fame” and “Superheroes,” alongside new music from the band’s latest chapter.

Presented by Midas Promotions

mapped.title: Sentosa 4D  AdventureLand
mapped.description:
Sentosa 4D AdventureLand, Sentosa's high-tech interactive entertainment zone is home to a diverse and exciting collection of highly sensorial experiences at Imbiah Lookout.

‘Chaos In Wonderland’, an immersive 4D show experience where you become part of the show through a synchronisation of this wide spectrum of special visual, sound, motion and 4D effects. Join Ed and Edda at the amusement park which turns into a crazy night of chaos with now living dinosaurs, pirates and other fairy tale characters!

The 4D motion-simulated, ‘Extreme Log Ride’ is where you will be riding through ravines and down valleys in a virtual “roller coaster”. In addition, ‘Haunted Mine Ride 4D’ promises riders a fast and exhilarating adventure as you plunge headfirst into an old dilapidated gold mine that just happens to be haunted.

‘Desperados’, the first 4D interactive shoot-out game in Asia offers a superior gaming experience. Ride on a motion-based saddles to take out bandits with your hand-held laser guns.

mapped.title: Shaggy Live in Singapore
mapped.venueName: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.venue: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.description:
After an explosive night with Sean Paul, we knew we had to keep the energy going. Singapore showed up in a big way — and we're ready to turn it up even higher.

Grammy Award-winning dancehall reggae icon Shaggy is returning to Singapore for an unforgettable night of reggae, dancehall and pure Caribbean energy. Presented by Offshore Entertainment, Shaggy "Mr Boombastic" Live in Singapore is happening on 2 October 2026 at The Star Theatre. It's gonna be a dancehall reggae concert you don't want to miss.

Born Orville Richard Burrell in Kingston, Jamaica and forged through the reggae dancehall underground of Brooklyn, New York, Shaggy is a U.S. Marine veteran turned global hitmaker. The only diamond-certified dancehall artist in music history, he has sold over 40 million albums and surpassed 5 billion streams on Spotify, ranking alongside Bob Marley and Sean Paul as one of the top three most-streamed Jamaican reggae dancehall artists of all time.

The catalogue of Shaggy songs defines the 90s reggae dancehall era, whose influence has only grown with time: from the diamond-selling "It Wasn't Me"  (1.25 billion Spotify streams) to the timeless "Angel", the Grammy-winning "Mr Boombastic", and the viral "Banana" phenomenon that reached 2 billion streams and 5 billion TikTok video views. His career also spans a Grammy-winning collaboration with Sting for the album "44/876", executive producing Spice's Grammy-nominated debut, and the Grammy-nominated "Com Fly Wid Mi" — Frank Sinatra's songbook reimagined in full Jamaican dancehall reggae style.

Don't miss Singapore's biggest reggae and dancehall concert of the year. Ticket sales are launching soon. Follow our socials and stay updated on Singapore concerts.

mapped.title: The Unstoppable Tour Shreya Ghoshal 2026
mapped.venueName: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.venue: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.description:
“The Unstoppable Tour” — Shreya Ghoshal Live 2026 

Celebrated as one of the most iconic and versatile voices in contemporary Indian music, Shreya Ghoshal brings her much-anticipated “The Unstoppable Tour” to audiences around the world—an electrifying live concert experience featuring her most beloved songs and a refreshed musical presentation. 

With a remarkable career spanning over two decades, Shreya Ghoshal has recorded extensively across multiple languages including Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Malayalam, Bengali, Kannada, and Marathi. She first rose to national prominence after winning Sa Re Ga Ma Pa, followed by her breakthrough debut in Devdas, which established her as a leading voice in Indian cinema. Over the years, she has earned multiple National Film Awards and Filmfare Awards, gaining recognition for her vocal precision, emotional expression, and extraordinary range. 

Following the phenomenal success of her previous international tour, “The Unstoppable Tour” marks a vibrant new chapter in her live performances. Audiences can look forward to a spectacular showcase of her greatest hits, songs that have defined moments in film and music, brought to life on stage with renewed energy and a fresh creative direction. 

Infused with a new flavour, the tour introduces updated arrangements, dynamic staging, and a contemporary edge while staying true to the timeless essence of her music. The result is a powerful, immersive concert experience that celebrates Shreya Ghoshal’s enduring artistry and her deep connection with fans across generations.

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | Mosaic Music Series
Silica Gel ASIA TOUR Syn.THE.Size: Ballad of You
mapped.description:
There's no pain, just joy when Silica Gel’s around: following their explosive live show at Baybeats 2025, this beloved South Korean indie rock band returns to Singapore for a full-fledged performance! 

Formed in 2013, the band—Kim Hanjoo, Kim Chunchu, Kim Geonjay, and Choi Woonghee—has crafted a distinctive sonic identity that blends psychedelia, art-rock, indie pop and experimental textures. Their name, inspired by the silica gel in a gum container, reflects the band’s spontaneous and adventurous approach to music-making—cheekily referred to by the band as “team-play rather than teamwork”.  

The band spent their early years in South Korea’s independent scene, fully dedicated to their music and quietly making waves with their self-titled debut album in 2016—the same year they won three rookie awards at the Korean Music Awards. After an extended hiatus where the band completed their mandatory military service, Silica Gel returned in full force with two singles, Desert Eagle in 2021 and No Pain in 2022. While retaining the complexity of their earlier compositions, these singles featured soaring vocals, irresistibly catchy guitar hooks and earworm melodies that fueled the band’s upward trajectory. In 2023, they continued their streak with Tik Tak Tok, winning Best Modern Rock Song for the third consecutive year at the Korean Music Awards, as well as achieving the prestigious Musician of the Year award.  

Deeply dedicated to their craft, the band continues to evolve their musical identity and push the boundaries of their sonic palette. On the heels of the release of their third full-length album in Aug 2026, Ballad of You, and nearly a year since their show at Esplanade in Oct 2025, the band will make their way back to Esplanade to greet fans in Singapore once again.

mapped.title: Slava's Snowshow [G]
mapped.description:
Created by Slava Polunin, cited by The London Times as “The Greatest Clown in the World,” Slava’s Snowshow is the winner of the Laurence Olivier Award for Best Entertainment (1998) and a Tony Award nominee for Special Theatrical Event (2009). This show transcends age, leaving audiences of all ages spellbound.

Experience the joy and playful spirit of childhood through this wordless, immensely visual captivating performance, where clowns guide audiences through foolish, mischievous, and magical moments.

The stage comes alive with swirling snow, sudden gusts of wind, and a giant spider web of white threads that stretches into the audience, drawing everyone into the playful chaos. Loved by both adults and children, the show invites guests into a shared world of laughter, wonder, and irresistible silliness, making it a truly unforgettable family experience.

mapped.title: 杨蒙恩脱口秀专场《独立喜剧分子》Young Moon Stand-up Special: Stand-Up Independents
mapped.description:
《脱口秀大会》《喜剧之王单口季》卡司杨蒙恩，带着他的个人专场首次登陆新加坡。

关于“独立喜剧分子”这个名字，杨蒙恩说：从出国到现在，以及2018到2019年在42公司工作的时候，是他作为脱口秀演员最快乐的时光。不属于任何脱口秀俱乐部、公司、厂牌或节目组，哪里有演出就去哪里，演或看，和遇见的朋友聊上几句。这也是脱口秀最初吸引他的地方：一个站在台上的普通人，没有什么包装和顾忌。

他选了一张证件照做官方海报。一张没精修过的一寸照，有缺点，有痦子，左右脸不对齐。这就是现在最真实的他：一个独立的、喜剧fēn子。

他希望这场专场和这张证件照一样：它是自由的，好笑的，但可以不那么正确的。

期待与新加坡的观众朋友们现场相见。

mapped.title: Singapore Percussion Association Presents: Friends in Rhythm
mapped.description:
Friends in Rhythm is an exciting and vibrant concert experience that brings together the unique colours of flute, soprano, percussion, and piano in a celebration of musical friendship and collaboration. Audiences can look forward to an engaging journey through a wide variety of genres, featuring beloved operatic arias, traditional folk songs, and dynamic contemporary works from around the world.

From intimate and expressive moments to bold and electrifying performances, the programme highlights the versatility and artistry of each instrument and performer. The expressive beauty of the human voice blends seamlessly with soaring flute melodies, rich piano textures, and the captivating energy of percussion, creating a concert atmosphere that is both fresh and unforgettable.

Join us for an evening of rhythm, storytelling, and musical connection — a celebration of music that crosses cultures, styles, and traditions. experience that brings together the unique colours of flute, soprano, percussion and piano in a celebration of musical friendship and collaboration. Audiences can look forward to an engaging journey through a wide variety of genres, featuring beloved operatic arias, traditional folk songs, and dynamic contemporary works from around the world.

mapped.title: Classics at Capitol - 2001: A Space Odyssey
mapped.description:
2001: A Space Odyssey 

1968. Dir: Stanley Kubrick,149 mins. (with 10 min. intermission),Colour,4K DCP (PG)

Starring Keir Dullea, Gary Lockwood, Douglas Rain

One of the great masterpieces of cinema, Stanley Kubrick’s science fiction epic 2001: A Space Odyssey, is a work of extraordinary cinematic vision. It begins in prehistoric times, then leaps forward into the future of 2001,telling of humanity’s capacity for self-destruction. 2001 remains prescient, especially in our era of Artificial Intelligence.

Kubrick wanted to take Science Fiction, a genre often seen as ridiculous, and create an interplanetary spectacle as realistic and believable as possible.  Sci-fi author Arthur C. Clarke developed the story, about a mysterious monolith that appears on the moon, which has previously visited earth during a critical stage of human evolution. We then follow an expedition to Jupiter through deep space on a covert mission, derailed by the increasingly odd behaviour of HAL, the distinctively voiced AI that controls their spaceship.

In addition to the extraordinary special effects photography, production design and the choreography of the ‘Dawn of Man’ sequence (memorably parodied in Barbie!),Kubrick made iconic use of classical music on the soundtrack, including Richard Strauss’s Also sprach Zarathustra as a recurring theme, and Johann Strauss’s The Blue Danube to accompany the elegant movement of space flight. 

On release in 1968 (eight months before the actual moon-landings),2001 overwhelmed and fascinated audiences. They were often perplexed by the final section of the film, which Kubrick designed to be a purely visual and sonic journey open to interpretation. It is still utterly mind-blowing 58 years later.

mapped.title: Spellbound - Suhani Shah Live in Singapore
mapped.description:
Spellbound will be the world famous mentalist, Suhani Shah's second public show in Singapore in a year's time.

During this show, you can expect a captivating experience filled with mind-bending illusions and interactive audience engagement.The show features a blend of mentalism, comedy, and storytelling, showcasing Suhani's unique ability to read minds and engage with the audience.

Suhani combines her psychological insights with magic, creating a performance that is both entertaining and thought-provoking. Suhani's show is known for its improvisational skills, where she interacts with the audience in a warm and bubbly manner, making it a memorable experience.

The performance is divided into various illusions, gradually building complexity and leaving audiences in awe.

Overall, Suhani Shah's show promises to be a spellbinding experience that will leave you spellbound!

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents
Feed Your Imagination (F.Y.I) 2026
A Sea of Stories Ensemble-Leading Workshop for Educators
By Open Score Project
mapped.description:
How do you turn a simple tune into something rich and dynamic? 

Join Open Score Project for an engaging, hands-on workshop designed specially for educators. Drawing inspiration from the musical performance A Sea of Stories: Music and Myths from Southeast Asia, this session introduces practical ways to foster collaboration and creativity in the music classroom. 

Participants will explore the ensemble’s open-score approach, learning to create, improvise and respond to one another through sound. Guided exercises will develop skills in listening, timing and ensemble awareness, offering strategies to help students build confidence and musical sensitivity. Bring along your own instrument and experience how improvisation can nurture teamwork, imagination and joy in collective music-making. 

Recommended for: Music Educators

Learning Objectives
Through this workshop, participants will learn to:
- Apply open-score techniques within an ensemble setting to develop a simple melody into richer musical textures.
- Facilitate collaborative music-making by guiding students to create, improvise and respond to one another through sound.
- Use improvisation as a teaching tool to encourage creativity, musical sensitivity and confidence in the classroom.
- Design classroom activities that nurture teamwork, imagination and joyful collective music-making using flexible instrumentation.

What to Expect
Participants will be actively involved in hands-on music-making throughout the session and are encouraged to bring their own instrument to play during the workshop.

mapped.title: An Evening with Tchaikovsky — Noah Zhou & the Last Minute Artist Orchestra
mapped.description:
Following the overwhelming response to the Last Minute Artist Orchestra's debut last year, British-Chinese pianist Noah Zhou returns to Singapore, joining conductor Amanda Kye Tan and the orchestra once more for a full evening devoted entirely to Tchaikovsky at Victoria Concert Hall. Hailed by BBC Radio for his "masterful, deeply emotional and dramatic" playing, Zhou is the first British-born Laureate of the prestigious Rachmaninoff International Piano Competition, and returned again this April for a solo transcription of Rachmaninoff's Piano Concerto No. 2 that The Straits Times called "heroic.

This thrilling programme features Tchaikovsky's iconic Piano Concerto No. 1 and his beloved Symphony No. 5, a concert of triumph, lyricism, and orchestral grandeur.

FEATURING
Last Minute Artist Orchestra
Amanda Kye Tan, conductor
Noah Zhou, piano*

PROGRAMME
TCHAIKOVSKY –
1812 Overture, Op. 49
Piano Concerto No. 1 in B-flat minor, Op. 23*
Symphony No. 5 in E minor, Op. 64

(Programme subject to change at the artists' discretion)

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | The Studios
Workshop: Theatremaking with Back to Back
Theatre (Australia)
mapped.venueName: Esplanade Bay Room (Meeting Point: Concert Hall entrance, 15 mins before start time)
mapped.venue: Esplanade Bay Room (Meeting Point: Concert Hall entrance, 15 mins before start time)
mapped.description:
In this workshop, come experience Back to Back Theatre’s devising processes through collaborations and conversations, and experiment with different ways of co-creating theatre.

Led by Associate Director of Back to Back Theatre Ingrid Voorendt (along with performers Scott Price, Sarah Mainwaring, Simon Laherty and Bron Batten), this workshop will equip you with practical tools for an arts-based classroom and/or for creative collaboration, as well as introduce you to principles for developing an inclusive theatrical practice.

This is a companion programme to MULTIPLE BAD THINGS (21 – 23 Aug 2026) at Esplanade – Theatres on the Bay. The Studios companion programmes are an invitation to deepen engagement with the artistic works and conversations raised in the season.

Over the last 30 years, Back to Back Theatre has crafted a body of work that questions the assumptions of what is possible in theatre, but also the assumptions we hold about ourselves and others. They are the first Australian company to win the Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement in Theatre in 2024, and were also a recipient of the International Ibsen Award in 2022.

This workshop is open to theatre practitioners and anyone with an interest in theatremaking to find out more about the artist's approach to theatremaking. You don’t need experience, just enthusiasm.

mapped.title: TIOT SPECIAL FAN EVENT AND CONCERT IN SINGAPORE
mapped.description:
TIOT LIVE IN SINGAPORE
Rising K-pop boy group TIOT brings their electrifying energy to Singapore with a powerful live show that captures the spirit of youth, ambition, and the defining moments of today. 

Under the theme “Time Is Our Turn,” the concert showcases TIOT’s journey as emerging global artists, blending high-impact performances with emotional storytelling. From explosive tracks like “Rock Thang” and “Flex Line” to more introspective songs such as “The Long Season,” the setlist highlights the group’s versatility and artistic growth. 

More than just a concert, this show is designed as an immersive experience—where music, performance, and fan interaction come together to create unforgettable memories. Through dynamic choreography, live vocals, and special stages that reveal each member’s individuality, TIOT invites fans to share in a moment that belongs to all of us. 

“TIOT LIVE IN SINGAPORE” is not only a celebration of music, but a declaration of youth, dreams, and the courage to claim your time.

mapped.title: Attack on Titan - Symphony from Paradis
mapped.description:
A colossal symphonic event begins — Attack on Titan - Symphony from Paradis, the first officially licensed Attack on Titan orchestral concert.

Brand-new symphonic arrangements curated by original composers Hiroyuki SAWANO and KOHTA YAMAMOTO, performed by more than 100 musicians and choir live on stage with HD giant-screen video projection from the TV series.

A symphony dedicated to the hearts — Shinzō wo Sasageyo!

A new chapter in the musical legacy of Attack on Titan begins with Attack on Titan – Symphony from Paradis, the first-ever official orchestral concert. Featuring fully symphonic arrangements curated and supervised by original composers Hiroyuki SAWANO and KOHTA YAMAMOTO, brought to life by more than 100 musicians and choir live on stage, this production offers a new experience shaped by the delicate nuance and emotional power of symphonic music. These newly created arrangements are crafted to unleash the full dramatic impact of the series’ iconic themes: tension, urgency, strength and melancholy, with HD video in perfect sync with music projected on a giant screen.

The music of Attack on Titan was always meant to feel bigger than the screen. Attack on Titan – Beyond the Walls - World Tour, a format centered on rock-orchestra performance and the faithful reproduction of the original tracks, received an extraordinary response and remains active, continuing to tour worldwide. It has been hailed as a critical success, having sold over 240,000 tickets to date, with multiple sold-out performances at landmark venues including Carnegie Hall, the Dolby Theatre, the Sydney Opera House, Wembley Arena, AsiaWorld-Arena, and Taipei Arena, among many others. It is now time to open a new artistic chapter with Attack on Titan - Symphony from Paradis, a powerful orchestral work newly arranged for large symphony orchestra and choir, and engineered to land with maximum emotional force. The arrangements are supervised by the score’s original composers and created by Nobuko Toda and Kaoru Wada to reveal new details, new textures, and new intensity, with the scale, color, and dynamic range that only a symphonic concert can offer.

Don’t miss Attack on Titan - Symphony from Paradis and experience the music of the TV series at full symphonic scale in an immersive live performance full of depth, nuance, and emotion!

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | Mosaic Music Series
Tortoise (USA)
mapped.description:
“Touch reminds you how much fun Tortoise are, how seriously they take these musical ideas and how utterly unseriously they take themselves.” – UNCUT magazine 

Tortoise is “body music for head people”, according to Pitchfork. Emerging out of Chicago, USA in 1990, the band was one of the few essential bands that charted a path for post-rock at a time when the genre was still defining itself. Unlike their international contemporaries—Mogwai favoured ominous atmospheres and weighty distortions while Sigur Ros drew inspiration from Icelandic landscapes to create sweeping ambient sounds—Tortoise was recognised for their eclectic influences and profound technicality. Landmark records like Millions Now Living Will Never Die and TNT bore elements of jazz and krautrock peppered with electronica flourishes, anchored by gnarly grooves and off-kilter time signatures. Body music for head people, indeed.  

In 2025, the band released their eighth album, Touch. Eschewing some of the minimalism that feature in older records, Touch unapologetically embraces grand gestures with aerodynamically re-engineered krautrock, hand-cranked techno rave-ups and spaghetti Western fanfares. The record is big, bold, fun, and a reintroduction to a band that truly does not need introduction.  

The records only tell half the story. Tortoise’s legacy goes beyond its recorded output, per Rolling Stone who deems Tortoise “a live marvel,” while Pitchfork further says the band’s performances reveal that “at heart, they’re a supremely fun band, wide open to all sorts of sonic possibilities.” Onstage, the cerebral architecture of their music gives way to something visceral and communal. Body music is, after all, meant to be felt. 

Now, Tortoise returns to Esplanade after fourteen years, having made two memorable appearances at Mosaic Music Festival in 2005 and 2011. This is an invitation to the believers, the curious, the jazz cats and the post-rock heads—feel the full weight of Tortoise’s legacy in this epic live show.

mapped.title: Trapped Escape Room Experience
mapped.dateText: Valid for 60 days from date of purchase
mapped.description:
Trapped is the #1 escape room in Singapore. (New Season 6) Featuring over 70++ latest escape room props with new animatronics. 7 exciting themes, 28 hidden chambers & 2 NPC actors in our new Cursed Video Tape & The Great Escape games.

You’re kidnapped and trapped in a room with your peers, and there seems to be almost no way out. Become the master at solving mysteries, discovering hidden clues, tunnels, and secret passage-ways and succeed to escape our next generation reality escape rooms! mapped.title: TRUE Live Tour PLAY! vol.3 -乱舞- in Singapore
mapped.description:
TRUE’s latest tour concept is inspired by traditional Japanese aesthetics and culture, creating the most intense, emotional, and “insane” stage performance of her career so far. 

The overall theme of the show is based on “Taisho Roman” and Japanese-style elements. “Taisho Roman” represents the unique cultural atmosphere of Japan’s Taisho era, blending traditional Japanese beauty with early modern Western influences to create a distinctive artistic world. Every aspect of the production — including stage design, costumes, visuals, and lighting — reflects this elegant yet dramatic aesthetic. 

Musically, the show also strongly incorporates Japanese influences, with many songs featuring special Japanese-style arrangements. Traditional Japanese-inspired melodies, rhythms, and sounds are fused with powerful rock energy and delicate Japanese aesthetics, creating a one-of-a-kind atmosphere that is both beautiful and intensely dynamic.

mapped.title: Two Worlds in One
mapped.description:
"Two Worlds In One", initiated by Cy Leo, reimagines the harmonica's possibilities through original harmonica and vocal works-exploring how the instrument can speak fluently across jazz, pop, and world music. The project invites audiences to encounter the harmonica in a fresh context, bringing its voice into a wider and more diverse musical world.

The project unfolds through an album production and an Asia tour, led by Cy Leo as composer/lyricist/editor and artistic director. and during the tour it will collaborate with protessional harmonica ensembles from different regions. At the same time, representatives from harmonica communities around the world will be invited to join selected pertormances. Through this shared stage, the program connects harmonica enthusiasts across Asia and helps the harmonica tradition continue to grow from city to city.

Each composition will be released in two versions: a harmonica instrumental version and a vocal version. The vocal versions feature pop and jazz singers from Hong Kong, South Korea, Japan, and the United States, offering a welcoming entry point for listeners who love vocal music-guiding them into the niche world of harmonica-centered sound.

The programme is supported by the Hong Kong Economic and Trade Office in Singapore.

mapped.title: ​​​​​​​2026 TWS TOUR ‘24/7:FOR:YOU’ IN SINGAPORE
mapped.description:
TWS will be bringing their new tour, 2026 TWS TOUR ‘24/7:FOR:YOU’, to Singapore on 10 October 2026 at Arena @ EXPO.

TWS (pronounced ‘Two-Us’), consisting of six members—SHINYU, DOHOON, YOUNGJAE, HANJIN, JIHOON, and KYUNGMIN—is a boy group introduced by PLEDIS Entertainment. The name is an abbreviation of ‘TWENTY FOUR SEVEN WITH US,’ bringing forward the expression ’24/7’ to symbolize their unwavering commitment to being with their listeners around the clock. As K-pop’s champions of youth, TWS guides listeners into their youthful evolution with their unique musical style that channels refreshing, unstoppable energy—dubbed ‘Boyhood Pop.’ Upon their breakout debut last year, the lead single “plot twist,” from their debut album Sparking Blue, became a long-running hit, charting on the Billboard Global 200 for 8 weeks and topping the annual chart of Korea’s leading streaming platform, Melon. Their three consecutive releases—including their debut album, 2nd mini album SUMMER BEAT!, and 1st single Last Bell all surpassed half a million sales, cementing their growing popularity in the music scene. The sextet’s remarkable rise was further recognized when the Billboard named them ‘K-Pop’s Rookie of the Month’ in January, and they were featured in the NME 100 as one of the emerging artists for 2025.

The 2026 TWS TOUR ‘24/7:FOR:YOU’ will begin at KSPO DOME in Seoul, followed by stops in Fukuoka, Hyogo, Kanagawa, Macau, Bangkok, Singapore, Kaohsiung. For the Singapore stop, TWS will bring their refreshing music, youthful energy, and heartfelt connection with fans to the stage, creating a special night to remember.

2026 TWS TOUR ‘24/7:FOR:YOU’ IN SINGAPORE
Date: 10 October 2026, Saturday
Venue: Arena @ EXPO

mapped.title: UNDERTALE: The Determination Symphony World Tour
mapped.description:
UNDERTALE: The Determination Symphony is an unforgettable concert experience featuring the most memorable themes from the hit indie game, newly arranged and performed live by a 25-piece orchestra. With gameplay footage displayed on a screen above the stage, audiences will be fully immersed in the world of UNDERTALE, experiencing its story and music like never before.  

Toby Fox, creator and composer of UNDERTALE, shared his excitement: "UNDERTALE is a game with a lot of music, it's good when skilled professionals play the music and people listen to it." 

Concert-goers can look forward to being filled with determination as the performance guides them through the Underground. From the Ruins and Snowdin to Waterfall, Hotland, and finally New Home, the concert brings iconic characters, battles, and emotional moments vividly to life through music.

mapped.title: The Romantic Piano Virtuoso - Solo Piano Recital by Alyssa Kok
mapped.description:
Step into the world of piano music of the 19th century with The Romantic Piano Virtuoso, a solo piano recital featuring young Singaporean classical pianist Alyssa Kok. 

The evening recital showcases four major piano works by composers Fryderyk Chopin (1810-1849) and Franz Liszt (1811-1886), two of the most important personalities of the Romantic Era. Spanning different genres, lengths and periods of composition, the piano pieces display sheer technical brilliance, authentic Romantic spirit and ethereal philosophical depth. In addition, the programme juxtaposes a duality of physical prowess and imaginative interpretation. Throughout the recital, one will catch a glimpse of the Polish composer Chopin's youthful, energetic compositional style of his Warsaw period, followed by his fully mature and metaphysical last style in his final years. One will also hear a potpourri of ideas of the Hungarian virtuoso pianist and composer Franz Liszt, expanding a theme by legendary violinist Niccolò Paganini into an etude, and experimenting on various dance themes from his journey to Spain. The multicultural background of the repertoire extends from Poland and Hungary to Italy, France, Austria, and Spain. 

Each piece will be introduced and performed by pianist Alyssa Kok, a young graduate from Europe with extensive training for over a decade with renowned pianists and professors of the region. A prizewinner of numerous international piano competitions, Alyssa Kok has staged piano recitals on several continents including Europe, North America, the Middle East, Asia and Oceania. With the mission of sharing music with audiences worldwide to make the world a better place, Alyssa Kok hopes to bring inspiration and light through her pianistic performance to all who will be present.

Programme: 


1. Fryderyk Chopin: Variations on "Là ci darem la mano" Op. 2  
2. Fryderyk Chopin: Sonata No. 3 in B Minor Op. 58 (I. Allegro maestoso, II. Scherzo: Molto vivace, III. Largo, IV. Finale: Presto non tanto) 

Break (15 minutes) 


3. Franz Liszt: Paganini Etude No. 3 "La Campanella" 
4. Franz Liszt: Rhapsodie Espagnole S. 254

mapped.title: Voices for Hospices 2026: Jimmy Ye and Friends For Good Charity Concert 慈怀之声暖我心 — 叶良俊与好友《友善》慈善演出
mapped.venueName: Drama Centre Theatre, National Library 100 Victoria St, #03-01, Singapore 188064
mapped.venue: Drama Centre Theatre, National Library 100 Victoria St, #03-01, Singapore 188064
mapped.description:
Voices for Hospices 2026: Jimmy Ye and Friends For Good Charity Concert, organised by the Singapore Hospice Council (SHC), brings communities together through music to raise funds and awareness for hospice and palliative care. Over the years, the concert has featured local artistes, healthcare professionals, and volunteers, using music as a powerful platform to foster conversations about serious illness, caregiving, grief, and living well until the end of life. Held in conjunction with World Hospice and Palliative Care Day, the concert aims to inspire individuals, healthcare professionals, and communities to support those living with life-limiting illnesses and to champion access to quality palliative care in Singapore. This year’s concert promises an intimate and meaningful evening of music, reflection, and giving. Audiences will journey down memory lane with Jimmy Ye’s beloved hits, performed alongside fellow artistes Joanna Dong, Chriz Tong, and Marcus Lee, with special performance by SHC Vice Chairman, Dr Chong Poh Heng, in a heartfelt celebration of friendship, compassion, and community.

Funds raised will be matched by the Tote Board Enhanced Fundraising Programme.

mapped.title: Wagner’s Siegfried
mapped.description:
The Orchestra of the Music Makers continues with the Singaporean premiere of the third instalment of Wagner's Ring Cycle. Conductor Lü Shao-Chia, director Tan Shou Chen, and a cast of internationally acclaimed singers, several of whom return to reprise their roles, bring Wagner's epic vision to life. 

Richard Wagner's Ring Cycle stands as one of Western opera's most ambitious undertakings—four works that reimagine Norse and Germanic mythology. Composed over three decades, the cycle demands extraordinary forces and stamina from both performers and audiences, but rewards them with some of the most transformative music ever written. 

In Siegfried, a young hero reforges his father's broken sword and slays a dragon to claim a ring of power. He discovers a warrior woman trapped in an enchanted sleep, surrounded by flames. Only someone truly fearless can reach her—and Siegfried has never learned what fear means. When he wakes her with a kiss, she surrenders her immortality for love. 

A semi-staged production sung in German with English surtitles. 

FEATURING 
Orchestra of the Music Makers  
Lü Shao-Chia, conductor 
Tan Shou Chen, director

Vincent Wolfsteiner (Siegfried) 
Dan Karlström (Mime) 
Michael Volle (Wanderer) 
Anna Harvey (Erda) 
Joachim Goltz (Alberich) 
Lukasz Konieczny (Fafner) 
Victoria Li (Woodbird) 
Stéphanie Müther (Brunnhilde) 

PROGRAMME 
RICHARD WAGNER — Siegfried

mapped.title: Dreams and Demons by Guangbin Wang
mapped.description:
Dreams and Demons features internationally acclaimed pianist Guangbin Wang, winner of the 2025 Nashville International Chopin Piano Competition Championship Title and Best Étude Performance Award. Recognized as one of the most outstanding young pianists of his generation, Wang presents a programme that journeys through worlds of light and darkness, elegance and passion.

The recital opens with Bach’s Italian Concerto, followed by Chopin’s Twelve Études, Op. 25, works of extraordinary virtuosity and poetic depth. Mozart’s Piano Sonata in C minor, K.457 reveals intense drama and emotional power, while Ravel’s Gaspard de la Nuit concludes the evening with its haunting imagery and dazzling technical demands.

Blind since early childhood, Wang possesses an exceptional memory and learns vast repertoire entirely by ear. His performances have captivated audiences in China and the United States, earning praise for their technical brilliance, imagination, and emotional sincerity.
A highlight of the evening is Wang’s remarkable gift for improvisation. Audience members will be invited to suggest a short musical phrase, which he will spontaneously transform into a complete piano work.

More than a recital, Dreams and Demons is a celebration of artistic creativity, resilience, and the transformative power of music, inviting audiences on an unforgettable musical journey.

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents | National Theatre Live
Mrs. Warren's Profession (Screening) – PG
mapped.description:
By Bernard Shaw 
Directed by Dominic Cooke    

★★★★★ ‘Dominic Cooke’s streamlined adaptation’ – Broadway World 
★★★★ ‘Imelda Staunton and real-life daughter Bessie Carter are both forces to be reckoned with’ – Radio Times 
★★★★ ‘Staunton and Bessie Carter make a wonderful double at’ – Financial Times 

Five-time Olivier Award winner Imelda Staunton (The Crown) joins forces with her real-life daughter Bessie Carter (Bridgerton) for the very first time, playing mother and daughter in Bernard Shaw’s incendiary moral classic. 

Vivie Warren is a woman ahead of her time. Her mother, however, is a product of that old patriarchal order. Exploiting it has earned Mrs. Warren a fortune—but at what cost? 

Filmed live from the West End, this new production reunites Staunton with director Dominic Cooke (Follies, Good), exploring the clash between morality and independence, traditions and progress.

mapped.title: Wuthering Waves Concert Tour: To the New World
mapped.description: Wuthering Waves, the open-world action RPG developed by Kuro Games, presents its first official live concert tour. The show features iconic music and songs from the game, performed live alongside guest vocalists. Combined with immersive stage production, lighting, and dynamic visuals, the concert delivers a spectacular audiovisual experience for players around the world. The tour will also bring fans together across the globe to experience the unique musical world of Wuthering Waves.

mapped.title: Monkey Goes West
mapped.venueName: The Ngee Ann Kongsi Theatre @ Wild Rice, Funan
mapped.venue: The Ngee Ann Kongsi Theatre @ Wild Rice, Funan
mapped.description:
Monkey Goes West is back! An affectionate and cheeky retelling of the beloved Chinese fantasy classic,this award-winning holiday musical is fabulous fun for the whole family.

When orphan Ah Tang runs away from home after quarrelling with his adoptive family,he finds himself transported to a new,strange land. There,he befriends a trio of fellow travellers: Wukong,the cheeky Monkey King; Pigsy,a brave but always-hungry warrior; and Sandy,a stubborn but loyal river ogre.

Together,the fearless foursome embark on the adventure of a lifetime,one fraught with trials and dangers aplenty. Can they contain the rowdy mischief of the fire-breathing Red Boy? Will they be able to defeat the monstrous shape-shifting King Bull? How in the world will they get their hands on the magic fan of the man-eating Princess Iron Fan? Most importantly,will Ah Tang ever find a place that he can call home,truly?

Join Wild Rice this holiday season for an epic thrill ride,written with cheeky,heartwarming wit by Alfian Sa’at,and featuring a brilliant score composed by Elaine Chan. Presented by The Ngee Ann Kongsi,this groundbreaking production will be spectacularly reimagined for the Wild Rice stage by visionary director Ivan Heng. Packed with catchy songs,hilarious gags,wushu and magic,Monkey Goes West will keep you and your family laughing well into the New Year. Come and be entertained… and enlightened!

Written by Alfian Sa’at
Directed by Ivan Heng
Music by Elaine Chan
Starring Ryan Ang,Sugie Phua,Tia Guttensohn,Siti Khalijah Zainal,Chua Enlai,Ebi
Shankara,Seah Janice and the First Stage Kids

mapped.title: 《东郭先生与狼》The Wolf and The Scholar
mapped.venueName: Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre Auditorium, Level 9 新加坡华族文化中心表演厅, 九楼
mapped.venue: Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre Auditorium, Level 9 新加坡华族文化中心表演厅, 九楼
mapped.description:
The Wolf and the Scholar is a family-friendly Mandarin stage production adapted from the classic Chinese fable Mr Dongguo and the Wolf. Set in a forest, the story follows the compassionate Mr Dongguo, who saves a wolf being chased by hunters, only to face danger when the wolf turns against him after escaping.

Through humour, music, lively characters, and audience interaction, the production explores themes of kindness, wisdom, gratitude, fairness, and moral responsibility. It also encourages audiences to reflect on environmental protection and the importance of peaceful coexistence between humans, animals, and nature.

Blending traditional Chinese storytelling with moral and cultural education, the production inspires children and families to practise compassion with discernment, respect life, and care for the natural world.

mapped.title: “World of Warcraft®: 20 Years of Music”
mapped.description:
Discover the legacy or relive your most cherished memories of World of Warcraft ®, one of the world’s most iconic video game franchises through an unforgettable symphonic experience. 

In partnership with Blizzard Entertainment®, Helvepic proudly presents the official concert series “World of Warcraft®: 20 Years of Music”, celebrating two decades of adventure through the prism of its legendary soundtrack. 

Performed by a full symphonic orchestra and choir, this two-hour musical journey chronicles the history of Azeroth, from the original World of Warcraft® to World of Warcraft®: Dragonflight. The program brings to life the franchise’s most epic and beloved compositions, from the gentle ambiance of Elwynn Forest to the sweeping Nordic majesty of the Howling Fjord, from Anduin Wrynn rallying the Alliance to Varian’s heroic sacrifice. 

Released in 2004, World of Warcraft® remains one of the most successful and enduring massively multiplayer online role-playing games in history. Over the past twenty years, its ever-expanding world and rich storytelling have captivated many players worldwide. With each expansion, Azeroth has grown in scale and depth, fostering a passionate global community and setting the standard for the MMORPG genre. And its music plays an integral role in the franchise’s lasting legacy. 

Vast and emotionally powerful, the soundtrack of World of Warcraft® ranges from intimate ambient themes to thunderous battle anthems. Blending classical orchestration, folk influences and choral grandeur, the music gives every land, every faction and every story arc its own identity, bringing the world of Azeroth to life. 

Created by Helvepic, a Swiss production company specializing in epic symphonic concerts, and developed in close collaboration with Blizzard Entertainment®, “World of Warcraft®: 20 Years of Music” premiered in Lausanne, Switzerland, in September 2024 to great acclaim. As the concert embarks on its World Tour, audiences across international venues are invited to experience this extraordinary celebration of twenty years of adventure, heroism and unforgettable music. 

Join us as we honor two decades of World of Warcraft® through epic, nostalgic and breathtaking performances that will resonate with fans and newcomers alike.

mapped.title:
Esplanade Presents
Feed Your Imagination (F.Y.I) 2026
The World of Our Own Workshop for Educators
By The Psychology Atelier
mapped.description:
This workshop equips educators with essential skills to create psychologically safe spaces where students can navigate loneliness and difficult emotions. Through it, they will know how to facilitate meaningful conversations with students about their challenging feelings, learning to recognise what they are communicating through their behaviour and how to respond in ways that foster trust and emotional security. Importantly, the workshop aims to strengthen educator’s own emotional foundation for being able to hold space for students' struggles with confidence and presence.

Educators will leave with concrete tools to help students feel genuinely understood and supported, while developing sustainable practices that protect their ability to remain emotionally available in the classroom.

Recommended for: Upper Primary – Lower Secondary Educators

Learning Objectives
Through this workshop, participants will learn to:
- Deepen the understanding of loneliness in children, and their search for companionship, comfort and validation, through a relational lens.
- Explore how safety, trust and connection are built (or disrupted) in everyday interactions and when children may be struggling to express what they need.
- Reflect on how their own relational patterns and emotional responses can affect children.
- Practise compassionate language that helps children feel seen and less alone and learn practical strategies to respond to children’s emotional needs in their role of educators.

mapped.title: Wild Rice Gift Voucher
mapped.description:
Share the magic of theatre with your loved ones! 

Looking for the perfect present for a special someone? Buy a Wild Rice gift voucher today, and send your loved ones on a theatrical adventure they’ll never forget!

Your friends and family will be spoilt for choice, as there is truly something for everyone at Wild Rice. Throughout the year, we showcase the very best of Singapore theatre – from family-friendly, crowd-pleasing musicals to bold, original and thought-provoking new work. 

Our gift vouchers – available in denominations of $20, $50 and $100 – can be redeemed against all shows presented by Wild Rice at The Ngee Ann Kongsi Theatre in Funan.

mapped.title: 婺动狮城 Wu Opera in Singapore
mapped.description:
Nam Hwa Opera proudly presents the return of one of China’s leading opera institutions — the Zhejiang Wu Opera Art Research Institute — to Singapore, bringing four shows of spectacular performances.

Wu Opera is widely regarded as one of the most visually thrilling forms of traditional Chinese opera. Renowned for its explosive martial arts sequences, innovative stage design, and fast-paced theatrical rhythm, Wu Opera combines high-energy physical performance with gripping storytelling. Its dramatic visual impact and dynamic narrative style captivate seasoned opera enthusiasts while also drawing in younger audiences and first-time theatre-goers.

Across four nights, four classic works will showcase the artistic richness and versatility of Wu Opera:

5 Aug | Wu Opera in Singapore – Three Battles with the White Bone Demon

6 Aug | Wu Opera in Singapore – Opera Highlights

7 Aug | Wu Opera in Singapore – The Legend of the White Snake

8 Aug | Wu Opera in Singapore – Mu Guiying

The production brings together some of the most distinguished artists in Chinese opera today. Leading the performances are Plum Blossom Award recipients Yang Xiayun, Lou Sheng, Chen Lili, and Zhou Hongwei, alongside an outstanding ensemble of performers from the Zhejiang Wu Opera Art Research Institute.

Adding further excitement to the programme, young artists from Nam Hwa Opera will also join the stage for a special artistic exchange. Audiences will have the rare opportunity to experience the bold dynamism of Wu Opera alongside the lyrical elegance of Teochew Opera, witnessing a vibrant dialogue between two traditional opera forms and the rising generation of performers.

Whether you are a long-time opera connoisseur or stepping into the theatre for the first time, this extraordinary production promises an unforgettable evening of spectacular martial artistry, legendary storytelling, and breathtaking stagecraft.

mapped.title: 2026 XLOV Asia Tour - SERVING-X In Singapore
mapped.venueName: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.venue: The Star Theatre, The Star Performing Arts Centre
mapped.description:
XLOV (엑스러브), the groundbreaking four-member group under WM Entertainment has captivated fans worldwide with their bold gender-free concept, boundary-pushing performances and magnetic stage presence.  

Consisting of Wumuti, Rui, Hyun, and Haru, XLOV will make their long-awaited Singapore debut as part of the 2026 XLOV Asia Tour "Serving-X". 

After much anticipation, fans in Singapore, also known as EVOLs, will finally have the opportunity to see XLOV perform live on stage. 

On 1 October 2026, fans will be treated to an unforgettable night as XLOV showcase their signature blend of high-energy choreography, powerful vocals, and genuine fan connection to the stage. More than just delivering flawless performances, the group creates an atmosphere where audiences feel seen, welcomed and part of something truly special. 

Beyond their music, XLOV has built a dedicated global community through interactive livestreams, fan calls and meaningful engagement with supporters. Their relationship with EVOL goes far beyond the stage, making every fan feel an integral part of the group's journey. 

This first Singapore concert marks a major milestone—not only for XLOV, but also for the local EVOL community that has supported, streamed and championed the group from afar. Now, the wait is finally over. 

Don't miss your chance to be part of history in the making as XLOV brings the Serving-X tour to Singapore for one unforgettable night.

mapped.title: Young Stars Ensemble 2026
mapped.description:
Join us for the Yamaha Young Stars Ensemble Concert 2026, a celebration of young musical talent, passion, and collaboration. Across two concert sessions: Morning and Afternoon. Our student performers will present an exciting variety of ensemble performances, showcasing the skills, confidence, and joy they have developed through music.

Morning Session: 10:00 AM
Late Afternoon Session: 5:00 PM

Held in conjunction with Yamaha Singapore's 60th Anniversary, this special concert celebrates six decades of inspiring generations through music education. Whether you're a music lover, a parent seeking inspiration for your child, or simply looking for an uplifting live performance, this is a wonderful opportunity to experience the energy and creativity of our young musicians.

Come and be inspired as these rising stars demonstrate how music brings people together and creates memories that last a lifetime.

mapped.title: Zarna Garg - The Million Dollar Excuses Tour
mapped.description:
With her unique blend of humor, wit, and fearless social commentary, Zarna Garg has emerged as a comedic trailblazer. An Indian immigrant mom based in New York City, Zarna showcases her sharp and relatable humor in her first comedy special, One in a Billion, available on Amazon Prime. Her second special, Practical People Win, is currently streaming on Hulu as part of Hulu’s Hilarious Comedy Series, marking another major milestone in her skyrocketing career. 

Zarna’s debut book, This American Woman, also made waves, reaching the New York Times Best Seller list, further solidifying her voice as both hilarious and culturally resonant.

Drawing audiences into a world where laughter and thought-provoking conversation coexist, Zarna has garnered critical acclaim. Jason Zinoman of The New York Times wrote, “Garg has the kind of presence that powers network sitcoms.”

She hosts the popular podcast The Zarna Garg Family Show alongside her entire family, diving into modern life’s big issues — from family dynamics to contemporary social challenges. The podcast is available on YouTube and wherever podcasts are streamed.

Don’t miss Zarna Garg in Singapore for the first time with her brand new show ‘Million Dollar Excuses’

mapped.description: This marks the largest travelling exhibition of artist He Xiangning’s artworks from the He Xiangning Museum in Shenzhen, presented in Singapore for the first time. A leading ink artist and a prominent political figure, the exhibition features over 50 ink works and extensive archival materials.

mapped.title: When Art Meets Nature at National Gallery
mapped.description: A children’s art exhibition co-curated by the Taoyuan Museum of Fine Arts and the National Gallery Singapore, inviting visitors into a sensorial journey to explore nature and reimagine new worlds that foster environmental sustainability.

mapped.title: POP MART CRY ME AN OCEAN at Singapore Oceanarium
mapped.description: Beneath the ocean, CRYBABY becomes a school of fish, carrying a quiet plea through their tears to cherish the circle of life and protect our shared home. Come discover how even the smallest actions can help restore harmony to our oceans.

mapped.title: Peacock Power: Beauty and Symbolism Across Cultures at Peranakan Museum
mapped.description: In celebration of Singapore’s 60th anniversary, this exhibition by the Peranakan Museum is all about the peacock and its symbolism in cultures across the world. The exhibition features over 100 works from the National Collection and lenders, from ceramics to textiles.

mapped.title: Passion is Volcanic: Desire in Southeast Asian Art (R18) at National Gallery Singapore
mapped.description: Passion is Volcanic: Desire in Southeast Asian Art (R18) explores how desire, eroticism and the body have shaped artistic practices and cultural discourses in Southeast Asia. Featuring rarely seen artworks from the National Collection alongside key regional loans, the exhibition embraces the power and presence of desire in art and life.

mapped.title: Lilytopia at Gardens by the Bay
mapped.description: Journey to ancient Egypt, where lilies, water lilies, and lilies of the Nile bloom alongside a majestic recreation of the Dendera Temple, hieroglyphic art, and fascinating sculptures in this spectacular display offering a glimpse into Pharaoh’s glorious treasures.
