offMap.title: SSCC Auditions 2026
offMap.description:
The Singapore Symphony Children’s Choir (SSCC) offers training and performance opportunities to children by developing their vocal technique, music literacy and musicianship, with an aim to help fledgling singers achieve their fullest musical potential. Singing with the SSCC requires discipline and teamwork, which in turn fosters confidence, self-esteem, and helps develop important life skills. Besides our own concerts, the SSCC offers the extraordinary experience of performing monumental choral classics with the combined forces of the Singapore Symphony choruses and orchestra.

Please note that the SSCC repertoire may include some liturgical works.

Find out more about the Singapore Symphony Children's Choir here.

Details

  • Date: 27 Sep 26
  • Time: From 9am
    Timeslots will be allocated upon registration
  • Fee: $25 per participant

For children aged 8 to 15 (born between 2011 and 2018).

We welcome all with and without music background, though auditionees aged 11 (Child's age is determined by the year of birth, i.e. born in/before 2015) and above will require a minimum of ABRSM (or equivalent) Music Theory and/or Performance Grade 3 qualification.

What to expect

Auditionees can expect to go through their audition song, aural and musicianship assessments with SSCC's friendly panel. Children with music backgrounds may be asked to try additional musicianship exercises to ascertain their capability.

Registration closes on Fri, 18 Sep 2026. If you have any questions, please email choral@sso.org.sg.

offMap.title: Mozart, Prokofiev and Brahms with François Leleux
offMap.description:
Playful exuberance and melodic charisma, words that describe both French conductor/oboist François Leleux and Mozart’s sunny Oboe Concerto, one of the finest works in the genre that’s a delight from start to finish. Brahms (in)famously took 21 years to finish his First Symphony, writing in the shadow of his illustrious predecessor, Beethoven. Its premiere in 1876 was just 40 years before the 26-year-old Prokofiev produced his Classical Symphony in 1917 – four decades that saw the Late Romantic Symphony transform into the many flavours of the 20th century such as Neoclassicism.

Please note the different venues and concert start times for 27 Feb and 28 Feb.

FEATURING
Singapore Symphony Orchestra
François Leleux, oboe/conductor

PROGRAMME
Prokofiev Symphony No. 1 in D major, Op. 25 “Classical”
Mozart Oboe Concerto in C major, K. 314
Brahms Symphony No. 1 in C minor, Op. 68

offMap.title: FOO FIGHTERS | TAKE COVER TOUR (AUSTRALIA & NEW ZEALAND)
offMap.description:
One of the biggest and most beloved rock bands of all time – Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Inductees Foo Fighters – make their hugely anticipated return to Australia and New Zealand next summer.

Renowned for their marathon sets and career-spanning performances, as the world's premier stadium rock band Foo Fighters have sold over 1.8 million records in Australia alone, with nine #1 albums to date – the most #1 in any territory for the group. With demand sure to be high for these nine shows, fans are urged to act quickly to secure tickets.

Foo Fighters are stoked to be sharing stages across Australia and New Zealand with 16 of the region's hottest rising acts, handpicked by the Foos:

Brisbane: Full Flower Moon Band (Brisbane) + Mini Skirt (Byron Bay)
Townsville: Full Flower Moon Band (Brisbane) + Downgirl (Sydney) 
Sydney: The Belair Lips Bombs (Frankston) + TEENS (Hobart) 
Newcastle: The Buoys (Sydney) + C.O.F.F.I.N (Sydney) 
Melbourne: The Belair Lips Bombs (Frankston) + FRENZEE (Melbourne) 
Adelaide: Kurralta Park (Adelaide) + Spooky Eyes (Launceston) 
Christchurch: Dartz (Wellington) + Seek Help! (Dunedin) 
Auckland: Dick Move (Auckland) + Ringlets (Auckland) 
Perth: Southern River Band (Perth) + Last Quokka (Perth)

offMap.title: Summer Sonic 2026
offMap.description:
Summer Sonic 2026 is one of Asia’s largest and most anticipated music festivals, bringing together a diverse lineup of global and regional artists across rock, pop, electronic, and alternative genres. Held simultaneously in Tokyo and Osaka, the festival delivers a high-energy, multi-stage experience featuring internationally acclaimed headliners and emerging talents.

Known for its vibrant atmosphere and world-class production, Summer Sonic provides fans with a unique opportunity to experience live performances from top artists in a dynamic outdoor and indoor setting. From electrifying performances to immersive festival vibes, Summer Sonic 2026 promises an unforgettable celebration of music and culture.

offMap.title:
Esplanade Presents | The Studios
Handle with Care
by Ontroerend Goed
offMap.description:
★ ★ ★ ★ ★ 
“A tiny theatrical miracle”  
– Bakchormeeboy 

★ ★ ★ ★ 
“We walk out glowing a bit brighter … as if we shared a secret”   
 – BroadwayWorld  

Handle with Care is a theatre performance in a box. 
No actors. No technical crew. 
Just a box. And you.

A box is mailed to the theatre. 
The instructions are clear:
Invite a group of people, on a specific night, at a specific time.
Let the audience take their seats.
Place the box at the centre of the stage.
The audience is waiting.
Then, an audience member stands up and opens the box.
The show has started.

Ontroerend Goed is returning to Singapore after a showing of its work £¥€$ at Singapore International Festival of Arts (SIFA) in 2023. Its previous outing with the Studios was in 2021 with TM, a live online performance for one audience member at a time.

And now, in 2026, hot on the heels of its successful presentation at the Hong Kong Arts Festival earlier in the year, Handle with Care puts you, the audience, in control. We provide the structure; you shape the experience. Choose your role—take the lead, or observe others. It’s all up to you. Together, you and other audience members will create something special: a shared experience filled with reflections on time, transience, and togetherness.

As one of Belgium’s most playful, least conformist theatre companies, Ontroerend Goed prides itself on making participatory theatre for people who don't like participatory theatre.

And don’t worry, there are no wrong choices in Handle with Care. You’ve got this. For one hour, you’ll live something unique, fleeting, and unrepeatable. Here. Now. Together. No one is watching. What’s yours, stays yours. Take good care of it. And please, send us a postcard. 

“There are no strangers here; only friends you haven’t yet met.” – William Butler Yeats

Credits

Concept & creation: Alexander Devriendt, Karolien De Bleser, Samir Veen, Leonore Spee & Charlotte De Bruyne
Design: Nick Mattan & Edouard Devriendt

offMap.title: 佳乐15周年金曲演唱会 Jia Le 15th Anniversary Hokkien Hits Concert
offMap.venueName: Resorts World Ballroom, Resorts World Convention Centre®
offMap.venue: Resorts World Ballroom, Resorts World Convention Centre®
offMap.description:
Singtel presents the "Jia Le 15th Anniversary Hokkien Hits Concert" — six iconic Hokkien stars: Weng Li You (翁立友), Lin Shan, (林姗), Chen Qian Wen (陳謙文), Huang Fei (黃妃) , Chris Hung (洪榮宏) and Chen Mei Feng (陳美鳳),  share one stage for the first time. Relive timeless classics and beloved hits across generations. An unforgettable night of musical memories awaits.

Weng Li You (翁立友)
Golden Melody Award-winning Hokkien pop king. Renowned for his soulful voice and emotional depth, he has captivated audiences for years with hits like 《坚持》,《我问天》,《夜市人生》.  A master of heartfelt live performances, Weng Li You is celebrated as a true icon of Hokkien love songs.

Lin Shan, Yvonne(林姗)
Acclaimed Taiwanese Hokkien recording diva. Known for her tender, nuanced vocals and flawless live delivery, she brings heartfelt emotion to every ballad. With a loyal fanbase built over years, Lin Shan is a defining female voice in Hokkien pop. Signature songs,《绵绵旧情》，《力量》，《伴你过一生》，《送你送到这》 and《粉红超跑》.

Chen Qian Wen, Wingle (陳謙文)
After debuting in the 2005 CHT MOD Star Talent Competition and joining idol group MODS in 2009, Chen Qian Wen first made a name for himself in 2019 with standout roles in the hit dramas 《春花望露》and 《天之蕉子》— earning widespread acclaim. Now active in both music and entertainment, he continues to impress with confident stage work, strong vocals, and versatile artistry. Handsome, charismatic, and multi‑talented, Chen is hailed as one of the most promising new‑generation stars.

Huang Fei (黃妃) 
A Golden Melody Award-winning Taiwanese Hokkien diva, she is renowned for her crystal-clear, powerful, and unmistakable voice. A true icon of Taiwanese Hokkien pop. Her signature song 《追追追》 has become a timeless classic. With a style that fuses tradition and innovation, she has stayed at the heart of the music scene for decades,
beloved by fans across generations.

Chris Hung ((洪榮宏)
Legendary Hokkien music icon. With a career spanning generations and a catalogue of timeless hits, his warm, heartfelt, and powerful voice has left an indelible mark on Chinese-language music. A true inspiration to countless younger artists. Signature songs include 《一支小雨伞》,《山頂黑狗兄》,《愛的小路》,《望月想愛人》,《雨那会落抹停》 and 《媽媽請你也保重》. 

Chen Mei Feng (陳美鳳)
Taiwan's Drama Queen — widely known as "Taiwan's Most Beautiful Obasan" (auntie). She has built a remarkable career spanning television, film, variety shows, and stage performances. With decades in the spotlight, she remains immensely popular, cherished for her warm, approachable image and magnetic stage presence. Actively engaged in a wide range of performances, she is regarded as one of the most versatile and iconic all‑round entertainers in the Chinese-language entertainment industry. Her signature song is 《繁华拢是梦》. 

Six charismatic vocalists, each with their own magic. From heartfelt ballads to beloved classics, their songs have been the soundtrack to countless lives — through youth, growth, and memories. Now sharing one stage, they will bring you a musical journey across generations, awakening your most profound emotions with every note.

offMap.title: Joe Hisaishi World Dream Tour
offMap.description:
Globally-renowned composer Joe Hisaishi joins forces with the Orchestre de Paris – one of the world’s foremost symphony orchestras – for his World Dream Tour. Joe Hisaishi is Composer in Association with the Philharmonie de Paris, where the orchestra is a permanent resident. This landmark event marks the orchestra’s first ever tour to Singapore, and the first major European/US orchestra tour to the country since 2020. Internationally celebrated for its refined French tonal tradition and uncompromising performance standards, the Orchestre de Paris brings Europe’s great symphonic heritage into vivid dialogue with Hisaishi’s distinctive musical voice, renowned for its lyrical beauty, contemporary sensibility, and powerful storytelling.

The programme opens with Claude Debussy’s Petite Suite, a luminous Impressionist gem of delicate colour and flowing texture. Hisaishi’s Symphonic Suite “Princess Mononoke” follows, transforming beloved film music into an expansive symphonic statement, heightened by the presence of a soprano solo. After intermission, his latest composition Concerto for Orchestra serves as a thrilling finale, an electrifying showcase that gives every section of the ensemble its moment in the spotlight.

FEATURING
Orchestre de Paris
Joe Hisaishi, conductor

PROGRAMME
Debussy: Petite Suite
Joe Hisaishi: Symphonic Suite “Princess Mononoke”
Joe Hisaishi: Concerto for Orchestra Southeast Asian Premiere

Joe Hisaishi
Joe Hisaishi, a composer and conductor, has established himself as a formidable force in contemporary music for his delicately crafted symphonic and solo works, as well as his globally successful film music. Hisaishi is greatly in demand as a conductor performing with the most notable symphony orchestras across the globe. With nearly 40 solo albums and over 100 film scores to his name, he is one of the most celebrated composers of our time. Joe Hisaishi currently holds positions with the Los Angeles Philharmonic, Philadelphia Orchestra, Royal Philharmonic Orchestra, and from September 2026 will be the Composer in Association with the Philharmonie de Paris.

Orchestre de Paris
Founded in 1967 and based at the Philharmonie de Paris since its opening in 2015, the Orchestre de Paris is France’s leading symphonic orchestra, comprising 119 musicians. It has been led by Music Director Klaus Mäkelä since September 2021, under whose tenure the orchestra has pursued an ambitious artistic vision combining international touring, recording projects with Decca, major commissions, and wide‑ranging educational initiatives. Highlights include the world premiere of Pascal Dusapin’s Antigone, a documentary film project, and major international tours in Asia. Committed to audience development and social impact, the Orchestra is closely involved in flagship initiatives such as Démos, a programme for musical and orchestral education with a social mission and La Maestra, an international competition promoting gender parity in orchestral conducting. From September 2027, Esa‑Pekka Salonen will assume the role of Chief Conductor, continuing the Orchestra’s distinguished artistic lineage

offMap.title: Bunker By Night 2026
offMap.description:
BUNKER BY NIGHT returns, transforming the Battlebox into a multi-room world of sound, movement, and encounter. Produced by Global Cultural Alliance and SAtheCollective, this edition draws from tales and shared stories. Across two nights, artists activate the bunker—inviting audiences into a sensory journey through memory, and play.

Night 1 - Friday, 28 August 2026, 8PM - 11PM
Dirk Stromberg | Lattice | MASMA DREAM WORLD | VYLT & FALLING ISLANDS

Night 2 - Saturday, 29 August 2026, 8PM - 10PM
Natalie & Marla | Gelap | SGStingray | Zihao & Ng Zuyou

BUNKER BY NIGHT is produced by Global Cultural Alliance in collaboration with ThirdSpace Sessions by SAtheCollective as part of Singapore Night Festival 2026.

offMap.title: Chinese Calligraphy Writing Workshop
offMap.description: Experience the beauty of Chinese calligraphy in this 2-hour 15-minute workshop designed for beginners. Begin with an introduction to its rich history and cultural significance, followed by a hands-on session exploring the “Four Treasures of the Study” — brush, ink, paper, and ink stone. Learn proper brush handling, posture, and practise basic strokes in the Kai script (楷书), including writing your own name. The session concludes with a personal artwork — a meaningful phrase or couplet — showcasing your new skills. This workshop offers a thoughtful and artistic introduction to calligraphy as both a cultural tradition and expressive art form.

offMap.title: An Introduction To Chinese Ink Painting Workshop - Tropical Fruits & The Nanyang School
offMap.description: Take a peek into the Nanyang School of art and gain an understanding on how our pioneer artists came to Singapore and developed a unique Nanyang style of Chinese paintings. This is a workshop which introduces Chinese ink painting through teaching learners how to paint and compose tropical fruits, which is one of the most characteristic subject depicted by artists such as Georgette Chen, Liu Kang, Chen Chong Swee. Create and take home your very own Chinese ink painting fruit painting.

offMap.title: 本地灯谜赏析工作坊Local Chinese Lantern Riddles Workshop
offMap.description:
华语讲座，使用PPT。阐述灯谜的构成及基本规则；本地灯谜的节选赏析。讲座结束将有谜题让参与者猜射，猜中者将获得小奖品。
The 2-hour Workshop will be conducted in Mandarin, Using PPT to narrate the structure of Chinese Lantern Riddles and the basic rules; to analyse and to appreciate selected local Chinese Lantern Riddles. At the end of the session, riddles will be created for the participants to guess. And there will be small prizes for those who have the correct answers.

offMap.title: Craft Your Story: A Common Touch Pottery Workshop
offMap.description:
Common Touch invites you to slow down and reconnect with your senses through the timeless art of pottery. Led by ceramic artists Fyon Cheong and Zestro Leow, the studio offers a welcoming space for people to create, bond, and experience the joy of clay.
In this 3-hour workshop, participants will explore two key pottery techniques:
1. ﻿﻿﻿Hand-Building (Pinching Technique) — Create one larger or two smaller pieces, then personalize with colors.
2. ﻿﻿﻿Wheel-Throwing — Try four attempts at the potter's wheel, selecting three pieces for firing.
Each participant will leave with five unique ceramic pieces (two hand-built, three wheel-thrown), ready for collection approximately 4 to 6 months.*Due to the high volume of artworks in the firing queue, waiting time may be extended.

No prior experience required-just a desire to create and connect. Perfect for creative escapes, bonding, or mindfulness. All materials and guidance provided. Suitable for all ages.

In clay, we find connection. In creation, we find joy. - Common Touch

Reservation Procedure

  • Important Note: Upon purchasing the ticket, you are required to verify the reservation with the operator directly via WhatsApp (+65 8886 6345).
    • Please provide your e-ticket's bar code when making a reservation.
    • If you are attending as a group with different booking account, kindly provide us a consolidated e-ticket's bar code of others who are attending with you to be seated together.
  • Bookings are strictly final and are not eligible for rescheduling.
  • Single seats available shown on the booking calendar refers to the last individual seat available.

Cancellation policy
No cancellations or refunds.

offMap.title: DuringMyTime.SG with Eng Tiang Huat
offMap.venueName: Eng Tiang Huat Chinese Cultural Shop
offMap.venue: Eng Tiang Huat Chinese Cultural Shop
offMap.description:
Step into a living journey of heritage, creativity and connection at Eng Tiang Huat Chinese Cultural Shop, a treasured Teochew family business founded by immigrant Eng Tiang Huat from Chenghai, Chaozhou (Teochew). Preserved across three generations, the shop is a living museum, where family stories, cultural traditions and rare artefacts bring Singapore's living heritage to life.

Hosted by third-generation owner Mr Jeffrey Eng, this exclusive experience unveils his family's migration journey through vintage photographs, the family genealogy book and personal stories passed down across generations. Discover the meanings behind Ang Cai (red banners), intricate opera costume embroidery and rare antique Teochew musical instruments. Capture the experience through mindful photography before enjoying an interactive percussion music session.

Enjoy:

  • Exclusive storytelling by Mr Jeffrey Eng

  • A rare look at original family archives and heritage artefacts

  • Mindful photography and hands-on percussion music-making

  • Homemade Teochew kuehs and cakes prepared by the Eng family

  • DuringMyTime.SG Game Quest with a collectible commemorative coin

  • Guided mindfulness practice

  • Tau Sar Piah to bring home a sweet taste of Teochew heritage

sessions.venueKey: Eng Tiang Huat Chinese Cultural Shop

venueOccurrences.publishedVenueName: Eng Tiang Huat Chinese Cultural Shop

offMap.title: DuringMyTime.SG with Traditional Chinese Medicine & Tea Culture
offMap.description:
(Meet-up details: 9.30 am on 28 Jul 2026 at Nanyang Old Coffee 268 South Bridge Road)

Step into a living journey of heritage, healing and connection through Singapore’s Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) culture and Tea Appreciation.

This immersive experience brings participants from Nanyang Old Coffee to the century-old Teck Soon Medical Hall and TANG TEA TeaHouse, uncovering stories of tradition, memories and wellness along the way.

Hear from Dr Chow Khai Shui of Teck Soon Medical Hall as he shares the origins of TCM, its evolution in Singapore and the story of a medical hall founded in 1925. Experience traditional herb-measuring practices firsthand before continuing into the calming world of tea appreciation, sound bath, and mindful rest.

Enjoy:
Coffee and toast, tea and light refreshments, a guided sound bath experience and bring home a collectible DuringMyTime.SG coin!

An introduction to Singapore’s TCM heritage and tea culture.

Stories and sharing by Teck Soon Medical Hall.

Hands-on traditional herb measuring experience.

Mindful photography tips for capturing living heritage.

Tea appreciation session exploring wellness.

Guided sound bath for reflection and rest.

Game Quest, mindfulness micro-practice & connection with community.

offMap.title: Mid-Autumn Festival Learning Painting Workshop
offMap.venueName: Visual Arts Centre at Dhoby Ghaut
offMap.venue: Visual Arts Centre at Dhoby Ghaut
offMap.description:
Celebrate the warmth and charm of the Mid-Autumn Festival in this 1.5-hour hands-on painting workshop, where participants will be guided step-by-step to paint colourful and festive lantern artworks. Drawing inspiration from traditional Mid-Autumn lanterns, glowing moonlight, and festive patterns, participants will learn how to design and bring their lantern paintings to life.
Throughout the session, basic drawing and colouring will be introduced. All art materials are fully provided, making the workshop suitable for beginners and those with prior art experience. Each participant will complete and take home their finished lantern painting as a meaningful keepsake of the Mid-Autumn Festival.

offMap.title: Therukoothu 101
offMap.description:
​Nadagamic 2026 brings together voices from Singapore and across the world - a meeting point of cultures, traditions and contemporary storytelling. Here, theatre becomes a shared language that connects us beyond borders.

THERUKOOTHU 101 WORKSHOP
An immersive foundational workshop designed for theatre-makers seeking to engage deeply with the traditional Tamil performance form of Therukoothu. Through physical training, rhythmic footwork, expressive storytelling and character embodiment, participants are introduced to the aesthetics, musicality and dramaturgy of this centuries-old art form. Rooted in tradition yet relevant to contemporary practice, the session offers practitioners a hands-on exploration of how Therukoothu’s bold physicality and narrative energy can inform and enrich modern performance-making.

ABOUT AK THEATRE LTD, SINGAPORE
AK Theatre Ltd is a dynamic theatre organization passionately dedicated to keeping the Tamil language, culture and performing arts alive for children, youth, and future generations. AK Theatre blends educational engagement with compelling stagecraft to enrich participants’ creative and cultural experiences. 
At its core, AK Theatre’s mission is to inspire confidence, imagination, and lifelong skills through a diverse range of workshops, stage and video productions, interactive festivals, and arts education programs. Their artistic work places a strong emphasis on Tamil and bilingual productions with Singaporean elements, making their offerings deeply relevant to local multicultural contexts. Their rich history of engaging children and families through theatre and cultural arts, offers broad audience appeal to festival collaborations.

offMap.title: Urban Sketching Workshop - Multiple Locations
offMap.venueName: Visual Arts Centre at Dhoby Ghaut
offMap.venue: Visual Arts Centre at Dhoby Ghaut
offMap.description: Discover Singapore’s cityscape through art in our immersive Urban Landscape Sketching City Tour – Perfect for beginners to advanced learners. This 2-hour workshop is guided by professional art instructors, participants will explore and learn about the history iconic locations such as CHIJMES, Marina Bay Sands, Chinatown, and Gardens by the Bay.  Thereafter, they will learn the basic skillset of observation, perspective and line drawing using using pencils, pens, and watercolour techniques. Each participant will complete a sketch to take home as a unique souvenir.

offMap.title: Caricature Drawing Workshop
offMap.venueName: Visual Arts Centre at Dhoby Ghaut
offMap.venue: Visual Arts Centre at Dhoby Ghaut
offMap.description: This is a 2-hour caricature drawing workshop designed for adults, with all art materials provided. Whether you're an art enthusiast or simply looking for a creative way to unwind, this session offers a fun and engaging introduction to the art of character caricature. Under the guidance of our experienced instructor, participants will learn the basics of caricature — from observing and sketching facial features, to exaggerating proportions, capturing personality, and using expressive line work to bring each drawing to life. You'll start with foundational sketching exercises, then move on to create your own finished caricature artwork, whether it’s a humorous self-portrait or a fun take on someone you know.

offMap.title: LKCNHM Public Workshops 2026
offMap.dateText: Sat, 25 July 2026 (SPECIMENS TO SPECIES WORKSHOP @ LKCNHM)Sat, 22 August 2026 (ADAPTATION & BIOMIMICRY WORKSHOP @ SENTOSA)
offMap.description:
LKCNHM Public Workshops 2026 

Discover the science behind biodiversity through two hands-on workshops led by Lee Kong Chian Natural History Museum, from uncovering how new species are identified to exploring how nature inspires real-world innovations. 

SPECIMENS TO SPECIES WORKSHOP @ LKCNHM  

Ever wondered how scientists identify and name new species? In this hands-on workshop, participants will explore how museum specimens are used to study biodiversity and learn the basics of taxonomy and scientific naming. Through a classroom discussion and guided gallery tour, participants will learn how specimens are collected, preserved, and used by scientists to identify species. Participants will also get to take part in interactive storytelling that simulates the stages of species discovery, offering insight into the scientific process behind describing new species.  

ADAPTATION & BIOMIMICRY WORKSHOP @ SENTOSA 

How does nature inspire innovation? Join us for the Adaptation & Biomimicry Workshop at Sentosa and discover how plants and animals adapt to their environments, and how these clever natural strategies have inspired human technologies. From a guided nature walk to hands-on activities, participants will explore Sentosa’s habitats, biodiversity,and the fascinating connections between nature and design.

offMap.title: 《那些年的歌2》演唱會 Songs That Keep Us Together 2 Concert
offMap.venueName: Resorts World Ballroom, Resorts World Convention Centre®
offMap.venue: Resorts World Ballroom, Resorts World Convention Centre®
offMap.description:
《那些年的歌2》演唱会集结8组华语乐坛传奇歌手及组合，每位皆是拥有个人演唱会号召力的重量级唱将，包括苏慧伦、万芳、苏永康、南方二重唱、林隆璇、黄舒骏、熊天平及林晓培。从校园民歌到90年代经典流行金曲，一首首陪伴无数人成长的旋律将再次响起，带领观众重返华语音乐黄金年代，在熟悉的歌声中重温那些年的美好时光。

苏慧伦 「影歌双栖实力女神」  — 出道逾30年的苏慧伦，以清新脱俗的形象与疗愈系嗓音深受乐迷喜爱。从《Lemon Tree》、《鸭子》、《傻瓜》到《被动》，一首首经典作品奠定她在90年代华语流行乐坛的重要地位，近年更凭藉专辑《面面》入围第31届金曲奖最佳华语女歌手奖。

万芳「心灵系歌后」— 出道超过30年，凭藉《新不了情》、《猜心》、《割爱》及《就值得了爱》等经典作品，以温暖细腻的嗓音唱进无数人的心中，成为华语乐坛最具疗愈力量的声音之一

苏永康「情歌王子」— 纵横华语乐坛逾30年的香港实力派歌手苏永康，从《男人不该让女人流泪》、《爱一个人好难》、《越吻越伤心》到《旧爱还是最美》，一首首经典情歌道尽爱情中的甜蜜与遗憾。

南方二重唱「民歌经典组合」— 自1980年代出道以来，南方二重唱以温暖和谐的双声线深受乐迷喜爱，《秋蝉》、《细说往事》、《野百合也有春天》及《风中的早晨》等作品，承载着许多人珍贵的青春回忆。

林隆璇「情歌教授」— 身兼歌手、创作人及音乐制作人，多年来创作无数脍炙人口的经典作品，近年积极投入流行音乐教育，并取得教育博士学位。除了演唱《你那么爱她》、《为爱往前飞》等代表作外，他亦创作《白天不懂夜的黑》、《难以抗拒你容颜》等传唱至今的经典情歌

黄舒骏「音乐才子」— 从《恋爱症候群》幽默描绘爱情百态，到《改变1995》对时代变迁的细腻刻画，再到《马不停蹄的忧伤》与《雁渡寒潭》等作品，其充满故事性的创作风格至今仍令人回味无穷。此次更是黄舒骏睽违近10年重返新加坡舞台。

熊天平「金曲最佳作词人」— 90年代华语乐坛极具代表性的创作型歌手，凭藉为齐秦创作的《火柴天堂》荣获第8届金曲奖最佳作词人奖。从《火柴天堂》、《夜夜夜夜》、《爱情多恼河》到《雪候鸟》，细腻真挚的词曲创作感动无数乐迷。

林晓培「摇滚天后」— 凭藉主打歌《烦》迅速走红，并一举夺得第10届金曲奖最佳新人奖。其后演唱电影《心动》同名主题曲，让《心动》成为传唱至今的经典代表作，《她的眼泪》与《缘》等歌曲亦展现其兼具摇滚力量与细腻情感的演唱实力。

offMap.title: 《星光闪耀》演唱会 2026 Shining Stars Concert 2026
offMap.venueName: Resorts World Ballroom, Resorts World Convention Centre®
offMap.venue: Resorts World Ballroom, Resorts World Convention Centre®
offMap.description:
华语乐坛三位实力唱将—杨宗纬、温岚与李佳薇，将于2026年9月19日齐聚新加坡圣淘沙名胜世界会议中心（Resorts World Convention Centre），携手呈献《星光闪耀》演唱会 2026，为观众带来一场精彩绝伦的音乐盛宴。 

杨宗纬深情动人的嗓音、温岚充满魅力的舞台表现，以及李佳薇极具震撼力的铁肺唱功，将在同一个舞台上绽放耀眼光芒。三位歌手将轮番献唱多首广受喜爱的代表作，从扣人心弦的抒情作品到热力四射的节奏歌曲，让观众沉浸在音乐与回忆交织的美好时刻。 

这不仅是一场汇聚实力与魅力的演唱会，更是一次难得的音乐相聚。无论是陪伴成长的熟悉旋律，还是令人惊艳的现场演绎，这场唱会都将为歌迷打造一个充满感动与惊喜的难忘夜晚。

offMap.title: 周柏豪·THE BLOOMING CHAPTER绽放之章粉丝见面演唱会 - 新加坡站 PAKHO CHAU·THE BLOOMING CHAPTER FAN MEETING CONCERT - SINGAPORE
offMap.venueName: Resorts World Ballroom, Resorts World Convention Centre®
offMap.venue: Resorts World Ballroom, Resorts World Convention Centre®
offMap.description:
Pakho Chau is a famous Hong Kong singer, songwriter and actor. Born on 12 November 1984, he debuted in 2007 and quickly became one of the most representative artists in contemporary Cantopop. 

Blessed with a warm and unique vocal voice, Pakho has released numerous classic Cantonese songs throughout his career. His heartfelt and melodious works are widely popular among audiences across Hong Kong, mainland China and the Greater Bay Area. He has received many important music awards and is highly recognised for his musical talent and consistent creative output. 

Besides music, Pakho Chau has also starred in various popular Hong Kong films and dramas. With his versatile performance and positive public image, he remains one of the most influential and beloved Hong Kong artists of his generation.
